content:
Monthly Summary — 2026-03 March 2026 achieved transformative breakthroughs across AI systems, robotics, and research infrastructure through systematic application of evolving constraint methodology. Error Recovery Benchmark completed v5.0 architectural redesign shifting from online physics injection to offline trajectory-based VLA context replay, while CalendarPro eliminated single-intent bottleneck via multi-intent LLM classification. Gadget toolkit evolved from quick-overview to deep-excavation research system supporting arbitrary-scale full-text analysis through chunked LLM merge, integrated with MCP server transformation and rclone sync infrastructure establishing clear git-vs-data boundaries. MIHD spatial transcriptomics proved Foundation Models achieve 100% cross-section retrieval vs 14% for PCA while discovering STAIG’s surprising robustness to gene encoder choice. TokenMonitor completed cross-platform migration with polished float ball UI, and ccplan skill introduced 10-phase requirement engineering with adversarial review catching 12+ critical issues pre-implementation. Month exposed recurring patterns of dependency version fragility, documentation drift, and LLM JSON instability while demonstrating 4x speedup via wave-based parallel agent execution and 81% demo reduction through Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy.
Monthly Overview Metric Value Active Days 31 / 31 Total Conversations 244 Projects 87 Tasks Completed 306 Tasks In Progress 23 Total Tokens 688,243,655 Total Cost $449.30 Claude Code Token 563,848,070 Claude Code Cost $380.41 Codex Tokens 124,395,585 Codex Cost $68.89 Daily Average Cost $17.28 Project Progress Error Recovery Benchmark / Robot Learning Systems (VLA/RoboTwin) (18 days active) — 🔄 active Achieved major architectural transformation from online physics perturbation to offline trajectory-based error injection matching VLA temporal context requirements. Established reliable training and evaluation infrastructure after resolving cascading configuration bugs, metric distortions, and environment setup issues. Production-ready state with complete pipeline from error scene generation through Phoenix training format conversion. Multi-week training campaigns launched with optimized resource allocation strategies.
Key Milestones:
v5.0 architecture redesign: offline scan+quota pipeline for VLA temporal context with replay-based error injection Complete production pipeline: 8 modules, 3,544 lines, 139 passing tests, 2920 error scenes generated Fixed BC-RNN configuration bug (missing object observation key) causing 0% coffee task success Completed BC-RNN evaluation pipeline with 4 critical bug fixes (demo_len, trigger_step, transport detection, RP calculation) Fixed MimicGen warping bug unblocking 10→1000 demo augmentation path (pos_delta application) Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy reducing demo requirements 81% (1,740→329) Launched Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning across 8 GPUs with optimized resource allocation Fixed Openpi-moe normalization bug and 56% gradient dilution in Robobrain Pi training Training optimization: 33% speedup via JAX 0.5.0→0.5.3 alignment RoboTwin data collection extended with 5 new per-frame variables for progress prediction Critical region annotation system for 10 manipulation tasks Fixed 4 critical VLA inference bugs and separated action_loss/task_loss monitoring Portable deployment package (252MB compressed) with SpaceMouse integration MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics) (17 days active) — 🔄 active Evolved from chaotic monolith to clean modular architecture while establishing comprehensive cross-sample evaluation framework and paper-ready pipeline. Key scientific findings: Foundation Models provide shared embedding space essential for zero-shot cross-section retrieval; STAIG spatial graph contrastive learning dominates over gene embedding quality; Harmony improves batch mixing but degrades semantic alignment due to dimension meaning misalignment from per-section training. Code quality significantly improved with O(N²)→O(Nlogk) algorithmic optimizations preventing HD data OOM.
Key Milestones:
Proved scGPT Foundation Model achieves 100% cross-section retrieval (SL@50=0.416) vs 14% for PCA Discovered STAIG framework’s robustness to gene encoder choice (Teddy 1024d ≈ PCA 50d, ARI ~0.495-0.497) Completed RM-IDEAL cross-sample benchmark revealing layer-specific performance (Layer_1/5: ρ 0.42-0.66, Layer_3/6: ρ -0.21 to -0.36) 6-phase codebase refactoring (deleted 250K+ dead code, modular architecture) CrossModalEnhancer module (350 LOC) with spatial k-NN attention avoiding single-token degradation STAIG fusion HD dataset support (fixed 6 technical issues for VisiumHD compatibility) Post-fusion Harmony experiments revealing batch correction vs semantic alignment trade-off TEDDY-G 400M foundation model integration completed DLPFC 11-section evaluation with 5 fusion strategies and Leiden clustering baseline STHD Ground Truth integration for HD dataset Fixed 3 critical spatial alignment bugs (barcode ordering, coordinate systems, path consistency) Pathologist annotation visualization with 100% match rate at 8um resolution Documentation cleanup: 25+ files → 4 core docs, file structure reorganization gadget (Research & Summarization Toolkit) (20 days active) — 🔄 active Transformed from daily report tool to comprehensive research infrastructure with deep-excavation capabilities. Deep-wide mode supports 100-paper full-text analysis via 47-chunk segmentation with LLM merge. Established clear git vs rclone boundary: git manages public code, rclone manages personal data. MCP integration enabled discovery by all AI agents while maintaining zero-dependency philosophy through subprocess isolation. Major infrastructure maturation with modularization, unified deployment, and bilingual documentation generation.
Key Milestones:
5-mode research tool architecture: fast/normal/deep/wide/deep-wide supporting arbitrary-scale full-text analysis Chunked long-text analysis system: 150K chars/chunk, dynamic timeout, LLM semantic merge breaking single-LLM capacity limit Unified research toolkit integrating paper discovery/profiler/citations into single CLI with three-stage evaluation MCP server transformation exposing 9 tools with stdout isolation and SystemExit handling Research scout multi-source expansion: arXiv → arXiv+bioRxiv+PubMed with unified caching Academic researcher profiler tool (15 modules, 2000+ LOC) with LLM trajectory analysis Completed Yuke Zhu comprehensive trajectory (2012-2026, 100-paper 47-chunk analysis) Profiled 30+ researchers including Chelsea Finn, Sergey Levine, Pieter Abbeel Git history cleanup removing 94MB personal data (22MB→516KB) with rclone sync infrastructure Fixed finalize mechanism deadlock causing infinite LLM API cost loop Monthly summarization tool implementation with batch consolidation of 30+ historical daily reports Unified Hugo deployment architecture with outputs/site staging Summarize module refactored (2930→8 modules, 72 tests), research module refactored (2934→7 modules) Translation architecture with local batch inference (vLLM/transformers) Extracted shared utilities into common/ package eliminating 400+ LOC duplication Production logging system with 77 print()→logger migrations Life Copilot / CalendarPro (Personal Butler) (7 days active) — 🔄 active Completed major architectural refactoring replacing dual-verify + 7-layer keyword hacks with LLM-native multi-intent classification. System now handles multi-action messages correctly (e.g., ‘schedule 3PM meeting and move dinner to 7PM’). Transformed from passive scheduler to proactive autonomous butler with situation monitoring, gap analysis, autonomous execution, and deep thought processing capabilities. All 424 tests passing post-refactor with technical debt audit revealing and remediating critical reliability issues.
Key Milestones:
Eliminated single-intent architectural bottleneck with Claude CLI multi-intent analyzer (424 tests passing) Personal Butler System comprehensive upgrade (16 new services, 21 file modifications, 68 tests) Context-aware intent classification system (360 tests passed) Fixed critical bugs (incomplete executor logic, 16 dangerous silent exception handlers) Fixed three-layer calendar query bug (LLM prompt placement, keyword fallback, past events visibility) Fixed duplicate meal scheduling bug via ISO datetime prioritization Comprehensive codebase audit archiving 2400+ LOC dead code Full optimization: semantic routing thresholds, hybrid keyword+embedding, system prompt splitting (530 lines → base + 11 fragments) Multi-agent orchestration (wave executor, monitoring agent, agent registry) Project rename from CalendarPro to Life Copilot across 15 files TokenMonitor (6 days active) — 🔄 active Transformed from macOS-only prototype to production-ready cross-platform desktop app. Eliminated technical debt through file reorganization (commands.rs 2466→131 lines), implemented polished float ball UI with Windows native region clipping, and established robust cost calculation via official ccusage integration. Resolved three-layer positive feedback loop causing persistent UI jitter through coordinated fixes across data, window, and computation layers.
Key Milestones:
Cross-platform migration completed (macOS → Windows/Linux/macOS) Float ball UI with drag-snap-expand interactions implemented Cost calculation migrated to ccusage CLI with transparent fallback 1300+ lines dead code removed, file structure reorganized Windows-specific bugs resolved (positioning, rendering, terminal popup) Fixed multi-layer dashboard jitter via coordinated three-layer fixes Claude Code Skills Development (5 days active) — 🔄 active Built sophisticated skill ecosystem for structured development workflows. ccplan evolved from simple planner to comprehensive requirement engineering system with prompt calibration, multi-intent decomposition, adversarial review catching 12+ issues pre-implementation, feasibility probing, and persistent constraint tracking. cchypothesis introduced systematic debugging via parallel hypothesis investigation with instrumented probing and Git safety checkpoints. Hub+spoke design pattern prevents prompt dilution while supporting multiple languages.
Key Milestones:
ccplan: 10-phase requirement engineering with ECL methodology and adversarial review cchypothesis: hypothesis-driven debugging with parallel investigation Feature Guard Protocol: cross-session persistent regression prevention Paper-style documentation framework (6-section structure) Optimize skill extended to 4 languages (Python/Swift/Rust/TypeScript) with hub+spoke architecture Skills ecosystem optimized (50→28 global, 40% context reduction) Desktop Video (7 days active) — 🔄 active Delivered production-ready v4.1 with critical sandbox permission fixes, native UI redesign, and innovative webpage wallpaper feature supporting arbitrary web content as desktop background. Resolved fundamental TCC permission issues by enabling App Sandbox, allowing bookmark-based access to user-selected files across app restarts. Now ready for App Store distribution.
Key Milestones:
Implemented complete WKWebView webpage wallpaper functionality (YouTube/Bilibili support) Fixed critical security-scoped bookmark system (100% restore failure eliminated) Liquid Glass UI redesign matching macOS Tahoe native aesthetics Playback mode bug fixes (PowerSave/PowerSave+/Automatic with Low Power Mode detection) Browse mode implementation with window layer optimization App Sandbox migration enabling persistent file access via security-scoped bookmarks Fixed history feature with NSOpenPanel fallback for permission errors GPU Monitoring Tools (3 days active) — ✅ completed Built production-grade GPU monitoring solution for containerized environments where nvidia-smi fails. Combined psutil (container-internal process info) with nvidia-smi (hardware state) in rich-based TUI. Addresses systematic gap in Kubernetes GPU resource observability.
Key Milestones:
Solved Kubernetes PID namespace isolation via /proc/fd + CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES inspection nvitop-style TUI with double-border tables, process grouping, auto-filtering Adaptive UI with alternate screen buffer and terminal size detection QCVLA (Latent Bridge) (2 days active) — 🔄 active Achieved complete technical comprehension of bridge mechanism (57.6% compute savings with 4% performance drop). Identified P0 quick-win (enable Action-Aware Channel Importance Loss) and high-value P1 improvements (Bridge Confidence Head). Proposed FLARE-inspired perturbation augmentation addressing DAgger saturation where bridge only seeing its own error distribution limits diversity.
Key Milestones:
Comprehensive architectural documentation covering 7-step pipeline and SingleStepDiT Identified DAgger saturation issue (+1pp plateau at 61% SR) Proposed 11 improvements from FLARE + 9 VLA papers cross-analysis Designed perturbation & bridging data augmentation to expand training distribution VLA Environment Setup (UniVLA/CALVIN/Openpi) (2 days active) — ⏸️ paused Environment configuration and documentation for multiple VLA research codebases. Resolved environment-specific import issues and rendering device conflicts. Created comprehensive build/test/evaluation documentation for future reference.
Key Milestones:
UniVLA CLAUDE.md documentation covering 3-stage training pipeline and 4 evaluation benchmarks CALVIN calvin_agent import fix via correct conda environment isolation Openpi-moe EGL device ID remapping for CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES compatibility Key Achievements Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 Architecture Redesign (2026-03-10, Error Recovery Benchmark) — Major architectural shift from online physics-force injection to offline two-stage pipeline: Stage 0 collect clean trajectories (human+VLA) → Stage 1 offline scan opportunities → Stage 2 quota scheduler (100 per task×error) → Stage 3 context replay (replay N-1 frames for VLA context, inject at frame N). Defined 11 error types × 3 deformation dimensions = 26 subtypes. VLA temporal context dependency requires offline trajectory-based injection, not online physics perturbation. CalendarPro Multi-Intent Architecture Refactor (2026-03-19, Life Copilot) — Eliminated fundamental single-intent bottleneck by replacing dual-verify + 7-layer keyword hacks with Claude CLI MultiIntentAnalyzer. System now handles multi-action messages correctly (e.g., ‘schedule 3PM meeting and move dinner to 7PM’). Reduced classification code complexity ~200 lines while adding 30 tests. All 424 tests passing. Single-intent classification was architectural constraint unfixable with keyword hacks. Gadget Research Tool Deep-Wide Mode with Chunked Analysis (2026-03-19, gadget) — Transformed research toolkit from quick-overview to deep-excavation system. Implemented chunking (150K chars/chunk), dynamic timeout (300s+120s/50K), and LLM semantic merge breaking single-LLM capacity limit. Successfully analyzed Yuke Zhu’s 100 papers via 47-chunk segmentation. Capability leap from ‘fast skim’ to ‘arbitrary-scale full-text analysis’ enabling comprehensive academic trajectory research. MIHD Foundation Model Cross-Section Retrieval Proof (2026-03-16, MIHD) — Proved scGPT Gene Foundation Model achieves 100% hit rate (SL@50=0.416) vs 14% for per-section PCA through controlled experiments. Established that zero-shot cross-section retrieval fundamentally requires shared embedding space from pretrained models—joint training violates zero-shot constraint. Foundation Models’ key advantage: provide shared embedding space without joint training. ccplan Requirement Engineering Skill with ECL Methodology (2026-03-26, Claude Code Skills) — Built 10-phase requirement engineering workflow with prompt calibration, multi-intent decomposition, adversarial review (12+ issues caught pre-implementation), feasibility probing, and Feature Guard Protocol for cross-session persistence. Successfully applied to 4+ complex projects catching critical design flaws before implementation. Phase 7 Red-Blue confrontation exposes design flaws at far lower cost than post-implementation refactoring. TokenMonitor Cross-Platform Migration Completed (2026-03-24, TokenMonitor) — Executed comprehensive ECL planning and implementation removing 350+ lines macOS-only code, adding Windows/Linux support via conditional compilation, implementing platform-aware UI (tray positioning, progress bar scaling), and building NSIS installer. Float ball UI with pointer-captured drag, 42px edge-snap, expand/collapse with blur trigger. Eliminated blocking issues for broader user adoption. MimicGen Warping Bug Fix Unblocking 10→1000 Augmentation (2026-03-19, Error Recovery Benchmark) — Fixed critical bug where pos_delta was computed but never applied to warped_actions. Success rate jumped from <5% to expected 50%+ for D0 errors. Unblocks augmentation path: 10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success = 1000 demos. Essential for scaling training data. MimicGen augmentation fundamentally about coordinate transformation, not noise injection. Git History Cleanup and Rclone Sync Infrastructure (2026-03-20, gadget) — Removed 452 personal files (94MB) from git history via filter-repo, reducing repo from 22MB to 516KB. Established clear boundary: git manages public code, rclone manages personal data. Created unified sync.py with push/pull/status/config commands supporting 4 data categories. Eliminated privacy risks and repo bloat. Clear separation establishes data privacy boundary. BC-RNN Evaluation Pipeline Complete Bug Fix (4 Cascading Bugs) (2026-03-07, Error Recovery Benchmark) — Fixed collector.py demo_length→demo_len field name error, trigger_step reading from wrong source, transport stage detection logic requiring runtime dynamic target position retrieval, and missing rp=1.0 for successful episodes. Resolved RP anomaly (0.003 vs Random 0.020) through systematic code audit revealing cascading failures. Configuration bugs can train for 600 epochs with 0% success because loss decreases normally but policy learns nothing useful. CrossModalEnhancer with Spatial k-NN Attention (2026-03-09, MIHD) — Architected cross-modal enhancement module using spatial k-NN neighbors as K/V sequences to avoid single-token attention degradation. Implemented CrossModalAttentionBlock and CrossModalEnhancer (~350 LOC) supporting 3 modes (gene→image, image→gene, bidirectional) with symmetric InfoNCE loss. Cross-attention on single tokens degenerates to linear projection; spatial neighbors form K/V sequences encoding neighborhood context. Recurring Problems 1. Training/Model configuration issues causing silent failures, severe performance degradation, or dependency fragility (10 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-01, 2026-03-05, 2026-03-06, 2026-03-07, 2026-03-09, 2026-03-10, 2026-03-11, 2026-03-12 Root Cause: Template-based config generation without per-task validation, missing observation keys (object state), normalization bugs (apply_tree strict=False silently skipping), checkpoint misconfigurations. PyTorch C++ extensions and CUDA libraries have strict ABI compatibility requirements—version mismatches manifest as operator registration failures, cuBLAS bugs on specific hardware, ABI breaks requiring recompilation, performance regressions. Zero-padding for architectural compatibility severely dilutes gradient signals (56% in RoboTwin case) if included in loss computation. JAX/XLA minor version upgrades (0.5.0→0.5.3) can deliver 20-30% training speedups through compiler optimizations. Status: ongoing - systematic fixes applied but validation gaps remain
2. Documentation-code mismatch causing confusion and blocking progress (11 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-02, 2026-03-04, 2026-03-05, 2026-03-07, 2026-03-08, 2026-03-09, 2026-03-10, 2026-03-15, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19, 2026-03-20 Root Cause: Documentation drift inevitable in active projects—outdated statistics, missing implementation details, incorrect pipeline diagrams, project.json ID mismatches. Rapid feature additions not immediately reflected in CLAUDE.md/README.md. Numeric values drift, path references outdated, internal contradictions accumulate. No automated detection of documentation staleness. Periodic exhaustive line-by-line verification catches what casual reading misses. Status: resolved - systematic code-to-doc alignment process with parallel exploration agents
3. LLM output truncation, JSON parsing failures, and format instability in batch generation (16 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-01, 2026-03-03, 2026-03-07, 2026-03-16, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19 Root Cause: Complex multi-day reports exceed 32K output token limits. Chinese-language nested JSON at edge of Claude’s format stability. Special character escaping unstable (Chinese quotation marks, technical term citations). Long-text fields exhibit truncation (especially final fields), unclosed strings, missing closing brackets in nested structures >3 levels deep. LLM generates incomplete/malformed JSON (quote escaping errors, missing closing brackets, mixed non-JSON text) especially in ultra-long structured output. Failure modes vary across sessions despite identical prompts. Status: workaround - layered fault tolerance (direct parsing→code block→brace matching→LLM repair with model escalation), manual JSON repair for complex cases, need automatic validation and retry
4. Spatial transcriptomics data alignment failures due to implicit index assumptions (6 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-09, 2026-03-10, 2026-03-11 Root Cause: Different loading functions (sc.read_10x_h5 vs sc.read_visium) and scanpy operations reorder barcodes unpredictably. Position-based array indexing silently maps to wrong spots when cache creation order differs from runtime reload order. Coordinate systems also vary between data sources. Spatial transcriptomics workflows involve multiple file I/O boundaries where index preservation is not guaranteed, but implicit position-based lookups are common anti-pattern. Status: resolved - explicit barcode-based alignment throughout pipeline
5. GPU resource management issues: idle processes, memory exhaustion, utilization bottlenecks (6 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-01, 2026-03-02, 2026-03-03, 2026-03-05, 2026-03-07 Root Cause: Idle model servers monopolizing GPU memory (Phoenix server 407GB across 7 GPUs at 0% utilization), action chunking reducing inference calls causing 10% GPU utilization, DataLoader num_workers=0 causing GPU waiting for CPU I/O, training duration estimation errors (13.5 days vs expected hours). High memory ≠ high utilization—needs multi-dimensional validation. Status: ongoing - case-by-case fixes but systemic monitoring lacking
6. Academic database name disambiguation failures causing merged profiles with incoherent paper lists (8 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-15, 2026-03-16, 2026-03-19 Root Cause: Common Chinese names like ‘Ming Yu’, ‘Xiaolong Wang’ match multiple researchers across disparate fields. Semantic Scholar merges based on name alone without institutional/domain verification. Single data source insufficient—lacks co-author clustering, topic consistency checks, temporal continuity validation. Affects ~30% of analyzed researchers. Status: workaround - multi-signal approach with co-author clustering, topic clustering, temporal continuity, institutional affiliation consistency
7. Cross-platform compatibility gaps in Tauri/Windows development (8 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-25, 2026-03-26, 2026-03-28 Root Cause: Windows-specific API differences (tray icon sizing 16-32px vs macOS 44px, SetWindowPos behavior with SWP_NOCOPYBITS, CREATE_NO_WINDOW flag requirements, taskbar positioning) and WebView2 rendering timing constraints. Platform abstractions (tauri_plugin_positioner) sacrifice accuracy for portability. Win32+WebView2 architecture has inherent 1-frame delay between native window operations and web re-layout. Status: resolved - platform-specific implementations with conditional compilation
8. Data synchronization issues in distributed collaboration across devices (5 occurrences) Dates: 2026-03-19, 2026-03-20, 2026-03-21 Root Cause: Rclone sync-all only downloaded logs not reports, causing finalized status checks to fail when other devices had already generated reports. Two-tier finalize mechanism (log + report) requires all sources finalized before marking report done. Historical False flags create permanent deadlock preventing report finalization. Must sync not only input data but also intermediate outputs for logic depending on output state. Status: resolved - complete state sync implemented
Human-AI Collaboration Trends Human-initiated insights: 92 items AI limitation patterns: Reactive debugging (rationalizing anomalies) rather than proactive suspicion of bugs AI limitation patterns: Missing resource validation before large-scale operations (training duration, dataset size) AI limitation patterns: Insufficient system-level thinking (treating files/modules independently vs holistic architecture) AI limitation patterns: Over-triggering plan mode despite user preference for direct execution AI limitation patterns: Lack of session-to-session memory and pattern recognition across related tasks AI limitation patterns: Over-reliance on static code analysis without runtime validation (missed base class execution paths) AI limitation patterns: Premature convergence to implementation: excessive AskUserQuestion/ExitPlanMode when requirements still evolving AI limitation patterns: Over-engineering documentation despite explicit constraints AI limitation patterns: Failed to proactively detect zero-shot constraint violations AI limitation patterns: Cannot autonomously detect self-generated JSON format errors—requires external validation AI limitation patterns: Over-reliance on exploratory agents when direct requirements given AI limitation patterns: Failed to anticipate edge cases in automated workflows (git stash collision, shell script no-change scenarios) AI limitation patterns: Initial architectural proposals optimized for minimal code change over correctness AI limitation patterns: Shallow analysis in auto-execution phases without explicit depth requirements Improvement areas: Develop systematic validation checklists for training launches (dataset size, per-step time, total duration, GPU allocation) Improvement areas: Implement proactive anomaly detection (metric inconsistencies, configuration mismatches) before user reports Improvement areas: Build session continuity for recurring task patterns (batch report generation workflow) Improvement areas: Enhance architectural reasoning to infer multi-source systems from individual script capabilities Improvement areas: Improve cost-benefit analysis for exploration depth vs implementation speed Improvement areas: Proactively question architectural assumptions when fixing symptoms repeatedly Improvement areas: Understand when NOT to use parallel agents—holistic architectural judgment better kept in primary context Improvement areas: Add self-validation loops for structured output (JSON schema checks before returning) Improvement areas: Balance efficiency optimization vs user preference for consistency Improvement areas: Recognize ‘rejection loop detection’: after 2-3 consecutive tool rejections, switch from ‘fix the plan’ to ‘what’s blocking us?’ clarification Improvement areas: Anticipate platform-specific constraints earlier through systematic research before proposing solutions Improvement areas: Quantify context costs and performance implications upfront rather than after user inquiry Improvement areas: Proactively suggest quality metrics and observable success criteria before implementation Monthly Learnings Digest Architecture (architecture) VLA temporal context dependency requires offline trajectory-based error injection, not online physics perturbation. Solution: replay N-1 frames to build context window, inject error at frame N, let VLA infer freely. This shifts from ‘initial state perturbation’ to ’trajectory interruption’ matching real-world failure modes. Key insight: VLA models need behavioral history to make contextually appropriate recovery decisions. (Source: 2026-03-10) Foundation Models’ key advantage for cross-sample tasks: provide shared embedding space without joint training. Zero-shot cross-section retrieval fundamentally requires this—no per-sample optimization can substitute. Batch correction (Harmony) and semantic alignment are orthogonal properties: linear correction cannot handle non-linear dimension meaning misalignment from independent training. Single-slice clustering and cross-slice tasks require different encoders. (Source: 2026-03-16, 2026-03-19, 2026-03-23) ECL (Evolving Constraint Language) methodology with adversarial review catches critical issues pre-implementation: 12+ findings across 4 projects including CRITICAL-level bugs. Phase 7 Red-Blue confrontation exposes design flaws at far lower cost than post-implementation refactoring. Persistent ECL documents + Feature Guard Protocol prevent cross-session regression via disk-persistent guards that survive context compaction. (Source: 2026-03-24, 2026-03-25, 2026-03-26, 2026-03-28) Ultra-large-scale text analysis paradigm: ①automatic segmentation (150K chars/chunk ~15-20 papers), ②semantic merge (LLM-driven not concatenation), ③dynamic timeout (300s+120s/50K chars), ④caching mechanism. All four essential for deep-wide mode usability. Breaks single-LLM capacity limit enabling arbitrary-scale full-text analysis. Pattern applies broadly to chunked LLM processing workflows. (Source: 2026-03-19) Single-intent classification is architectural constraint unfixable with keyword hacks. CalendarPro’s 7-layer keyword override system treated symptoms of broken abstraction. Multi-intent LLM analysis eliminates need for all heuristics. Keyword hacks (always-general, context-dependent) were symptoms, not solutions. EventBus + BackgroundCoordinator + ContextAssembler pattern enables modular AI assistants with pub-sub decoupling. (Source: 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19) Git vs rclone responsibility boundary: git manages reproducible public code/config (requirements.txt, scripts, CLAUDE.md), rclone manages personal data/runtime outputs (logs, reports, cache, results, content). Mixing causes repo bloat (94MB), privacy risks, collaboration obstacles. Clear separation establishes data privacy boundary and enables independent versioning strategies. (Source: 2026-03-20) Hub+spoke design pattern for multi-variant systems: shared general framework (hub) + on-demand specific configurations (spokes) prevents both duplication and prompt dilution. Applied successfully to optimize skill (4 languages) and cross-platform code (conditional compilation). Single-language focus directive is key mechanism preventing dilution when processing one variant. (Source: 2026-03-25, 2026-03-26) Cross-attention on single tokens degenerates to linear projection. Spatial transcriptomics provides natural solution: k-NN neighbors form K/V sequences encoding neighborhood context. This pattern (spatial neighbors as attention context) is broadly applicable to spatial omics architectures. Avoids single-token degradation while preserving biological spatial structure. (Source: 2026-03-09) MCP tool design principle: tools for Claude Code should return content by default (markdown reports, JSON data) with optional file persistence, enabling in-context use without filesystem round-trips. When wrapping CLI tools for protocol-based systems, stdout/stderr isolation is first-class architectural concern requiring context managers for clean, reversible redirection. (Source: 2026-03-09) Multi-source data architectures (demo injection, policy rollout injection, natural failure capture) should be explicit in documentation as first-class citizens. Two-phase data collection (seed discovery + deterministic replay) separates stochasticity from data quality. Single source of truth pattern: Centralize auto-generated content in staging directory with link-or-copy sync to final locations—prevents dual-write inconsistencies. (Source: 2026-03-07, 2026-03-22) Debugging (debugging) Evaluation metric distortion often cascades from multiple bugs: BC-RNN’s demo_len=0 + trigger_step=0 + transport always False + successful RP≠1.0 jointly caused RP drop from 0.5 to 0.003. Training-vs-evaluation mismatch systematic checklist: observation keys, environment initialization, action space bounds, data normalization, camera setup. Silent failures in data preprocessing (apply_tree strict=False) allow scale mismatches to propagate through entire training. (Source: 2026-03-01, 2026-03-05, 2026-03-06, 2026-03-07) Name disambiguation #1 data quality issue in automated researcher profiling—affects ~30% analyzed. Requires multi-signal approach: co-author clustering, topic clustering, temporal continuity, institutional affiliation consistency. Single data source (Semantic Scholar) insufficient. Faculty homepages more authoritative than co-authorship inference. LLMs can serve as knowledge bases for unstructured metadata (conference awards, paper prestige) when APIs don’t provide it. (Source: 2026-03-15, 2026-03-16, 2026-03-19) MimicGen augmentation fundamentally about coordinate transformation, not noise injection. pos_delta (source→target offset) must be applied to action trajectories. Without this, 10→1000 augmentation collapses due to <5% success rate. Coherent action sequences more effective than per-frame noise for robotic manipulation. Coordinate transformation bugs can be masked by state replay (set_sim_state_flat), surfacing only during fresh initialization. (Source: 2026-03-16, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19) PyTorch 2.9 + CUDA 12.9 on RTX 2080 Ti has cuBLAS bug causing illegal memory access on large matrix operations. Workaround: mini-batch processing. Zero-padding for architectural compatibility severely dilutes gradient signals (56% in RoboTwin case) if included in loss computation—always mask loss to real dimensions only. PyTorch/torchvision have strict version pairing requiring explicit pinning. (Source: 2026-03-09, 2026-03-10, 2026-03-11, 2026-03-12) Spatial transcriptomics workflows require explicit barcode-based alignment at every junction. Position-based matching breaks when adata reloaded from different sources or reordered by scanpy. Solution: maintain bc_to_cache_idx dictionaries throughout pipeline, use barcode lookup for all coordinate assignments. Never assume index preservation across file I/O boundaries. (Source: 2026-03-09, 2026-03-10, 2026-03-11) Hypothesis-driven debugging with parallel investigation scales better than sequential trial-and-error: 3 subagents investigating H1/H2/H3 concurrently accumulate evidence faster. Intelligent dual-track (static code analysis + instrumented runtime probing) preserves speed while adding depth only when needed. Each hypothesis needs isolated instrument→run→analyze→cleanup cycle with Git safety checkpoints. (Source: 2026-03-27, 2026-03-28) Multi-layer feedback loops require simultaneous intervention at all layers: Data (payload equality checks, grace periods) + Window (settle delays, throttling) + Computation (boundary conditions). Fixing single layer only weakens but doesn’t eliminate the cycle. Observable success criteria (like footer ‘Xs ago’ text position) better than abstract metrics for debugging UI issues. (Source: 2026-03-27, 2026-03-28) Distributed collaboration state sync design principle: must sync not only input data (logs), but also intermediate outputs (reports), otherwise logic depending on output state (finalized checks) fails across devices. Complete state sync prevents resource waste (repeated LLM calls). Two-tier finalize mechanism requires all sources finalized before marking report done. (Source: 2026-03-19, 2026-03-20) Documentation-code synchronization requires parallel exploration agents (one per major directory), verify all numbers from filesystem/JSON, never trust document claims. Large refactoring must follow topological ordering: migrate depended functions first, then delete source files, ensuring every intermediate state runnable. Context loss requires tracing complete call chain from data collection through usage. (Source: 2026-03-01, 2026-03-02, 2026-03-04, 2026-03-05, 2026-03-15, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19) Class imbalance in binary classification requires explicit gradient balancing. Low overall loss can mask poor minority class learning when 96% samples are one class. pos_weight effectively rebalances gradient contributions—pos_weight=10 raised effective positive ratio from 4% to ~30%. Exception handlers should never use empty pass—at minimum log with logger.error/warning to prevent ‘Schrödinger’s state’. (Source: 2026-03-05, 2026-03-07, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19) Tools (tools) CSS transitions affecting layout dimensions trigger ResizeObserver on every frame. Solution: Delay layout change until after visual transition completes (e.g., ‘opacity 0.8s ease, max-height 0s linear 0.8s’). Win32+WebView2 architecture has inherent 1-frame delay between native and web operations. Only position:fixed bypasses this timing dependency. SWP_NOCOPYBITS breaks WebView2—each frame shows blank until browser re-renders. (Source: 2026-03-26, 2026-03-27, 2026-03-28) Tauri v2 cross-platform constraints: set_title() macOS-only, requires set_tooltip() fallback; capability system requires explicit permission declarations (missing permissions cause silent failures); tray icon size differences (16-32px DPI-aware Windows vs 22px macOS); plugin abstractions sacrifice accuracy—pixel-perfect tray positioning needs platform-specific Win32 API. CREATE_NO_WINDOW (0x0800_0000) flag prevents console popup for Windows GUI subprocess. (Source: 2026-03-24, 2026-03-26, 2026-03-28) macOS TCC permission mechanism: non-sandbox apps cannot create security-scoped bookmarks (only non-scoped bookmarks that resolve URLs without granting access), causing persistent file access to fail across app restarts. Must enable App Sandbox (com.apple.security.app-sandbox) for persistent file permissions. Combine sandbox (solves root cause) + NSOpenPanel fallback (temporary authorization) for robust defense-in-depth. (Source: 2026-03-02, 2026-03-07, 2026-03-12) subprocess.run with capture_output=True has no stdin—any interactive prompts hang indefinitely. Always use –yes/–non-interactive flags for package managers in automation. PowerShell execution policy blocks .ps1 wrappers on Windows; .cmd wrappers work universally. Windows PowerShell: inline Python via stdin (python -) bypasses execution policy restrictions. (Source: 2026-03-17, 2026-03-21, 2026-03-22) JAX requires explicit backend selection via JAX_PLATFORMS, not just CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES masking. Conda-installed CUDA toolkits place headers in architecture-specific paths (targets/x86_64-linux/include) not top-level. Multi-user shared conda env import conflicts: set PYTHONPATH=/user/src:$PYTHONPATH to prioritize user’s source. HPC compute nodes typically lack internet—disable cloud logging (WANDB_MODE=disabled) for batch jobs. (Source: 2026-03-01, 2026-03-05, 2026-03-07) Kubernetes PID namespace isolation prevents nvidia-smi from showing container processes. Solution: /proc//fd/ inspection for /dev/nvidia* device files + CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES env var. Monitoring tools opening all GPUs without CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES are false positives. Collect information within respective namespaces then aggregate. (Source: 2026-03-10, 2026-03-11) Backward compatibility requires comprehensive coverage: CLI entry points, module import paths, config file formats, data file structures. Shim re-export pattern enables zero-modification compatibility for downstream consumers. Migration smoke tests (47+ import contract tests run before and after refactoring) create safety net for confident large-scale changes. (Source: 2026-03-24, 2026-03-25) git filter-repo correct tool for purging large files/sensitive data from history (22MB→516KB). Requires full workflow: backup .git, run filter-repo, restore structural files (.gitkeep), force-push, update all clones. Automated cleanup workflows must distinguish tracked vs untracked files: git restore handles tracked; untracked need explicit rm/rmdir. Prefer stateless cleanup over stash stack collision risk. (Source: 2026-03-20, 2026-03-28) LLM long-text JSON output fault tolerance requires layered mechanism: direct parsing→code block extraction→brace matching→LLM repair (model escalation haiku→sonnet→opus). Single strategy cannot cover all failure modes. Chinese quotation marks ("") must be escaped as ASCII double quotes. All failures should be logged for manual review. Error recovery should implement cost-graduated escalation rather than binary fail/succeed. (Source: 2026-03-13, 2026-03-16, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-19) Multi-phase prompt workflows need explicit continuation instructions at EVERY boundary: CONTINUOUS EXECUTION MANDATE (global) + ‘→ NEXT:’ transitions (per-phase) + multi-turn protocols (per-phase). Auto-execution phases require quantitative depth requirements (minimum N findings, M layers, 1+ probe) to prevent ‘fast but shallow’ behavior—qualitative ‘be thorough’ insufficient. (Source: 2026-03-25, 2026-03-26) Skills’ context cost is quantifiable: 15K-20K tokens for 250+ skill indices. Project-specific allocation superior to global installation—reduced 50→28 global skills via three-layer strategy: move relevant to project level, delete unrelated language skills, remove non-coding domain skills. 40% context reduction without functionality loss. (Source: 2026-03-25) Domain Knowledge (domain) STAIG spatial graph contrastive learning framework exhibits high robustness to gene encoder choice: Teddy-G 400M (1024d, ARI=0.495) ≈ PCA 50d (ARI=0.497). STAIG’s spatial graph structure dominates clustering performance, gene embedding quality contribution diluted. Foundation Model cross-domain transfer gap: TEDDY disease classification SOTA but spatial clustering underperforms PCA due to optimization target mismatch (disease discrimination vs variance maximization) and 1024d high-dimensional space disadvantaging KMeans. (Source: 2026-03-29, 2026-03-30, 2026-03-31) Recovery demo data augmentation three-layer strategy: scene configuration (different error start states), cross-degree (D0→D1/D2 via rotation), cross-subtype (within-RBG cross-augmentation). Combined with MimicGen trajectory segmentation, reduces data requirements 81% (1,740→329 demos). Recovery Behavior Groups abstract 29 subtypes by motor primitives enabling systematic data collection. (Source: 2026-03-16, 2026-03-17) Academic trajectory analysis multi-angle validation: Through iterative analysis of same researcher’s different paper subsets (1 to 100 papers), build stereoscopic cognition from micro to macro. Year-stratified sampling (group by year, sort within year by award weight then citations, take ≤10/year) preserves temporal structure and maintains both trajectory narrative and top work per period. ‘Few but deep’ iterative > ‘many but shallow’ one-shot. (Source: 2026-03-13, 2026-03-19) RM-IDEAL uses WWL (Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman) graph kernel for spatial niche matching: iterative neighbor label hashing → node feature distributions → Wasserstein optimal transport between subgraphs. DLPFC tissue layer characteristics directly impact cross-sample matching: Layer_1 (thin, boundary-clear) and Layer_4 (cell-dense, unique) show Spearman ρ>0.6; Layer_3 (largest, heterogeneous) shows ρ<-0.2. (Source: 2026-03-05, 2026-03-09, 2026-03-11) Token Usage Statistics Total: $449.30 — 688.2M tokens
Peak Day: 2026-03-07 — $90.64 / 109.1M tokens
Daily Average: $17.28
summary: March 2026 achieved transformative breakthroughs across AI systems, robotics, and research infrastructure through systematic application of evolving constraint methodology. Error Recovery Benchmark completed v5.0 architectural redesign shifting from online physics injection to offline trajectory-based VLA context replay, while CalendarPro eliminated single-intent bottleneck via multi-intent LLM classification. Gadget toolkit evolved from quick-overview to deep-excavation research system supporting arbitrary-scale full-text analysis through chunked LLM merge, integrated with MCP server transformation and rclone sync infrastructure establishing clear git-vs-data boundaries. MIHD spatial transcriptomics proved Foundation Models achieve 100% cross-section retrieval vs 14% for PCA while discovering STAIG’s surprising robustness to gene encoder choice. TokenMonitor completed cross-platform migration with polished float ball UI, and ccplan skill introduced 10-phase requirement engineering with adversarial review catching 12+ critical issues pre-implementation. Month exposed recurring patterns of dependency version fragility, documentation drift, and LLM JSON instability while demonstrating 4x speedup via wave-based parallel agent execution and 81% demo reduction through Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy.
title: Monthly Summary 2026-03

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-31 Daily Overview Completed: Prepared the MIHD paper by defining the framework for Figures 1-6, integrating Leiden clustering with STHD pathology annotation ground truth, and running the DLPFC Leiden baseline and HD multimodal evaluation experiments. Methodology: Generated a structured weekly plan using ccplan, parallelly modified multiple modules of the pipeline (evaluation_planner, runner, data_loader), submitted the SLURM job, and fixed bugs in conda activation and vision extraction paths by running the script locally instead of remotely. Impact: Obtained comparison data between Leiden and KMeans (plus 15% ARI) and an evaluation baseline for HD STHD ground truth, providing quantitative support for Figures 2/4 of the paper. The paper’s framework is now ready for writing. We have completed this week’s work plan for the MIHD paper, integrated Leiden clustering, and added STHD ground truth integration. We also successfully ran the DLPFC Leiden baseline and HD multimodal evaluation experiments, obtaining crucial experimental data.
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Figure 1-6 Framework Planning — Followed the structure of four papers: MISO (Nat Methods), STAIG (Nat Comms), QueST (arXiv), and Yuan (Nat Methods). Defined the framework for Figure 1, the DLPFC benchmark for Figure 2, the ablation study for Figure 3, the HD application for Figure 4, the cross-slice query for Figure 5, and the bio-discovery query for Figure 6. Written the ECL YAML and plans.md files. ✅ FEAT-003: STHD Ground Truth Integration — Added the _load_sthd_annotations() function to automatically load 8um_squares_annotation.csv (545913 entries). Combined CE and Tumor data (Neoplasm + Non-neoplastic Epithelium → Epithelium). 17502/17502 spots were perfectly matched, with 3 clusters identified. The data_preparer.py file saved the ground truth to the cache. 🔄 FEAT-005: HD Full Fusion Strategy Evaluation — Ran the HD pipeline locally, completing 10/13 experiments (pca, mlp, uni2, hipt, resnet50, concat, mean, element_wise_sum, attention, staig_fusion). The status of basic_contrastive, adln_attention, and llava_mlp is pending confirmation. 🔄 FEAT-002: Running Leiden Baseline Experiments — Submitted the SLURM job 44829398 (fixed the issue after conda activation). There are 99 experiments in total (9 combinations × 11 sections). 7/9 methods were completed, but staig_fusion_leiden and llava_mlp_leiden were not completed due to timeout after the first submission (4 hours), and are still running after the second submission (8 hours). Implementation & Fixes ✅ FEAT-001: Integrating Leiden Clustering into the Pipeline — Modified utils/clustering.py to add binary search to match the target number of clusters. Added a clustering_method field in evaluation_planner.py to affect the key/exp_name. Used job.clustering_method in runner.py. Added leiden_baseline experiments in pipeline_config.yaml. ✅ FEAT-004: Supporting ARI/NMI in HD Evaluation — Modified runner.py to calculate ARI/NMI when HD has ground truth, replacing the previous logic that only calculated Silhouette. ✅ Fixed HD Vision Extraction Bug — VisionExtractor did not receive the crop_dir parameter, resulting in failed HD vision extraction (could not find cropped_fullres.tif). Fixed vision_extractor.py by adding the crop_dir field, and models/vision_extractors.py by adding the image_dir_override kwarg. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Leiden clustering needs to be compared with KMeans, but Leiden controls the number of clusters based on the resolution parameter, making it impossible to accurately match the number of clusters. Solution: Implement binary search in cluster_with_leiden(). Iterate through the [0.01, 10] range to adjust the resolution until the number of clusters matches, with a maximum of 20 iterations.
Key Insight: The relationship between Leiden’s resolution and the number of clusters is monotonic. Binary search can efficiently match the desired number of clusters, allowing fair comparison with KMeans.
General Issues 2. HD pipeline Vision Extraction failed: VisionExtractor used the data_root/section_id path to find images, but HD data was in the crop_dir, causing 11/13 experiments to fail. Solution: Add the crop_dir parameter to VisionExtractor.init. Pass image_dir_override=crop_dir to extract(). Use image_dir_override preferentially in _load_image_and_coords(). Pass crop_dir to VisionExtractor when creating it in runner.py.
Key Insight: The image storage structures of DLPFC and HD data are completely different. The pipeline needs to explicitly distinguish the image paths for these two datasets.
3. SLURM scripts used source activate General, causing conda environment activation to fail. All Leiden experiments ran on GPU-free login nodes. Solution: Replace source activate in SLURM scripts with eval $(conda shell.bash hook) && conda activate. Resubmit the job.
Key Insight: SLURM nodes on the DCC cluster need to initialize the conda shell hook explicitly.不能直接使用source activate。
4. In run_pipeline.py, there is a default logic error in the dataset configuration. When the hd_global section exists, it automatically switches to the HD dataset, causing the Leiden DLPFC experiment to run in HD mode. Solution: Change the default value of the dataset to DLPFC. Only switch to the HD dataset when –dataset is explicitly specified.
Key Insight: The existence of the hd_global section in the configuration file should not affect the default dataset selection.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Layout Design of Paper Figures 1-6 Role Approach Human Referred to the structures of three competitive papers: MISO, STAIG, and QueST. Emphasized the unique value of biological findings and queries. AI The AI system retrieved the specific figure structures from the four papers. Proposed a detailed layout for Figures 1-6 and section structures. The final plan was determined based on the structure of STAIG. Difference Analysis: Humans provide creative direction and reference, while AI retrieves details and integrates them into a specific plan. The final plan is the result of collaboration.
CE+Tumor Ground Truth Merging Strategy Role Approach Human Clearly specified that Neoplasm (Tumor) and Non-neoplastic Epithelium (CE) should be merged into one cluster as the ground truth for HD evaluation. AI The AI asked about the exact meaning of CE+Tumor, but could not infer it directly from the original description. Difference Analysis: Domain knowledge (pathology) drives design decisions, which requires human input. AI can implement the plan correctly, but needs human clarification of intentions. P5 T Cell Meaning Role Approach Human P5 refers to Patient 5 in the STHD paper, not a patient in our own HD dataset. AI The AI mistakenly thought that there was a Patient ID in the HD dataset, confused by the fact that HD only has a single sample. Difference Analysis: Humans understand the goal of the query by referring to external literature (STAIG paper), not by referring to internal patient groups in the current dataset. Implementation Level Do HD Experiments Require SLURM Job Submission? Role Approach Human Directly pointed out that HD data is small (17502 spots), so it can be run locally without SLURM. AI The AI assumed that all experiments require GPU computing resources. First, it submitted the SLURM job (44830562). Difference Analysis: Humans understand better the relationship between data size and computing resources. AI is too conservative in using cluster resources. Canceling the SLURM job and running locally saves time waiting in queues.
AI Limitations General Limitations Did not consider the structural differences in the image paths of DLPFC and HD datasets. VisionExtractor implemented a hardcoded lookup logic for data_root/section_id, which needs to be fixed after human discovery of failures. Misjudged the way conda is activated in the HPC environment. SLURM scripts used source activate, which does not work on compute nodes. This caused environmental problems in the first two jobs. Could not determine whether the 17502 spots of HD dataset require GPU/SLURM resources. Over-reliance on cluster resources needs human correction. Launched a background agent for web searches, but it took too long, resulting in poor user experience. Lack of management of asynchronous tasks’ time consumption. Learnings Key Learnings Leiden clustering performed similarly to KMeans on HD data (pca_leiden ARI≈0.333 vs pca ARI≈0.288 on DLPFC). It significantly improved for simple gene-only methods, but its benefits decreased for graph-based learning methods like STAIG. STAIG’s advantage comes from its spatial graph structure, which is independent of clustering algorithms. Simple fusion strategies (concat/mean/element_wise_sum) on HD had lower ARI than single-modal PCA (0.19-0.20 vs 0.351), indicating that naive fusion may lead to information dilution in large-scale HD data. Attention and staig_fusion were exceptions (attention ARI=0.342). On HD data (STHD Ground Truth, 3 clusters: Epithelium/Connective Tissue/Smooth Muscle), vision-only UNI2 had the highest NMI (0.387), but ARI was lower than PCA (0.344 vs 0.351). NMI and ARI may not be consistent for different data sets, so both metrics should be reported together. The HD part of MISO (Nat Methods) used Visium HD colorectal cancer to show the spatial expression of SPP1 (tumor-associated macrophages) and CEACAM6. This can serve as a reference framework for verifying markers genes in MIHD Fig 4. Practical Learnings The matching between STHD annotation files (8um_squares_annotation.csv) and adata obs can be achieved using r_big/c_big coordinates. The matching rate can reach 100%. After combining CE and Tumor data, 3 meaningful clusters were identified (Outside can be ignored for 4 spots). Conversation Summaries ✅ ccplan generated MIHD paper preparation weekly plan (deadline: 4/5) 04:10:39.284 | claude_code The user submitted 7 tasks this week via /ccplan (Leiden baseline, HD pipeline, Query, paper planning, etc.). The AI searched through the code library and papers, generating the layout scheme for Figures 1-6 and the ECL YAML for 4 parallel workflows. After the user approved the plan, the AI created 7 tasks and established dependencies. Three tracks (Leiden/STHD GT/paper structure) started simultaneously.
✅ FEAT-001/003/004: Realized Leiden clustering and STHD ground truth integration 04:10:39.284 | claude_code Realized both Leiden clustering (binary search to match n_clusters) and STHD pathology annotation loading (combining CE and Tumor). This involved 6 files: clustering.py, evaluation_planner.py, runner.py, data_loader.py, data_preparer.py, and pipeline_config.yaml. All import tests passed. STHD matching rate was 100% (17502/17502). HD load_section_data() correctly returned 3 clusters.
🔄 HD local experiments completed, results written to plans.md 04:10:39.284 | claude_code Ran the HD pipeline locally ([[HYMTPH_0]]), completing 10/13 experiments. Key results: PCA gene-only ARI=0.351 was optimal. UNI2 vision NMI was the highest (0.387). Simple fusion (concat/mean) performed worse than single-modal methods. Updated the results of Leiden and HD to docs/plans.md, adding key findings #6 (Leiden +15% on gene-only) and #7 (HD 3-cluster evaluation established).
🔄 Submitted SLURM experiment job and fixed two bugs (conda activation + vision path) 04:10:39.284 | claude_code When submitting the SLURM job for the first time (Leiden 44767007, HD 44767008), two bugs were found: conda activation failed and HD vision extraction path was incorrect. After fixing these bugs, the Leiden job was resubmitted (44829398, 8h). After the user pointed out that HD data was small enough to run locally, the HD SLURM job was canceled (44830562), and it was executed locally instead.
Token Usage Total: $5.80 — 9.4M tokens
summary: 为 MIHD 论文准备完成了本周工作规划、Leiden 聚类集成、STHD Ground Truth 集成，并成功运行 DLPFC Leiden baseline 与 HD 全流程评估，获得关键实验数据
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-31

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-30 Daily Overview What was done: Integrated TEDDY-G 400M into the MIHD pipeline, completed gene embedding extraction for all 11 DLPFC slices, evaluated 5 non-STAIG fusion strategies, and conducted STAIG fusion experiments. How it was done: Extracted embeddings piece by piece in a dedicated conda teddy environment and saved them in standard cache formats. Fixed an error in the staig_gene_feat cache in evaluation_planner.py, and ran Phase 1 extraction and Phase 2 evaluation in stages. Impact: Confirmed that Teddy+UNI2+STAIG (ARI≈0.495) is comparable to PCA+UNI2+STAIG (ARI≈0.497), revealing the robustness of the STAIG framework towards gene encoder selection. This provides experimental evidence for future encoder replacement strategies. Completed the embedding extraction and multi-strategy fusion benchmark for TEDDY-G 400M on all 11 DLPFC slices, and wrote a W13 weekly report summarizing the findings from last week’s experiments.
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Teddy + STAIG Fusion Evaluation — Completed evaluation for 10/11 slices (151676 failed due to model collapse); Avg ARI=0.495, compared with PCA+STAIG’s 0.497. Teddy outperformed in 6/10 slices. ✅ Full Extraction of TEDDY Embeddings (11 Slices) — Extracted 1024d gene embeddings for 11 DLPFC slices (151508–151676) in a conda teddy environment and cached them in embeddings_cache/gene/teddy/. Total size: 185MB. ✅ Non-STAIG Fusion Evaluation with Teddy (5 Strategies × 11 Slices) — Evaluated 5 configurations in Phase 2: teddy_only, teddy_uni2_concat, teddy_uni2_mean, teddy_uni2_attention, teddy_uni2_llava_mlp. Best ARI for llava_mlp was 0.305. ✅ UNI2 staig_strict Visual Feature Extraction — Extracted UNI2 staig_strict variants for STAIG fusion (256×256 patches, no ImageNet normalization, frequency domain filtering), covering all 11 slices. Implementation & Fixes ✅ W13 Weekly Report — Combined docs/plans.md, git history, and file timestamps to create docs/WEEKLY_REPORT_2026_W13.md, summarizing two main experiments and key conclusions from last week. ✅ Fixed Error in Evaluation_planner.py Staig_gene_feat Cache — In 151676, the planner checked only 3000 genes instead of 5000, causing a sys.exit(1). Now it dynamically checks the actual file. ✅ Research on TEDDY Foundation Model Background — Retrieved the full technical details of the TEDDY paper (arXiv:2503.03485) through web search: bidirectional Transformer + supervised ontology pre-training (disease/tissue/cell type), 116M cell training data, and 5 scale options (10M–400M). ✅ Feasibility Assessment for TokenMonitor HPC Construction — Checked RHEL 9.7 HPC environment: Node.js/npm were available, but systems libraries like webkit2gtk4.1-devel were missing. No sudo privileges allowed, so TokenMonitor couldn’t be built on HPC. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Teddy’s gene-only ARI (0.186) is worse than PCA’s (0.255). The Foundation Model fails to surpass simple baselines in clustering tasks. Solution: Analyzed the root cause: Teddy aims to classify diseases rather than maximize variance. The 1024d high-dimensional space worsens KMeans performance. The training data is dissociated scRNA-seq, which differs from spatial transcriptomics distribution. Key Insight: The fundamental tension between the “generalized representation” of the base model and its “clustering adaptability” exists. PCA directly maximizes data variance, making it naturally suitable for KMeans. However, the Foundation Model learns disease-specific representations, leading to systematic transfer losses in downstream clustering tasks.
General Issues 2. evaluation_planner.py incorrectly cached staig_gene_feat for 151676, causing Phase 2 to terminate with sys.exit(1). Solution: Modified the has_staig_gene_feat check logic to dynamically parse the actual number of top_genes in slice configurations (151676 used 5000 instead of default 3000), correctly identifying cached data. Key Insight: The implicit configuration split between slice-specific configurations (top_genes from staig_utils.py’s num_gene field) and the planner’s hard-coded defaults can lead to unnoticed false negatives.
3. In the STAIG fusion evaluation, slice 151676 experienced model collapse, resulting in ineffective clustering results. Solution: Skipped this slice and reported the average ARI of 10/11 slices (ARI=0.495). Key Insight: 151676 is a known difficult slice for STAIG. Its collapse is unrelated to the gene encoder and is a systemic issue caused by the data characteristics of this slice.
4. TokenMonitor couldn’t be built on HPC due to lack of Rust, webkit2gtk4.1-devel dependencies, and no sudo privileges. Solution: Suggested three approaches: build the binary on a local machine with permissions, ask a HPC administrator to install dependencies, or use GitHub Actions CI to build a Linux package. Note that Tauri applications require GTK/WebKit system libraries, which are typically unavailable in HPC environments, and the module system doesn’t provide these libraries. HPC is not designed as a target environment for GUI applications.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Should we continue with STAIG fusion after non-STAIG results are available? Role Approach Human After seeing that llava_mlp’s best ARI was only 0.305 and other fusion strategies were weaker than PCA, I requested additional STAIG fusion experiments. AI When presenting non-STAIG results, AI only provided a list of options without prioritizing them. Difference Analysis: Humans have prior knowledge about which fusion strategy is most likely to match SOTA. Therefore, they actively push for it. AI tends to leave decision-making to users and doesn’t make proactive predictions.
Implementation Level Discovery and Fixing of the Staig_gene_feat False Positive Bug Role Approach Human Not involved; fully discovered and fixed by AI. AI AI traced the trigger path of sys.exit, checked the actual file (5000.npz vs 3000.npz), identified the implicit configuration split, and fixed the issue. Difference Analysis: This is an active contribution from AI. Humans didn’t notice the bug, while AI independently discovered and fixed the issue during Phase 2 troubleshooting.
Method of Organizing Last Week’s Work Role Approach Human Asked about the plan and actions taken last week, expecting a comprehensive summary. AI AI combined the plans in docs/plans.md, Git log timeline, file timestamps, and experiment results to reconstruct last week’s work from multiple perspectives. Difference Analysis: Humans asked questions at a higher level, while AI systematically broke down the work into layers like “plan vs execution vs unfinished,” and provided quantitative support.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Failed to deeply analyze the root cause of the STAIG model collapse in 151676. It was simply attributed to being a “known difficult slice.” No attempts were made to adjust hyperparameters like tau or num_epochs to avoid this issue. General Limitations Failed to identify the hardcoded problem with staig_gene_feat topgenes in evaluation_planner.py. Only discovered the root cause after sys.exit actually triggered. The background task progress monitoring strategy is inefficient. Using multiple rounds of sleep+ls polling, rather than more elegant solutions like inotifywait or structured progress files, leads to a lot of waiting time. There was a syntax error when constructing multiple-line Python f-string in bash commands (unterminated string literal). The command needs to be modified to work properly. Learnings Key Learnings The STAIG fusion framework is highly robust towards gene encoders: Teddy’s 1024d model (ARI=0.495) and PCA’s 50d model (ARI=0.497) perform almost the same under the STAIG framework. This indicates that the STAIG’s spatial graph contrast learning mechanism dominates clustering performance, and the impact of gene embedding quality is greatly diluted. The Foundation Model suffers from systematic transfer losses in cross-domain downstream tasks. Teddy is SOTA in disease classification (ACC=0.72), but its performance in spatial transcriptomics clustering is inferior to PCA’s (0.186 vs 0.255). The reason is the mismatch in optimization goals (differentiation vs variance maximization). HVG1500 (direct concatenation of original high-variance genes, without dimensionality reduction) has an ARI of 0.330 on single slice 151673, which is better than all Foundation Models. This suggests that high-dimensional original features may be beneficial on some slices by preserving more local variance information. Practical Learnings The implicit split between slice-specific configurations (e.g., staig num_gene) and the pipeline’s global default values is a high-risk bug. It should be dynamically parsed in the cache checking logic instead of being hardcoded. Conversation Summaries MIHD Pipeline ✅ TEDDY-G 400M Full DLPFC Benchmark: Embedding Extraction → Multi-Fusion Evaluation → STAIG Fusion 00:01:05.759 | claude_code In the DCC, extracted TEDDY 1024d embeddings for all 11 slices using a conda teddy environment. Then, evaluated 5 non-STAIG fusion strategies. The best ARI for gene-only was 0.186, and for llava_mlp it was 0.305. Users requested additional STAIG fusion. AI extracted UNI2 staig_strict visual features, identified and fixed the false positive bug in evaluation_planner.py where staig_gene_feat was checked as 5000 instead of 3000. Finally, completed the Teddy+STAIG evaluation for 10/11 slices (ARI=0.495), which is similar to PCA+STAIG’s performance (ARI=0.497).
✅ Review of MIHD Experiments Last Week and Generation of Weekly Report 21:13:39.566 | claude_code Users asked about the plan and actual work last week. AI combined the plans in docs/plans.md, Git log (latest commits ended on 2026-02-22, no new commits), file timestamps, and experiment results. It organized the work into two main tasks: 8 encoders benchmark (151673 single slice) and full Teddy fusion experiment. Based on this, AI wrote the docs/WEEKLY_REPORT_2026_W13.md, including the purpose, methods, results, and key conclusions of the experiments.
✅ Research and Introduction to TEDDY Foundation Model 22:58:07.566 | claude_code Users requested a detailed introduction to the TEDDY foundation model. AI searched for the complete technical details of the paper (arXiv:2503.03485, Merck/BCG X) through web searches. It included information on the bidirectional Transformer BERT style, 5 parameter sizes (10M–400M), TEDDY-G/TEDDY-X variants, supervised ontology pre-training (four special tokens: disease/tissue/cell type/sex), 116M cell training data, and comparison with scGPT/Geneformer. It also discussed the integration paths and limitations in relation to the MIHD project.
TokenMonitor 🔍 Feasibility Assessment for Building TokenMonitor on HPC (RHEL 9.7) 21:22:13.566 | claude_code Users asked about the current system information and whether TokenMonitor could be built. It was found that Node.js/npm were available, but Rust/Cargo wasn’t installed. Systems libraries like webkit2gtk4.1-devel were missing, and no relevant modules were found in the module system. There was no pre-built release on GitHub. It was suggested to install Rust using rustup, contact a HPC administrator to install system dependencies, or use GitHub Actions CI to build a Linux package. However, it was pointed out that this GUI application requires X11/Wayland support to run on HPC login nodes.

title: Bug Journal 2026-03-30

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W13 (2026-03-23 ~ 2026-03-29) This week’s core work focused on multi-model embedding expansion and issue triage for the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. On March 23, environment initialization was completed on the DCC HPC cluster, and historical challenges in the MIHD project were systematically documented — for the first time structuring the root cause of cross-section retrieval failure (incomparable embedding spaces) and the single-section ARI gap as formal documents. On March 29, four critical bugs in the TEDDY foundation model extraction script were successfully fixed, the first DLPFC section embedding extraction was completed, and batch runs were launched, establishing the foundation for a multi-model comparison benchmark.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-23 ~ 2026-03-29 Active Days 2 / 7 Total Conversations 6 Projects Involved 3 Completed Tasks 6 In-Progress Tasks 1 Total Tokens 18,874,903 Total Cost $11.79 Daily Average Cost $5.89 Project Progress MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics Multimodal Clustering (2 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Systematically reviewed historical issues and persisted them as docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md, including root cause analysis of cross-section retrieval failure Identified root cause of cross-section task failure: PCA/STAIG independent processing produces incomparable embedding coordinate systems; scGPT shared space achieves average SL@50=0.416 Fixed 4 critical bugs in the extract_teddy() script, successfully completed TEDDY embedding extraction for section 151508 (4384 spots) Launched batch TEDDY embedding extraction for the remaining 10 DLPFC sections, establishing a four-model comparison framework: scGPT/Geneformer/C2S/TEDDY Blockers:
⚠️ Single-section ARI remains below target (current 0.546 vs. target 0.60+); 4-phase ablation experiment plan not yet executed ⚠️ Root cause of mclust falling back to KMeans due to zero-variance embedding in section 151676 unknown ⚠️ Batch TEDDY embedding extraction (10 sections) still running as of March 29 DCC HPC Cluster Environment Initialization (1 active day) — ✅ completed Completed:
Configured rclone sync.json; completed sync of 4 data categories (summarize/research/test/website) via sync.py pull Investigated GPU resources: common (RTX 2080), scavenger-gpu (A6000/RTX 5000-6000 Ada), scavenger-h200 (H200×8) Installed cchelper skill to ~/.claude/skills/cchelper/ Key Tasks ✅ Fixed 4 critical bugs in extract_teddy() script (2026-03-29) — Discovered and fixed via systematic source code review: ① incorrect import of Tokenizer changed to GeneTokenizer; ② model forward parameter input_ids changed to gene_ids; ③ output access last_hidden_state changed to outputs[‘cell_emb’]; ④ added gene symbol to Ensembl ID mapping logic. ✅ MIHD project issue review and documentation (2026-03-23) — Reviewed current state of the MIHD project (best ARI 0.546, root cause of cross-section retrieval failure, list of fixed bugs), generated structured issue summary document docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md. ✅ TEDDY paper analysis and architecture evaluation (2026-03-29) — Analyzed arXiv 2503.03485, reviewed TEDDY model architecture (Teddy-G/X two encoding types, 6 variants, up to 400M parameters), evaluated performance on disease classification benchmarks (held-out donor multi-class classification 8% higher than Nicheformer, linear probe far superior to fine-tuning). 🔄 DLPFC embedding batch extraction (2026-03-29) — Extracting 1024-dimensional cell embeddings for 11 DLPFC sections in the teddy conda environment; section 151508 complete, remaining 10 sections still running. Issues and Solutions 1. TEDDY extraction script used a non-existent Tokenizer class and incorrect model API (input_ids, last_hidden_state), making the script completely non-functional [MIHD] (2026-03-29) Solution: Read TEDDY source code to confirm correct API: GeneTokenizer, gene_ids parameter, outputs[‘cell_emb’] output key, and added Ensembl ID mapping (60% of genes in the DLPFC dataset are mappable)
2. MIHD cross-section retrieval almost completely failed (10 out of 14 query directions with SL@50=0) [MIHD] (2026-03-23) Solution: Diagnosed root cause as PCA/STAIG independent processing per section producing incomparable embedding spaces; switched to scGPT embeddings (shared space), achieving average SL@50=0.416 with 100% hit rate
3. TEDDY inference with batch_size=32 caused GPU OOM (32GB VRAM), because each sequence is up to 2048 tokens long [MIHD] (2026-03-29) Solution: Reduced batch_size from 32 to 8, bringing memory usage under control
4. New DCC machine had no ~/.config/gadget/sync.json, preventing sync.py from running [DCC Environment Initialization] (2026-03-23) Solution: Inferred rclone_remote configuration from existing summarize config, manually created sync.json and verified remote directory structure before executing pull
Learnings Domain Knowledge (domain) In cross-section tasks in spatial transcriptomics, the comparability of embedding spaces (shared coordinate system) is more critical than local embedding quality. Foundation models (scGPT/TEDDY) are naturally suited for cross-section retrieval due to their fixed representation spaces; PCA/STAIG independent processing produces incomparable coordinate systems and is not suitable for cross-section tasks. The optimal method for single-section clustering (STAIG) and the optimal method for cross-section retrieval (scGPT) are different; different encoders should be selected for different task objectives, and a unified optimal solution should not be pursued. TEDDY-G (rank-value encoding) outperforms TEDDY-X (binned expression); linear probe far outperforms fine-tuning (RA: 0.56→0.93), indicating high embedding quality but susceptibility to overfitting during fine-tuning; 8% higher than Nicheformer on held-out donor multi-class classification. Tools (tools) Single-cell foundation models in the HuggingFace ecosystem (TEDDY, scGPT, etc.) have significantly different forward interfaces; standard HuggingFace interfaces cannot be assumed. Source code must be read to confirm the gene_ids parameter name and output dict structure. TEDDY’s vocab uses Ensembl IDs (43808 tokens) rather than gene symbols. Architecture (architecture) TEDDY uses rank-value encoding, retaining the top 2048 genes as a token sequence. The sequence length is longer than models like scGPT, resulting in higher VRAM requirements and the need for smaller batch sizes during inference. AI Usage Notes Effective patterns:
✓ Systematic code review (read source → verify API → compare implementation), proactively discovering hidden bugs unknown to the user, saving debugging time ✓ Choosing to confirm rather than guess when requirements are ambiguous (private file sync requirements), avoiding incorrect execution ✓ Persisting verbal analysis results as structured documents (PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md), completing knowledge externalization with user initiative Limitations:
✗ Cross-session memory not persisted: unable to recall previous TEDDY test discussions, requiring users to re-provide background information ✗ PDF parsing failure: unable to process binary content when directly fetching arXiv PDFs, requiring fallback to HTML version ✗ Relies on existing documentation for project summaries; cannot infer undocumented implicit progress or autonomously prioritize ablation experiments Outlook for Next Week Next week’s priorities: ① Confirm completion of batch TEDDY embedding extraction (10 DLPFC sections) and integrate four-model (scGPT/Geneformer/C2S/TEDDY) comparative evaluation results; ② Execute the 4-phase ablation experiments in the MIHD single-section ARI improvement plan, focusing on diagnosing the root cause of zero-variance embeddings in section 151676; ③ Based on multi-model comparison results, decide whether a combined encoder strategy is needed (scGPT for cross-section, STAIG for single-section clustering).
