title: Bug Journal 2026-03-24

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-23 Daily Overview Completed tasks: Synchronized gadget project data on the DCC HPC cluster, installed the cchelper skill, and conducted research on GPU resources. Additionally, I identified the key challenges in the MIHD spatial transcriptomics multimodal clustering project and documented them in a problem summary document. Execution method: Used rclone + sync.py to fetch data from Google Drive. Utilized SLURM commands (sinfo/sacctmgr) to query cluster resources. Manually installed the cchelper skill directory in ~/.claude/skills/. Then, I integrated all issues by analyzing the plans.md/RESULTS.md diagnostic reports. Impact: The new device DCC was configured from scratch to a working environment. The historical issues of the MIHD project were systematically organized into PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md, providing a structured entry point for subsequent ablation experiments and debugging. The DCC HPC cluster was initialized with rclone synchronization, GPU node research, and skill installation. The current issues of the MIHD research project were identified and documented.
Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ MIHD project progress and issue analysis — Reviewed the current status of the MIHD project (best ARI 0.546, root cause of cross-slice retrieval failure, list of fixed bugs) and created a structured problem summary document docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md. Implementation & Fixes ✅ DCC environment initialization: rclone synchronization of Google Drive data — Created ~/.config/gadget/sync.json on the DCC cluster. Synchronized the four types of data from gdrive:gadget to the local machine using sync.py pull. Successfully synchronized 11 directories and 3 sets of files. ✅ DCC GPU node research — Used sinfo/sacctmgr to find available GPU partitions for the user account (yizhanglab): common (RTX 2080), gpu-common, scavenger-gpu (including A6000/RTX 5000-6000 Ada), and scavenger-h200 (H200×8). The TRES limit for the account is cpu=100, mem=500G. ✅ Installing cchelper skill in DCC — Installed the skills/cchelper directory into ~/.claude/skills/cchelper/, which includes SKILL.md, helper.md, ecl-schema.md, and methods-catalog.md. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. MIHD cross-slice retrieval almost completely failed (10 out of 14 queries had SL@50=0) Solution: It was determined that the root cause was PCA/STAIG processing each slice independently, resulting in an uncomparable embedding space. The solution is to use scGPT embedding (shared space, average SL@50=0.416, 100% hit rate). Key insight: The comparability of the embedding space (whether it has a shared coordinate system) is more fundamental than the quality of the embedding. Different encoders should be used for cross-slice tasks and single-slice clustering.
2. The ARI of MIHD single slices differs from the SOTA (current 0.546 vs target 0.60+) Solution: A four-stage ablation experiment plan was developed (clustering ablation → batch effect analysis → retrieval probe → 151676 special diagnosis). This plan has not been fully executed yet. Key insight: The zero variance of the 151676 embedding caused mclust to fail. Retracing to KMeans reduced the mean value, but the root cause remains unknown.
General Issues 3. There is no ~/.config/gadget/sync.json on DCC, preventing sync.py from running Solution: AI retrieved the existing summarize config to get the rclone remote configuration. Manually created sync.json and verified the remote directory structure before executing pull. Key insight: Configuration files do not automatically migrate during device initialization. It’s necessary to infer from existing configurations and rebuild them.
4. When using cp -r to copy the cchelper directory, files are copied to ~/.claude/skills/ instead of maintaining the sub-directory structure Solution: After identifying the issue, created sub-directories using mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/cchelper. Then, moved the scattered files in mv. Key insight: cp -r will copy the contents of the source directory directly if the destination directory already exists. It’s necessary to confirm the destination path before executing this command.
Human vs AI Approaches Strategic Level Documenting problem summaries Role Approach Human Users actively asked to write detailed problems into an.md file after AI summarized them. This promoted the documentation of knowledge. AI AI typically responds in conversation form without actively suggesting to document results Difference Analysis: Humans have the ability to convert conversation content into documents. AI tends to provide answers in conversation form rather than actively documenting results.
Implementation Level Clarifying private file synchronization needs Role Approach Human Users requested to synchronize private files on Google Drive. Their intentions were unclear, and they didn’t specify target directories or specific folders. AI AI listed the directory structure on Google Drive and then asked for specific requirements. It didn’t make any assumptions and proceeded with the execution Difference Analysis: AI chose to confirm rather than guess when the requirements were unclear. This is the correct approach, but the conversation didn’t result in actual synchronization.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations When analyzing MIHD projects, AI relied entirely on existing documents (plans.md/RESULTS.md/diagnostic reports). It couldn’t infer hidden progress or determine the priority of ablation experiments. General Limitations Copying directories ignored the behavior difference of cp -r when the destination already existed. This resulted in files being copied instead of maintaining the sub-directory structure. This issue was discovered and corrected by AI itself. Searching for rclone configuration using glob search timed out in large HPC file systems (20 seconds). It’s necessary to switch to a more precise path search strategy. Learnings Key Learnings In spatial transcriptomics cross-slice tasks, the comparability of the embedding space (shared coordinate system) is more important than the local quality of the embedding. Foundation Model (scGPT) is suitable for cross-slice retrieval because it outputs a fixed representation space. However, PCA/STAIG processes slices independently, making the coordinate system non-comparable. The optimal method for single-slice clustering (STAIG) is different from the optimal method for cross-slice retrieval (scGPT). Different encoders need to be chosen for different tasks, rather than trying to find a unified optimal solution. Practical Learnings The scavenger-h200 partition on the DCC cluster provides H200×8 nodes (although they are抢占式). These nodes are important resources for training tasks that require high memory. The TRES limit of 100 CPU cores and 500G of memory is a hard constraint when submitting large jobs. Conversation Summaries Gadget (DCC) ✅ Synchronized all data from Google Drive according to sync.py configuration 19:27:27.829 | claude_code The user requested to synchronize the content of Google Drive to DCC locally using sync.py configuration. AI found that sync.json didn’t exist. It inferred that the remote path was gdrive:gadget. After creating the configuration file, it performed a dry-run preview, and then executed the actual pull.成功地同步了summarize/research/test/website 共11个目录，6个新目录没有被同步。
✅ Queryed available GPU nodes and partitions on the DCC SLURM cluster 20:57:23.648 | claude_code The user asked about available GPU nodes on the DCC cluster. AI used the sinfo/sacctmgr command to find out that the user account yizhanglab could access common (RTX 2080), gpu-common, scavenger-gpu (including A6000/RTX 5000-6000 Ada), and scavenger-h200 (H200×8) partitions. The TRES limit was set at cpu=100/mem=500G. AI also provided example srun commands for each partition.
✅ Installed the cchelper custom skill in DCC 21:03:52.648 | claude_code The user requested to load the skills within the project. AI found the skills/cchelper directory (Evolving Constraint Planning skill) and installed it in ~/.claude/skills/. During this process, cp -r caused files to be copied to ~/.claude/skills/ instead of maintaining the sub-directory structure. AI then discovered this issue and corrected it using mkdir+mv. Finally, it confirmed that 4 skill files were correctly installed in the ~/.claude/skills/cchelper/ subdirectory.
🔄 Initially explored rclone configuration on DCC to synchronize private Google Drive files 19:20:48.829 | claude_code The user requested to synchronize private files on Google Drive using the existing rclone configuration on the DCC cluster. AI confirmed that rclone was available and that gdrive: remote was configured. It listed the directory structure on the cloud (ADnet/ZLibrary/gadget/summarize/tmp). Since the user didn’t specify the target folder and local path, the conversation ended without actually synchronizing the files.
MIHD ✅ Analyzed the current progress and main issues of the MIHD project and wrote them into PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md 21:08:23.848 | claude_code The user asked about the progress of the MIHD project and the issues encountered. After reading the plans.md/RESULTS.md diagnostic reports, AI identified that the project had completed the evaluation of the framework and 10 fusion strategies. There were also four unresolved issues (ARI gap/cross-slice retrieval failure/151676 crash/scGPT paradox), as well as five fixed historical bugs. The user further requested to write the issues into a document. AI created docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md, which contained a structured summary of 4 major sections.
Token Usage Total: $0.71 — 0.7M tokens
summary: 在 DCC HPC 集群完成环境初始化（rclone 同步、GPU 节点调研、技能安装），并梳理 MIHD 研究项目的当前问题并写入文档。
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-23

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W12 (2026-03-16 ~ 2026-03-22) Week 12 achieved major breakthroughs across five strategic fronts: spatial transcriptomics (proved Foundation Models essential for zero-shot retrieval with scGPT achieving 100% vs 14% PCA, while discovering Harmony’s semantic-batch alignment trade-off through large-scale refactoring), robotics infrastructure (stabilized VLA inference with 4 critical bug fixes, completed 90% error benchmark generation with 2920 scenes, unblocked MimicGen 10→1000 augmentation path via coordinate transformation fix), research intelligence (transformed from 2-mode to 5-mode system with chunked long-text analysis validated on 30+ researchers including comprehensive Yuke Zhu trajectory), life management systems (eliminated single-intent architectural bottleneck with multi-intent classifier, archived 2400+ LOC dead code achieving comprehensive codebase health restoration), and infrastructure consolidation (established unified outputs/site staging architecture, removed 94MB personal data from git history with rclone sync boundary, completed 30+ days historical documentation consolidation). Advanced QCVLA with FLARE-inspired improvement analysis proposing perturbation & bridging augmentation to address DAgger saturation, while systematic refactoring across 21 files fixed O(N²) cKDTree bottleneck preventing HD data OOM.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-16 ~ 2026-03-22 Active Days 7 / 7 Total Conversations 37 Projects 23 Tasks Completed 67 Tasks In Progress 6 Total Tokens 73,112,955 Total Cost $42.71 Claude Code Token 38,972,458 Claude Code Cost $23.42 Codex Token 34,140,497 Codex Cost $19.29 Daily Average Cost $7.12 Project Progress VLA/Robotics Infrastructure (3 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Fixed 4 critical inference bugs (None check, prompt reset, log coverage, state update) Implemented separated action_loss/task_loss monitoring with has_aux=True pattern Generated 2920 error benchmark scenes across 6 tasks (90% complete) Optimized CALVIN dataset conversion with schema validation Enhanced GPU monitoring tool with interactive navigation and deduplication Integrated LIBERO custom test suite Blockers:
⚠️ Error benchmark D0 samples insufficient for some types (<10 samples) ⚠️ Threading task only injects collision_empty due to phase_labels all being pre_reach MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics (4 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Proved scGPT Foundation Model achieves 100% cross-section retrieval (SL@50=0.416) vs 14% for PCA Generated comprehensive visualization pipeline with 44MB PDFs Executed large-scale refactoring: 9 optimizations across 21 files Fixed O(N²) cdist→cKDTree preventing OOM on 17K+ spot HD data Centralized NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS constant, discovered missing adaln_attention Discovered fundamental limitation: Harmony improves batch mixing metrics (ASW_batch +36%) but degrades niche-level semantic alignment due to dimension meaning misalignment Prepared comprehensive Claude Code demo for lab meeting covering plan mode, permissions, AI overconfidence examples Launched multi-agent exploration for diagnosing clustering ARI gap (0.475 vs SOTA 0.66+) Blockers:
⚠️ Batch correction and semantic alignment are orthogonal properties—linear correction cannot preserve semantic correspondence when embeddings trained independently have misaligned dimension meanings QCVLA Latent Bridge (2 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Achieved complete technical comprehension of 7-step pipeline Created comprehensive CLAUDE.md documentation Designed improvement_analysis.md with 11 proposals from FLARE + 9 VLA papers Proposed perturbation & bridging augmentation to address DAgger +1pp saturation Identified P0 quick-wins and P1 high-value improvements Configured remote development server (athena.egr.duke.edu) Error Recovery Benchmark (4 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Designed Recovery Behavior Grouping taxonomy (29 subtypes→5 RBGs) Fixed CompositeBodyObject coordinate transformation bug via git archaeology Implemented complete 8-module pipeline (3,544 lines, 34 tests) Launched v5 scene generation: 14,500+ scenes with real-time monitoring Reduced human demo requirements from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings) Unblocked 10→1000 demo augmentation path by fixing MimicGen _replay_with_warping to actually apply pos_delta transformation Optimized task completion model training with pos_weight=10 BCE loss balancing 4% positive samples Created portable deployment package (252MB compressed) with 139 passing unit tests Implemented training scene generation with ControlledRng for uniform magnitude distribution (1320 scenes across 132 subtypes × 10 samples) Designed 3-tier data pool architecture (Training 10/subtype → Augmentation 200/subtype → Evaluation 100+/subtype) Blockers:
⚠️ V5 scene generation still running ⚠️ D0 sample replenishment needed for some error types ⚠️ Only 11/29 error subtypes have generated scenes—need to complete scene generation for remaining 18 subtypes Research Profiler System / Gadget (Knowledge Management Tools) (5 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Validated profiler with 30+ researcher trajectory analyses Implemented structured JSON output with breakthroughs, themes, methodology evolution Exposed systematic issues: name disambiguation 30% failure, award recall ~0% Completed three-agent code quality review fixing 6 issues Research tool transformed from 2-mode to 5-mode architecture (fast/normal/deep/wide/deep-wide) with chunked long-text analysis supporting arbitrary-scale full-text analysis Completed Yuke Zhu comprehensive academic trajectory (2012-2026, 100 papers, 47-chunk segmentation with LLM merge) Consolidated 30+ days historical daily reports (Feb-Mar 2026) and generated W05-W12 weekly reports Fixed finalize mechanism deadlock preventing infinite LLM API cost loop Implemented JSON repair pipeline with 4-stage fault tolerance Established unified outputs/site staging architecture as single source of truth Built website deployment system with link-or-copy sync, bugJournal hierarchical dropdown navigation, and integrated benchmark report publishing Blockers:
⚠️ Name disambiguation requires multi-signal approach beyond single database ⚠️ Award identification needs live API integration (OpenReview, DBLP) Life Copilot (formerly CalendarPro) (3 days active) — ✅ completed Accomplishments:
Implemented BATCH_UPDATE intent across 8 files Added semantic routing with 21 utterances and keyword scorer Created handler with calendar integration for multi-task operations Improved GENERAL fallback to preserve substantive LLM replies Wrote 21-test comprehensive test suite (all passing) Eliminated fundamental single-intent architectural bottleneck by replacing dual-verify + 7-layer keyword hacks with Claude CLI multi-intent analyzer Fixed three-layer calendar query bugs (LLM prompt placement, keyword fallback, include_past parameter, Discord error feedback) Completed comprehensive codebase health overhaul: archived 2400+ LOC dead code, fixed duplicate meal scheduling, maintained 373-424 passing tests throughout refactoring Completed project rename from CalendarPro to Life Copilot across 15 files Implemented dev mode auto-restart with watchfiles and optimized test suite with pytest-timeout Website Infrastructure (2 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Removed 452 personal files (94MB) from git history via git filter-repo, reducing repo size from 22MB to 516KB Established clear data privacy boundary: git manages public code/config, rclone manages personal data/runtime outputs Fixed three deployment blockers: PaperMod theme update with conflict resolution, update.sh robustness, Hugo future content date handling Created sync.py centralized rclone wrapper with push/pull/status/config commands Implemented website/sync_staging.py with bootstrap migration for first-run cutover Key Tasks ✅ MIHD cross-section embedding diagnostic proving Foundation Models essential (2026-03-16) — Benchmarked 5 methods (PCA, STAIG, Raw HVG, UNI2, scGPT); scGPT achieved 100% hit rate vs 14% for PCA; generated 5 comprehensive PDFs (44MB each) plus 35 per-layer PDFs ✅ Error recovery demo collection plan with Recovery Behavior Grouping (2026-03-16) — Designed 29 subtypes→5 RBGs taxonomy with MimicGen augmentation; implemented 8-module pipeline (3,544 lines, 34 tests); reduced human demos 81% (1,740→329) ✅ Multi-intent classifier refactor eliminating CalendarPro’s single-intent bottleneck (2026-03-19) — Replaced dual-verify + 7-layer keyword hacks with Claude CLI MultiIntentAnalyzer supporting multi-intent decomposition in single message. Added 30 tests, all 424 tests passing. ✅ Gadget research tool 5-mode system architecture upgrade with chunked long-text analysis (2026-03-19) — Extended from 2 modes to 5 modes (fast/normal/deep/wide/deep-wide), implemented _chunk_papers_by_year greedy algorithm (150K chars/chunk target), dynamic timeout formula (300s+120s/50K chars), TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT for semantic integration ✅ Yuke Zhu comprehensive academic trajectory analysis (2026-03-19) — Systematic year-stratified analysis revealing evolution from Visual Genome visual understanding → AI2-THOR embodied intelligence → robosuite/RoboTurk ecosystem building → MineDojo/Voyager LLM-driven agents → geometric representations + VLM alignment (h-index 56, 24,611 citations). Final deep-wide mode auto-segmented 100 papers into 47 chunks with LLM merge. ✅ Design and execute personal data removal from git history + rclone sync infrastructure (2026-03-20) — Executed 8-step plan: updated .gitignore, git rm –cached 435 files, created sync.py with 4-category support, git filter-repo removed 94MB across 24 commits (repo 22MB→516KB), established clear boundary: git=public code, rclone=personal data ✅ Fix CompositeBodyObject coordinate transformation bug (2026-03-16) — Root-caused via git archaeology (commit 398af01b); restored missing locations_relative_to_corner logic; bug masked by HDF5 state replay for 3 months 🔄 Launch v5 error scene generation pipeline (2026-03-16) — Generating ~14,500 error scenes (100 scenes/subtype × 29 subtypes × 5 tasks) with tqdm progress tracking at 4 levels ✅ Research profiler system development and validation (2026-03-16) — Validated with 30+ researchers (Chelsea Finn, Yuke Zhu, Sergey Levine, etc.); identified systematic issues: name disambiguation 30% failure, award recall ~0%, JSON instability 20% ✅ Gadget project code quality review (/simplify) (2026-03-16) — Three-agent code review of 443KB diff; found and fixed 6 issues: missing import, duplicated hash, mkdir in hot path, wasteful LLM call, redundant analysis ✅ QCVLA documentation and improvement planning (2026-03-17) — Created CLAUDE.md covering 7-step pipeline; generated improvement_analysis.md with 11 proposals; extracted FLARE techniques (perturbation, hard failure mining, bridging) ✅ VLA inference system bug fixes (2026-03-17) — Fixed 4 critical bugs: None check order causing crashes, prompt not reset causing infinite loop, debug log threshold inconsistency, task status not updated ✅ Implement BATCH_UPDATE intent for CalendarPro (2026-03-18) — Added new intent across 8 files with semantic routing (21 utterances), keyword scorer, LLM prompts, handler with calendar integration, and 21-test suite ✅ Post-fusion Harmony alignment for spatial transcriptomics (2026-03-19) — Implemented –harmony flag, ran experiments on sections 151508-151673. Key finding: Harmony improves batch metrics (ASW_batch +36%) but degrades semantic alignment due to non-linear dimension misalignment from per-section independent training ✅ Comprehensive codebase audit and Phase 2 refactoring (2026-03-19) — Mapped 29K LOC, identified 8 orphaned modules, archived unused code with README, merged duplicate parse_datetime_with_timezone, deleted broker_models.py and 3 orphaned config settings. All 373 tests pass post-refactoring. ✅ MimicGen warping bug fix unblocking 10→1000 augmentation path (2026-03-19) — Fixed _replay_with_warping to actually apply pos_delta transformation to warped_actions (was computed but never applied). Enables 10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success = 1000 demos. ✅ Batch consolidation of 30+ days historical daily reports (2026-03-19) — Batch-generated approximately 30+ days of historical reports using gadget summarize tool chain covering MIHD, error-recovery-benchmark, CalendarPro, Desktop Video, gadget projects ✅ Finalize mechanism bug diagnosis preventing infinite LLM cost loop (2026-03-19) — Diagnosed two-tier finalize deadlock where log layer requires (target_date != today() AND conversation_count unchanged) and report layer requires all_sources_finalized ✅ Fix rclone sync-all bug causing repeated LLM calls (2026-03-20) — Discovered _cmd_merge_sync_all only called _rclone_download_logs() but not reports, causing finalized status checks to fail. Implemented _rclone_download_reports() ✅ Design and implement unified outputs/site staging architecture (2026-03-22) — Created common/site_staging.py with resolve_site_staging_root, write_site_content, copy_site_static helpers; defined outputs/site/{content,static} as canonical staging root preventing dual-write inconsistencies ✅ MIHD codebase refactoring and optimization (2026-03-17) — Applied 9 fixes: centralized NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS (added missing adaln_attention), hoisted device resolution (removed 8 duplicates), replaced O(N²) cdist with cKDTree ✅ Training loss monitoring separation (2026-03-17) — Modified BaseModel.compute_loss to return (loss_array, aux_dict) tuple; implemented separate action_loss/task_loss logging with has_aux=True ✅ Optimize task completion model training with weighted BCE loss (2026-03-19) — Diagnosed class imbalance (4% positive samples), implemented pos_weight=10 in BCE loss balancing gradient contributions ✅ Comprehensive line-by-line documentation verification (2026-03-20) — Launched 3 parallel Explore agents to verify every factual claim in 10 documentation files against actual codebase. Fixed 7 discrepancies Problems & Solutions 1. Per-section independent PCA/STAIG produces incomparable embedding spaces, causing cross-section retrieval to fail (14-29% hit rate) [MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics] (2026-03-16) Solution: Use Foundation Models (scGPT Gene FM, UNI2 Vision FM) that apply same pretrained weights to all sections, producing naturally comparable embeddings. scGPT achieves 100% hit rate with avg SL@50=0.416.
2. Post-fusion Harmony alignment improved batch mixing metrics but degraded cross-sample niche retrieval quality [MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics] (2026-03-19) Solution: Analyzed results and concluded Harmony’s linear correction cannot handle non-linear dimension meaning misalignment from per-section independent training. Recommended Joint STAIG multi-section training for next iteration.
3. Scholar database name disambiguation failure affects ~30% of analyzed researchers, merging multiple people into single profiles [Research Profiler System] (2026-03-16) Solution: Added explicit warning mechanism with ⚠️ markers in trajectory_summary flagging h-index/citation discrepancies; recommended pre-processing step clustering papers by semantic similarity, institutional affiliation, co-author network.
4. Coffee machine model appeared disassembled in simulation during env.reset(), but HDF5 replay showed correct assembly [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-16) Solution: Traced MimicGen commit 398af01b to discover missing locations_relative_to_corner coordinate transformation. Restored code blocks converting corner-relative to center-relative positions in _append_object().
5. Collecting error recovery demos for 6 tasks × 29 subtypes requires 1,740 samples which is prohibitively expensive [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-16) Solution: Group 29 subtypes into 5 Recovery Behavior Groups (RBGs) based on motion primitives; collect 5-10 demos per RBG per task; use MimicGen/IntervenGen augmentation to generate 100+ samples per subtype. Reduced to 329 demos (81% savings).
6. MimicGen augmentation failing: pos_delta computed but never applied to action warping, causing <5% success rate [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-19) Solution: Fixed _replay_with_warping to apply pos_delta to warped_actions[:, :3] before adding noise, enabling proper object-centric trajectory transformation. Expected success rate: 50%+ for D0 translation errors.
7. QCVLA DAgger Round 2 only improved +1pp (60%→61% SR), indicating saturation [QCVLA Latent Bridge] (2026-03-17) Solution: Proposed perturbation & bridging data augmentation: during DAgger rollout, randomly inject short coherent action sequences followed by reverse recovery actions. Captures large-magnitude deviations bridge would never naturally produce.
8. VLA inference task completed but infinite loop sending done messages because current_prompt was not reset [VLA/Robotics Infrastructure] (2026-03-17) Solution: Set current_prompt = "" after task completion and call _publish_state(“idle”); moved None check before task_completed.item() to avoid crashes on non-pi05 models.
9. MIHD NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS tuple duplicated across files, with adaln_attention missing from both [MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics] (2026-03-17) Solution: Centralized NEEDS_COORDS_FUSIONS as constant in models/Fusion.py, imported by both files. Added missing adaln_attention to tuple, fixing bug where this fusion would fail to receive spatial coordinates.
10. MIHD refine_labels_spatial_majority used O(N²) cdist causing OOM on Visium HD data with 17K+ spots [MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics] (2026-03-17) Solution: Replaced scipy.spatial.distance.cdist with scipy.spatial.cKDTree.query which has O(N*k) complexity for k-nearest neighbor search. Changed from full distance matrix to on-demand radius neighbor queries.
11. VLA training combined action_loss and task_loss into single value logged to wandb, unable to monitor each separately [VLA/Robotics Infrastructure] (2026-03-17) Solution: Modified compute_loss to return (combined_loss, {“action_loss”: …, “task_loss”: …}) tuple; training loop uses nnx.value_and_grad(…, has_aux=True) to unpack auxiliary metrics and add to info dict.
12. CalendarPro could only handle single intent per message, losing subsequent actions [Life Copilot] (2026-03-19) Solution: Replaced single-intent dual-verify flow with Claude CLI MultiIntentAnalyzer returning list of intents. Updated _process_message_queue to loop through intents and combine responses. Removed 7-layer keyword override system.
13. CalendarPro calendar query returned ’no events today’ for ’this week’ and ’tomorrow’ queries immediately after creating events [Life Copilot] (2026-03-19) Solution: Three-layer fix: (1) added Query Intent Classification to BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, (2) added keyword fallback detection in _handle_query(), (3) fixed get_today_events() to include_past=True, (4) added _pending_warnings for Discord error feedback.
14. Initial deep-wide analysis of Yuke Zhu with single 5MB prompt (100 papers full-text) far exceeded LLM processing capacity [Gadget] (2026-03-19) Solution: Implemented three-layer solution: ①added _MAX_PROMPT_CHARS=150K threshold to auto-trigger segmentation; ②_chunk_papers_by_year greedy algorithm grouping by year + per-paper splitting when single year too large; ③TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT using LLM to merge 47 chunk analyses.
15. Task completion model training plateaued at loss~0.035 despite grad_norm=0.7-1.0 with 4% positive vs 96% negative samples [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-19) Solution: Applied positive sample weighting (pos_weight=10) in BCE loss to balance gradient contributions, effectively raising positive sample ratio from 4% to ~30%.
16. Gadget summarize finalize mechanism deadlock causing sync-all to repeatedly invoke LLM merge wasting API costs [Gadget] (2026-03-19) Solution: Diagnosed two-tier finalize (log + report) requires all source logs finalized before marking report finalized. Root cause: export sets _finalized=True only when (target_date != today() AND conversation_count unchanged). Solution: re-run export on affected devices.
17. Personal data files tracked by git and pushed to GitHub, blurring code repository vs data storage boundary with privacy risks [Gadget] (2026-03-20) Solution: Designed three-layer cleanup: (1) git rm –cached to remove tracking; (2) git filter-repo rewrite all 24 commit history; (3) use rclone unified management for personal files. Reduced repo from 22MB to 516KB.
18. When user ran merge on other devices generating finalized reports, local merge –sync-all still judged all dates as non-finalized [Gadget] (2026-03-20) Solution: Discovered _rclone_download_logs only downloads logs not reports, causing local finalized checks to fail. Implemented _rclone_download_reports() to download remote reports before checking finalized status.
19. PaperMod theme outdated causing Hugo v0.157.0 build failure due to missing get-page-images partial [Website Infrastructure] (2026-03-22) Solution: Cleaned macOS resource fork files causing git corruption, stashed local changes and pulled latest theme, manually resolved head.html merge conflict preserving upstream theme-toggle plus local MathJax and checkbox scripts.
Learnings Architecture (architecture) Foundation Models’ key advantage for cross-sample tasks: provide shared embedding space without needing joint training; pretrained weights guarantee comparability across arbitrary inputs. Zero-shot cross-section retrieval fundamentally requires this property—no amount of per-sample optimization can substitute. Batch correction methods like Harmony provide global distribution mixing but cannot preserve semantic alignment when independent training produces embeddings with misaligned dimension meanings. This requires shared training (Joint STAIG) or foundation models. Batch mixing and semantic alignment are orthogonal properties. Single-intent classification is architectural constraint that cannot be fixed with keyword hacks. Multi-intent LLM analysis eliminates need for all heuristics. CalendarPro’s 7-layer keyword override system was treating symptoms of broken abstraction. Git vs rclone responsibility boundary must be clearly defined: git manages reproducible public code and configuration, rclone manages personal data and runtime outputs. Mixing causes repo bloat (94MB personal data), privacy risks, and collaboration obstacles. Multi-layered intent classification requires coordinated updates across 8+ files: enum, semantic routes, keyword scorer, LLM prompts, context assembly, dual verify defaults, handler + routing, plus derived data. Missing any layer causes classification failure. Bootstrap migration strategy for source-of-truth cutover: when transitioning from scattered deploy targets to centralized staging, first-run detection with automatic content relocation prevents data loss and eliminates manual pre-migration burden. Distributed collaboration state synchronization design principle: must sync not only input data (logs), but also intermediate outputs (reports), otherwise logic depending on output state (like finalized checks) will fail across different devices. State machines require explicit reset of all related variables at each termination node; implicit assumptions that ’next loop will naturally change’ easily lead to unpredictable behaviors like infinite loops. Spatial queries on large point clouds (10K+ spots) must use tree-based structures. O(N²) distance matrices cause OOM. cKDTree with O(N*k) neighbor queries scales to HD spatial omics data. JAX/Flax’s value_and_grad(…, has_aux=True) elegantly returns additional monitoring metrics from loss function without affecting gradient computation—the standard pattern for multi-metric monitoring. Hot path optimization golden rule: read path assumes resources exist, write path creates resources. DiskCache mkdir moved from read (_path_for called frequently) to write (put() called rarely) for 10x efficiency gain. Tools (tools) Name disambiguation is #1 data quality issue in automated scholar profiling (affects ~30%). Requires multi-signal approach: h-index vs field coherence, citation temporal distribution, institutional metadata, co-author network analysis. Ultra-large-scale text analysis engineering paradigm: ①automatic segmentation (150K chars ~15-20 papers optimal granularity) ②semantic merge (LLM-driven merge not concatenation) ③dynamic timeout (linearly scaled based on prompt length, 300s baseline+120s/50K chars) ④caching mechanism (avoid retry cost). subprocess.run with capture_output=True has no stdin - any interactive prompts will hang indefinitely; always use –yes/–non-interactive flags for package managers and CLI tools in automation. Cross-project technical transfer requires distinguishing ‘directly applicable’ from ‘conceptually adaptable’. FLARE→QCVLA transfer was mostly conceptual (perturbation thinking, expert mixing) rather than code-level. Parallel agent exploration (3 agents for intent routing, task handling, general protection) is more efficient than sequential file reading for complex diagnostics. Each agent provides focused perspective that synthesizes into complete understanding. Background Bash tasks in Claude Code don’t inherit conda init; must use either ‘conda run -n ENV’ or absolute Python executable paths. Direct conda activate fails with CondaError in non-interactive subshells. Structured output validation should be architecturally decoupled from generation—two-stage ‘generate then repair’ pattern more robust than single-shot perfect output for complex nested schemas. git filter-repo is the correct tool for purging large files/sensitive data from git history (22MB→516KB repo size reduction), but requires full workflow: backup .git, run filter-repo, restore structural files, force-push, update all clones. Hugo theme updates require three-layer approach: ①git repository integrity (clean corrupted resource fork files), ②dependency alignment (theme version compatible with Hugo version), ③local customization preservation (manual conflict resolution). Documentation-code drift is inevitable and accumulates silently over time; periodic exhaustive line-by-line verification (using parallel Explore agents for speed) is necessary to catch numeric values, path references, and internal contradictions. Link-or-copy synchronization pattern: os.link provides zero-copy atomic updates when filesystem supports hard links; automatic fallback to shutil.copytree ensures cross-platform compatibility without manual intervention. Debugging (debugging) MimicGen-style augmentation is fundamentally about coordinate transformation, not noise injection. pos_delta (source→target object offset) must be applied to action trajectories. Without this, 10→1000 augmentation collapses due to <5% success rate. Coordinate transformation bugs in composite model systems can be masked by state replay mechanisms that overwrite XML-defined positions, surfacing only during fresh initialization (env.reset). Large-scale code refactoring should use parallel Agent-based multi-dimensional review (reuse/quality/efficiency) to systematically discover issues. This uncovered 9 problems including latent bugs, performance bottlenecks, maintainability issues. Proactive code review > reactive fixes. Even when user only asks ‘how it’s implemented’, expand to ‘are there problems’. Proactively discovering potential bugs is more valuable than fixing them after users encounter issues. Class imbalance in binary classification requires explicit gradient balancing. Low overall loss can mask poor minority class learning when 96% of samples are one class. pos_weight effectively rebalances gradient contributions. DateTime field parsing order matters critically—always prioritize ISO-format fields (start_iso) over display-format strings (start_time ‘08:00’) to avoid silent parse failures that break existence checks. Error injection benchmark quality depends on task phase segmentation completeness; incomplete phase recognition causes some error types to be uncoverable. Need to verify all tasks cover expected phases during opportunity map stage. Process GPU usage detection requires both environment variable inheritance check AND actual device file descriptor verification to avoid false positives from shell processes. Download failure diagnosis should follow data-first approach: when systematic failures occur, verify data availability (grep for expected patterns in config files) before deep-diving into code logic. Dataset format conversion must read schema files (like RLDS features.json) to confirm actual field names before assuming common conventions or guessing. Shell script robustness principles: ①check git state before operations rather than relying on exit codes, ②add error handling for filesystem operations, ③explicitly exempt normal failure scenarios when using set -e. Domain Knowledge (domain) Recovery demo data augmentation three-layer strategy: scene configuration (different error scenes as start states), cross-degree (D0→D1/D2 via rotation perturbation), cross-subtype (within-RBG cross-augmentation). Combined with MimicGen trajectory segmentation, reduces data requirements 81%. When DAgger-style training saturates, expand data distribution via structured perturbations (coherent action sequences + recovery) rather than per-frame noise. For robotic manipulation, temporal coherence matters—physics and inertia make sequential perturbations more realistic. ‘Why not before’ 反事实推理 framework reveals breakthrough timing dependencies: technology prerequisites (GPU/pretrained models), data availability (large-scale datasets), theoretical foundations (new concepts). Academic success highly dependent on ’technology stack maturity’ and ’time window’ matching. LLM-based award identification without live API integration has fundamental recall limitations—training data cannot keep pace with conference proceedings. Hybrid approach needed: LLM screens candidates → DBLP/OpenReview API verifies → human resolves conflicts. Robotics field paradigm shifts 2016-2023: deep imitation learning (2016-2019) → cross-embodiment large-scale datasets (2020-2022) → vision-language-action foundation models (2023+). Each window only 2-3 years; researchers who pivot during transitions capture disproportionate impact. Academic trajectory analysis multi-angle validation method: Through iterative analysis of same researcher’s different paper subsets (from 1 paper to 100 papers), can build stereoscopic cognition from micro (single representative work deep analysis) to macro (complete publication list trajectory characterization). Year-stratified analysis strategy superior to either full-corpus single-pass (prompt too long/analysis shallow) or random sampling (misses temporal evolution). Balances temporal dimension completeness with paper quality. Research infrastructure building pathway: Yuke Zhu’s academic trajectory reveals field breakthroughs often accompanied by infrastructure-type work (AI2-THOR simulation, robosuite benchmark, RoboTurk crowdsourcing, MineDojo dataset). For emerging research directions, prioritizing reproducible evaluation benchmarks and data collection toolchains has more strategic value. Recovery Behavior Groups (RBG) abstraction: classify 29 error subtypes by required motor primitives (re-grasp, retrieve, retract, redirect, realign) rather than error taxonomy. Enables data sharing within groups—more practical than per-error-type collection. Fallback templates in AI systems should preserve useful LLM-generated content rather than blindly overwriting it. Check if LLM reply is substantive (>20 chars, not template phrase) before replacing with hardcoded fallback. Keyword scoring can capture patterns that embedding similarity misses: 2+ distinct completion verbs is simple heuristic for batch updates that complements semantic routing. Explicit pattern detection still valuable in neural systems. AI Usage Notes Effective Patterns:
✓ Parallel agent-based exploration for complex diagnostics (3 agents analyzing intent routing, task handling, general protection simultaneously) ✓ Proactive code review finding unasked-for bugs (discovered 4 VLA inference issues when user only asked ‘how does this work’) ✓ Cross-project technical transfer (extracted FLARE perturbation & bridging concepts and adapted to QCVLA DAgger saturation) ✓ Systematic root cause analysis (traced RoboCasa download failures through 4 layers to discover 0 MimicGen entries in box_links_ds.json) ✓ Three-agent code quality review (reuse/quality/efficiency dimensions) for large diffs (443KB gadget refactoring) ✓ Reading all affected files in parallel before implementation to understand dependencies (8-file BATCH_UPDATE feature) ✓ Has_aux=True pattern for returning auxiliary metrics from loss functions without affecting gradients ✓ Parallel Explore agents for exhaustive documentation verification - launched 3 agents to verify 10 files simultaneously, found 7 discrepancies ✓ Multi-round iterative analysis with different paper subsets built comprehensive researcher profiles - Yuke Zhu profiling through 10+ analysis rounds from 1 paper to 100 papers ✓ Batch Edit tool for synchronized documentation updates - updated 6 README/tutorial files maintaining consistency across codebase ✓ Chunked long-text analysis with LLM merge - 47-chunk segmentation with semantic integration broke single-LLM capacity limit enabling arbitrary-scale deep analysis ✓ Structured JSON repair pipeline with model escalation - four-stage repair (direct parse → code block → brace match → LLM repair with haiku→sonnet→opus) Limitations:
✗ Conference award knowledge severely outdated (post-2024) with near-zero recall (12/13 queries empty) ✗ Failed to consider zero-shot constraint when proposing joint PCA and Procrustes alignment initially ✗ Cannot disambiguate same-name researchers without external signals (affects ~30% of analyzed researchers) ✗ Spent 4 rounds fixing conda/environment/path workarounds before user clarified fundamental requirement (use claude_cli backend) ✗ Chinese-language nested JSON generation unstable (20% failure rate requiring repair pass) ✗ Misinterpreted detailed user plan as trigger for planning workflow rather than specification for direct execution ✗ Cannot directly determine root cause of threading task phase recognition issue without further debugging ✗ Cannot verify fix effectiveness without running code (e.g., loss separation needs actual training to confirm) ✗ Cannot handle interactive password entry for SSH authentication (required user manual ssh-copy-id) ✗ Limited understanding of project historical context, needs gradual learning through git log and documentation ✗ Long JSON generation exhibits systematic format instability - over 12+ repair cycles required for daily reports and research analyses ✗ Failed to predict optimal chunking parameters through reasoning - required 5 retries adjusting chunk size and timeout formula ✗ Did not proactively audit _finalized default value logic across export vs merge stages when adding –force flag ✗ Over-delegated codebase audit to 3 parallel agents despite task requiring holistic architectural judgment ✗ Initial inability to autonomously detect homonymous researchers from paper metadata alone ✗ Failed to anticipate all shell script edge cases during update.sh repair - required multiple user test-feedback cycles Next Week Outlook Continue MIHD dual-track research addressing both ARI improvement (ablation experiments to diagnose 0.475 vs 0.66+ SOTA gap) and batch effect reduction (Joint STAIG multi-section training as recommended solution for semantic-batch alignment trade-off). Complete error recovery benchmark training data collection with human demo collection for remaining 18 error subtypes and MimicGen augmentation pipeline execution (targeting 1000 demos via 10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success rate). Begin testing QCVLA perturbation & bridging augmentation to break DAgger saturation plateau. Apply MIHD refactoring learnings and cKDTree optimization to production HD datasets. Monitor VLA training runs with newly separated action_loss/task_loss metrics in wandb. Establish gadget monthly report generation workflow integrating newly completed weekly reports W05-W12. Monitor Life Copilot production stability with multi-intent classifier in real-world usage. Consider extending research profiler capabilities based on Yuke Zhu case study learnings (homonym detection, cross-iteration temporal causality inference). Deploy website with new staging architecture and benchmark integration for public visibility. Consider implementing QCVLA P0 quick-win (Action-Aware Channel Importance Loss) and P1 improvements (Bridge Confidence Head for adaptive scheduling).
Token Usage Statistics Total: $42.71 — 73.1M tokens
Peak Day: 2026-03-21 — $11.20 / 16.6M tokens
Daily Average: $7.12
summary: Week 12 achieved major breakthroughs across five strategic fronts: spatial transcriptomics (proved Foundation Models essential for zero-shot retrieval with scGPT achieving 100% vs 14% PCA, while discovering Harmony’s semantic-batch alignment trade-off through large-scale refactoring), robotics infrastructure (stabilized VLA inference with 4 critical bug fixes, completed 90% error benchmark generation with 2920 scenes, unblocked MimicGen 10→1000 augmentation path via coordinate transformation fix), research intelligence (transformed from 2-mode to 5-mode system with chunked long-text analysis validated on 30+ researchers including comprehensive Yuke Zhu trajectory), life management systems (eliminated single-intent architectural bottleneck with multi-intent classifier, archived 2400+ LOC dead code achieving comprehensive codebase health restoration), and infrastructure consolidation (established unified outputs/site staging architecture, removed 94MB personal data from git history with rclone sync boundary, completed 30+ days historical documentation consolidation). Advanced QCVLA with FLARE-inspired improvement analysis proposing perturbation & bridging augmentation to address DAgger saturation, while systematic refactoring across 21 files fixed O(N²) cKDTree bottleneck preventing HD data OOM.
title: Weekly Summary 2026-W12

content:
Gadget/summarize ✅ Batch-generated historical weekly reports for W05-W12 19:30:00.000 | claude_code The dailySummary.py tool was used to generate structured weekly reports for weeks 5 to 12 in 2026. The reports covered MIHD spatial transcriptomics (STAIG alignment fixes, scGPT fusion, Harmony experiments), error recovery benchmarks (error injection frameworks, three-pool architecture), CalendarPro (intention classification upgrades), RoboBrain (task completion detection), Life Copilot (multi-intention reconstruction), and Pi0.5 LoRA training. These trainings were tested on DCC, Tianhe, MacBook, and TzJsDesktop devices. Special emphasis was placed on the weekly reports for multiple high-density parallel projects, including some phased breakthroughs. ✅ Deployed to GitHub Pages successfully after resolving blocking issues in deployment 20:55:34.064 | claude_code Due to an outdated PaperMod theme lacking the get-page-images section, Hugo build failed. The theme was updated to the latest version, and head.html merge conflicts were resolved. Additionally, local MathJax and checkbox customizations were preserved. Two issues in the update.sh script were fixed: handling no changes (added git diff –cached –quiet check) and race conditions in the find function (added 2>/dev/null || true handling). The entire deployment process was completed and deployed to GitHub Pages.✅ Reorganized the Hugo navigation structure and unified the deployment architecture 01:21:42.819 | codex Implemented a hierarchical bugJournal dropdown menu based on custom title sections, with daily/weekly/monthly submenus. content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-21 Daily Overview Completed Work: Synchronized repository status using git and rclone, fixed subprocess vulnerabilities in the summarization tool, integrated Codex usage tracking into the monthly report, upgraded the ECC plugin to use Opus 4.6 on all proxies, and created detailed documentation. Implementation Methods: Used a git conflict resolution mechanism with remote priority, corrected Windows paths using Path.as_posix(), added the –yes flag in npx subprocess calls, modified monthly_summary.py to summarize codex_token_usage separately, edited YAML files for 28 proxies using sed, and created tutorials and contributor documentation. Impact: Established a reliable data synchronization workflow between devices and cloud storage, eliminated suspended subprocess calls that hindered daily summarization, allowing the monthly report to fully track Codex usage, and utilized Opus 4.6 and effortLevel maximums to improve AI inference quality, providing clear introductory materials for the ECC plugin system. Configured the development environment, fixed critical vulnerabilities in the summarization process, integrated Codex support, and upgraded all AI proxies to Opus 4.6
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ ECC Plugin Exploration and Configuration — Explored Everything Claude Code plugin (28 proxies, over 47 skills), upgraded all proxies from sonnet/haiku to opus, changed effortLevel from ‘High’ to ‘Max’, and created detailed ECC_TUTORIAL.md. ✅ Fixing Npx Subprocess Suspension Issues — Discovered that without the –yes flag, npx would cause interactive prompts during subprocess execution to block the collection of token usage data. Added the –yes flag to all 3 npx calls in daily_summary.py. ✅ Integrating Codex into Monthly Summary Process — Modified monthly_summary.py to separate codex_token_usage from token_usage, added combined_token_usage_summary for charts, updated format_monthly_markdown to include Codex section, and updated all documents (README.md, summarize/README.md, AGENTS.md) to mention Codex support. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Rclone Data Synchronization Settings — Created ~/.config/gadget/sync.json configuration file, extracted personal data from gdrive:gadget/ (12 directories), and fixed Windows path errors in sync.py when using Path.as_posix(). ✅ Git Repository Synchronization and Merge Conflict Resolution — Pulled latest changes from origin/main, resolved 13 merge conflicts by accepting the remote version, and discarded local changes based on user preferences. ✅ Document Updates (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) — Added missing CLI parameters (–paper, –author-id, –homepage, –deploy) to research/CLAUDE.md; created a 395-word contributor guide AGENTS.md that includes project structure, commands, and conventions. ✅ PowerShell Execution Policy Diagnosis — Discovered that PowerShell execution policy blocked npx/codex.ps1 wrapper, provided workaround using cmd wrapper and Set-ExecutionPolicy for permanent fix. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Infinite suspension when calling npx @ccusage/codex@latest from Python subprocess Solution: Add the –yes flag to npx calls to skip the interactive “Continue?” prompt, as capture_output=True in subprocess execution prevents stdin input.
Key Insight: Subprocess with capture_output=True has no stdin input, so any interactive prompt will hang indefinitely; always use –yes or -y flags for package managers in automated scripts.
2. Monthly summary still cannot summarize or display Codex token usage despite daily data exports Solution: Add a separate codex_token_usage summary path in aggregate_token_usage(), create combine_usage_summaries() for combined summaries, update format_monthly_markdown to include Codex section, and update all documents (README.md, summarize/README.md, AGENTS.md) to mention Codex support.
Key Insight: When adding new data sources to the summary process, not only need to update input parsing but also summarize logic, LLM input cleaning (remove mechanical fields), and output formatting.
General Problems 3. Windows Path().parent generates backslashes, causing rclone remote path issues (e.g., ‘gdrive:gadget/website\personal’) Solution: Use Path.as_posix() to convert Windows paths to forward slashes for rclone remote paths.
Key Insight: Even on Windows, rclone remote paths must use forward slashes; explicit.as_posix() conversion is required for path operations when building remote URIs.
4. PowerShell execution policy blocks npx and codex command.ps1 wrappers Solution: Suggest using cmd wrappers (npx.cmd, codex.cmd) as a temporary solution, and Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned for a permanent fix.
5. Git merge conflicts occur after pulling changes from remote Solution: Use git stash, then git pull, and finally accept the upstream version and discard the stash to resolve remaining conflicts.
Key Insight: Synchronizing to remote after local cleanup is more straightforward using –theirs to accept remote versions, especially if local changes are intended to be discarded.
Human and AI Approaches Strategic Level Methods for Learning ECC Plugins Role Method Human Users prefer detailed step-by-step tutorials with expected results documented for each step AI AI initially tried to automate exploration and report generation, then shifted to a structured approach using planning mode Difference Analysis: Humans prefer self-paced learning with clear checkpoints; AI defaults to automation and delegation to subagents. Users’ methods are better for building muscle memory and understanding.
Model Selection Strategy for ECC Agents Role Method Human Users want all agents upgraded to Opus 4.6 for optimal quality, willing to accept higher costs AI AI suggests a layered approach (Opus for core agents, Sonnet for routine tasks, Haiku for lightweight tasks) to balance cost and quality Difference Analysis: Humans prioritize consistency and maximum capability; AI prioritizes cost optimization. Users’ choices reflect a focus on quality over cost in this personal project.
Git Conflict Resolution Methods Role Method Human Users know they need to discard local changes and match them exactly with remote changes since they’ve already cleaned their personal data on GitHub AI AI initially tried to preserve local changes by popping a stash and merging two versions Difference Analysis: Humans have knowledge of recent repository cleanups; AI lacks this knowledge and retains all work by default. Context is crucial for destructive operations.
AI Limitations Key Limitation - AI initially didn’t understand that users wanted a self-guided tutorial. When asked to “help me get familiar with this plugin, use the current project,” AI instead launched an automatic subagent exploration program. General Limitations AI suggested a proxy model hierarchy optimized for cost, while users clearly wanted high-quality services (all agents using Opus models), but AI didn’t correctly interpret user preferences. In creating documentation, the initial AI version of AGENTS.md had 402 words (exceeding the 400-word target), requiring some content to be removed, although word limits were clearly specified. Learning Outcomes Key Learnings When using subprocess.run with capture_output=True, no standard input is automatically received—any interactive prompt will hang indefinitely; always use –yes/–non-interactive flags for package managers and other CLI tools in automated processes. When adding new data sources to the aggregation pipeline, you must update: (1) input collection, (2) aggregation logic, (3) LLM input cleaning (remove new fields from prompts), (4) output rendering—missing any of these steps will result in data loss. Windows path objects generate backslashes; when building URIs or remote paths that require forward slashes (e.g., rclone, URLs, Unix paths), Path.as_posix() must be used to avoid cross-platform compatibility issues. The Claude Code plugin architecture consists of 5 layers forming a closed loop: skills (passive knowledge) → agents (specialized reasoning) → commands (user-facing) → hooks (automated triggers) → continuous learning (feedback capture) → skills. Practical Learnings On Windows systems, due to PowerShell execution policies, npm installed commands have both.ps1 (blocked) and .cmd (runnable) wrappers; .cmd is a general alternative. ECC’s effortLevel has 4 levels: low/medium/high/maximum; “maximum” level is the newly added feature, allowing up to 31,999 thinking tokens and deepest reasoning capabilities. Dialogue Summary GitHub Parent Directory ✅ Plugin Installation Command 19:19:43.111 | claude_code User executed local commands to add and install the ’everything-claude-code’ plugin market and plugin, then reloaded the plugin. No interaction from AI beyond command confirmation.
Gadget ✅ Git Synchronization and Rclone Data Pulling 02:35:05.863 | claude_code Triggered 13 merge conflicts when pulling latest changes from remote. AI initially tried to preserve local changes, but user explicitly requested full matching of remote changes (personal data already cleared). Resolved this issue with git reset –hard origin/main. Then set up rclone sync.py, fixed Windows path errors (backslash → forward slash using as_posix()), and extracted all personal data from gdrive:gadget/. Submitted changes with path fixes.
✅ Fixing Npx codex Suspension Issues 19:15:43.784 | claude_code User reported that ’npm exec @ccusage/codex@latest –json –breakdown’ hung indefinitely. AI diagnosed that without the –yes flag, npx would trigger an interactive “Continue?” prompt, but subprocess.run(capture_output=True) had no stdin input, causing the hang. Resolved this issue by adding –yes to all 3 npx calls in daily_summary.py.
✅ Adding Codex Support in Monthly Summary 19:09:16.955 | codex User wanted to include Codex output and expected ccusage to track Codex usage. AI found that daily exports already supported Codex, but monthly summaries did not. Updated monthly_summary.py: removed codex_token_usage (similar to token_usage), added aggregate_token_usage() to summarize Claude and Codex data separately, created combine_usage_summaries() for combining summaries/charts, updated format_monthly_markdown() to include Codex section. Also updated documentation (README.md, summarize/README.md, AGENTS.md) to mention Codex support. Verified through syntax checks and smoke tests.
✅ Creating AGENTS.md Contributor Guide 18:50:04.708 | codex User requested a repository contributor guide. AI scanned the project structure, read existing summarize/AGENTS.md and test/AGENTS.md, checked git history for commit conventions. Created root AGENTS.md (397 words) covering single repository structure, tool commands (summarize/research/test/website), Python style (PEP 8, Black, Ruff), testing methods (no centralized test suite, instead targeting specific tools), commit conventions (regular commits, no co-authors), and security considerations.
✅ Diagnosing Npx/codex Blocking in PowerShell 19:09:16.955 | codex User reported that ’npx codex’ was not working in the current environment. AI checked the path and found npx.ps1 and codex.ps1, but PowerShell execution policy blocked the.ps1 scripts. Explained that cmd wrappers (npx.cmd, codex.cmd) were the immediate solution, and Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned for a permanent fix.
🔍 Plugin List Status Check 19:24:26.931 | claude_code User ran local commands/plugin list, /cost, /status—no action required from AI.
Gadget Research ✅ ECC Plugin Exploration and Configuration 21:18:16.758 | claude_code User ran/init to view CLAUDE.md, which was already very comprehensive. AI added 2 missing configuration parameters (–paper, –author-id). User then wanted to learn how to use ECC plugins. AI explored plugin structure (28 proxies, over 47 skills, 59 commands), developed a detailed tutorial plan, read internal agent definitions and skills. User explicitly requested a hands-on tutorial. AI created ECC_TUTORIAL.md with 6-step guided exercises. User then requested all agents use Opus 4.6 + effortLevel maximum. AI updated settings.json to maximum effortLevel and batch edited 28 proxy files to make their model level opus (per user request, 1 document updater remained sonnet).
✅ CLAUDE.md Documentation Review 20:37:15.819 | claude_code User ran/init. AI found CLAUDE.md was already very accurate and up-to-date, just adding 2 missing CLI parameters (–paper, –author-id for configuration commands).
Token Usage Total: $11.20 — 16.6 million tokens

content:
Key Learnings### Practical Learning Points When building a centralized synchronization infrastructure (sync.py), it’s important to support the --dry-run and --category flags from the beginning. This allows for safe incremental testing and selective synchronization without having to upload all data. Migrating rclone remote paths (gdrive:summarize/ → gdrive:gadget/) is less risky because atomic moves are used. When modifying remote paths, it’s necessary to check the integrity of the configuration migration. This includes updating the config.json in summarize/daily_summary.py, research/research_scout.py, test/sync logic, website/Hugo integration, sync.py global configuration, and existing remote data migration scripts. AI implemented a fix for _rclone_download_reports(). Users also proposed removing all personal files from git (452 files, 94MB) and using rclone instead.
