content:
Yongfeng Ding — Researcher Analysis Report Analysis Mode: fast | Analysis Time: 2026-03-16T00:12:19
Rating: Early Researcher (18.6/100) Basic Metrics Metric Value Institution Unknown h-index 2 Total Citations 24 Recent Citations in Last 5 Years 24 Number of Papers Published 4 Papers Published in Top Journals 0 Publication Period 2023 - 2026 Semantic Scholar 2241760086 Research Trajectory Yongfeng Ding is an emerging researcher in the early stages of their academic career. They began publishing papers in 2023, with only 4 works to date. While they don’t possess the characteristics of a ‘field expert’, they have shown some originality in the quantitative analysis of microstructures in composite materials. Their key contribution was the development of the ‘degree of randomness’ metric in 2023, which is used to assess the uniformity of fiber distributions in composite materials. This methodological work has received 16 citations, indicating recognition within the field.
It’s worth noting that in 2026, there was a paper on EEG emotion recognition that had nothing to do with the previous three papers (in the field of traffic injury prevention). This significant shift across disciplines is either indicative of the researcher exploring interdisciplinary collaboration or possibly a case of people with the same name but different identities. Overall, this researcher is still in the early stages of academic accumulation, with too little research output to determine their long-term direction.
Breakthrough Works 1. A new quantitative method to evaluate the spatial distribution of fibres in composites: the degree of randomness (2023) Description: Proposed a new quantitative metric called ‘degree of randomness’, which is used to assess the randomness of fiber distributions in composite materials, providing a measurable mathematical description of fiber distribution uniformity.
Why it couldn’t be done before: There was a lack of a unified quantitative framework for evaluating the randomness of fiber distributions. Researchers relied on visual judgment or indirect metrics such as nearest-neighbor distances, lacking a dimensionless metric that could be compared across different material systems. The key insight was to transfer the concept of randomness from statistical physics to the analysis of microstructures in composite materials.
Impact: Provided a standardized evaluation scale for evaluating algorithms that generate virtual microstructures. This directly supported an algorithm comparison work published in the same year, which received 6 citations.
2. Comparison of three algorithms generating virtual microstructures in terms of the degree of randomness (2023) Description: Based on the ‘degree of randomness’ metric, this paper systematically compares the performance of three algorithms that generate virtual microstructures in composite materials, evaluating their ability to reproduce realistic fiber distributions.
Why it couldn’t be done before: There was a lack of a unified evaluation criterion for comparing different algorithms for generating virtual microstructures. Each algorithm was developed independently and could not be compared across disciplines. The introduction of the ‘degree of randomness’ method made this systematic comparison possible.
Impact: Received 16 citations. This is the most influential work of the researcher so far, providing an algorithm selection reference for the community of composite material digital twins and multi-scale modeling.
3. Micromechanical modelling of unidirectional continuous fibre-reinforced composites: A review (2025) Description: A systematic review of micromechanical modeling methods for unidirectional continuous fiber-reinforced composites, outlining the evolution and current state of research in this field.
Why it couldn’t be done before: This is a review article written after the researcher established a quantitative method for microstructures in 2023. They had now acquired an overview of the entire micromechanical modeling field from a more macroscopic perspective.
Impact: Received 2 citations (published in 2025, with citation count still accumulating). It helps to integrate scattered literature on micromechanical modeling and provides a systematic reference for researchers new to this field.
Research Directions Quantitative analysis of microstructures in composite materials and randomness analysis Micromechanical modeling of fiber-reinforced composites (Possibly cross-disciplinary) EEG feature selection and emotion recognition Methodological Evolution The researcher started by proposing a new quantitative metric (degree of randomness method, 2023), which was then applied to empirical research on algorithm evaluation in the same year. This shows a compact two-step approach: ‘proposal of method → verification of method’. In 2025, they turned to writing a review article, indicating that they began to attempt to establish a field perspective rather than focusing solely on individual technical points. If the EEG paper in 2026 belongs to the same researcher, it indicates a break from methodology—from statistical quantification in materials science to physiological signal processing and machine learning feature selection. There is no clear path of knowledge transfer between these areas, indicating an unclear cross-disciplinary motivation.
Overall, the methodological evolution so far is relatively shallow, with too few papers published to determine whether the direction will continue to focus on this area.
Field Impact Yongfeng Ding’s impact in the field is currently limited to the specific direction of microstructural analysis in composite materials. Their key contribution was the proposal of the ‘degree of randomness’ metric and its application in algorithm evaluation. With only 3 years of publication, 4 papers, and 24 citations (mainly in material-related journals), their influence is still localized and early-stage. They have not yet achieved a level of systematic impact on the entire field of composite materials or related areas. However, if they continue to focus on quantitative analysis of microstructures and expand into multi-scale modeling applications, there is potential for them to develop significant academic influence.
Top 20 Papers with High Citations # Year Citations Title 1 2023 16 Comparison of three algorithms generating virtual microstructures in terms of the degree of randomness 2 2023 6 A new quantitative method to evaluate the spatial distribution of fibres in composites: the degree of randomness 3 2025 2 Micromechanical modelling of unidirectional continuous fibre-reinforced composites: A review 4 2026 0 Optimization and validation of multiscale feature selection for EEG-based recognition of drivers’ negative emotions.
summary: Yongfeng Ding is an emerging researcher in the early stages of their academic career. They began publishing papers in 2023, and currently have only four publications to their name. Therefore, they do not yet possess the characteristics of a ’leading expert in the field’. However, they have demonstrated certain levels of originality in the area of quantitative analysis of composite materials’ microstructure. Their key contributions focus on the concept of ‘randomness’, which was introduced by them in 2023.
title: Yongfeng Ding — Researcher Profile

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-16 Daily Overview Completed Work: Parallel execution in the robotics research field (MIHD spatial transcriptomics diagnosis, implementation of error recovery benchmark tests, bug fixes in robosuite), data engineering processing (CALVIN format conversion, v5 scenario generation), and development of intelligent tools for research purposes (developed an academic analysis system that includes trajectory analysis by over 30 researchers). Completion Methods: Controlled experiments were conducted to demonstrate that the scGPT base model achieves 100% accuracy in zero-shot cross-part retrieval, while PCA only reaches 14%. A method for grouping recovery behaviors was designed, reducing the number of demonstrations from 1,740 to 329. Git archaeology was used to fix the coordinate conversion issue in CompositeBodyObject. A complete error recovery process was implemented (3,544 lines of code, 139 tests). Structured JSON output and format validation were applied in batch academic analysis. Impact: Made scGPT the gold standard for zero-shot cross-part retrieval. It can generate over 14,500 error scenarios for VLA training. A producible error recovery data process was provided. Research methods were verified, and systematic issues were identified (name ambiguity errors, award recognition problems, unstable JSON format). DCC Completed Work: MIHD cross-part embedding diagnosis experiments were conducted, documentation was written, and a complete visualization process was developed. Completion Methods: Benchmark tests were carried out, comparing the performance of PCA/STAIG/Raw-HVG/UNI2/scGPT on 7 specific types. PDF reports with 5 panel layouts at both method and layer levels were generated. A bilingual diagnostic report with visualizations was prepared. Impact: Demonstrated that the scGPT base model has an accuracy of 100% in zero-shot cross-part retrieval (SL@50=0.416), while each-part PCA has an accuracy of 14%. Five comprehensive PDF files (each 44MB) and 35 layer-level PDF files were provided. It was confirmed that zero-shot constraints require a pre-trained model. MacBook Completed Work: An AGENTS.md contributor guide was created for qcvla_bridge_pipeline, and previous work from CVPR was reviewed. Completion Methods: The repository structure was studied, build/test commands were extracted, and the methodology of FLARE for LoRA multi-experts and fault classification was reviewed. Impact: A 366-word contributor guide was created. Reproducible ideas were identified: LoRA expert routing, ID/OOD fault classification, perturbation-based enhancement methods. TzJsDesktop Completed Work: Comprehensive development and verification of an academic analysis system: including trajectory analysis by over 30 researchers, JSON repair workflows, conference award recognition, and code quality reviews through /simplify. Completion Methods: Academic analysis was performed in batches, structured JSON outputs were generated (trajectory summaries, breakthrough points, research topics, methodological evolution). Iterative environment debugging was done (from conda to Claude CLI). Code reviews were conducted on three agents, and six issues were identified. Impact: The success rate of the analysis process was verified to be about 90%. Systematic issues were identified (about 30% name ambiguity errors, award ID recall rate of about 0%, JSON format instability of 20%). The code library was optimized (eliminated unnecessary mkdirs, wasted LLM calls, duplicate hashes). tianhe Completed Work: Error recovery benchmark tests were conducted: a data collection plan was designed, errors in CompositeBodyObject were fixed, v5 scenario generation was initiated, and a recovery demonstration process was implemented. Completion Methods: Git archaeology was used to restore the missing coordinate conversion code. A sbatch script with MUJOCO_GL=egl and tqdm monitoring was created. Eight new modules (recovery types, splitters, collection/enhancement/conversion scripts) were implemented, and 34 tests were conducted. A five-stage process from error state to Phoenix training format was designed. Impact: The coffee machine model can now assemble correctly. Real-time progress tracking is performed, generating over 14,500 error scenarios. The complete recovery process has been tested (139 tests passed). The need for human demonstrations has been reduced from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings). MIHD cross-part embedding diagnosis experiments were completed, demonstrating the importance of the base model in spatial transcriptomics. An error recovery process was designed (data reduced by 81%). Key coordinate errors in robosuite were fixed. Large-scale scenario generation was initiated. A systematic academic analysis system was developed, involving analysis by over 30 researchers.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ MIHD Cross-Part Embedding Diagnosis Experiment – Benchmarked cross-part retrieval for 5 embedding methods (PCA, STAIG, Raw HVG, UNI2, scGPT). ScGPT gene base model achieved 100% accuracy in zero-shot cross-part retrieval, while each-part PCA had an accuracy of 14%. Comprehensive PDF visualizations and bilingual documents were generated. ✅ Error Recovery Demonstration Collection Plan and Process Implementation – A method for grouping recovery behaviors was designed (29 sub-types → 5 RBGs), and the MimicGen enhancement strategy was adopted. A complete process from error scenario NPZ to Phoenix MCM/Diffusion strategy training format was implemented (8 modules, 3,544 lines of code, 34 tests). The number of human demonstrations was reduced from 1,740 to 329 (81% savings). ✅ Fixing Coordinate Conversion Errors in CompositeBodyObject – The root cause was found using git archaeology (commit number 398af01b). The missing position_relative_to_corner conversion logic in robosuite was restored. This error caused the coffee machine model to disintegrate during environment reset (hidden for 3 months using HDF5 state replay). 🔄 Initiating v5 Error Scenario Generation Process – A Slurm batch job was set up on GPU node an46 (job number 49363). Using 1,000 MimicGen-enhanced demonstrations, 100 scenarios were generated (29 sub-types × 5 tasks = approximately 14,500 error scenarios). A 4-level tqdm progress tracking was added. ✅ Development and Verification of Academic Analysis System – An academic trajectory analysis tool was developed and verified. It can handle data from over 30 researchers (Chelsea Finn, Yuke Zhu, Sergey Levine, Ruoshi Liu, etc.). Structured reports with breakthrough points, research topics, and methodological evolution were generated. Systematic issues were identified (about 30% name ambiguity errors, award ID recall rate of about 0%, JSON format instability of 20%). ✅ Debugging of Academic Environment Configuration – Issues such as conda activation errors, ModuleNotFoundError caused by ‘common’ packages, and PYTHONPATH problems were resolved. The API backend selection problem was solved after 5 rounds of debugging. The final solution was to directly use Python path + PYTHONPATH injection + –api Claude CLI. Implementation and Fixes - ✅ VLA-RoboTwin Video Progress Visualization – The 4-layer inference chain (pi0.py → policy.py → pi_model.py → deploy_policy.py) was modified. Model-predicted control progress was overlayed on the evaluation video. Support was added for displaying 1D and 2D progress using cv2. ✅ CALVIN Dataset Converter: rlds → lerobot Conversion – Existing calvin→rlds and calvin→lerobot scripts were integrated. rlds_to_lerobot.py was created. tfds.builder was used to load the RLDS dataset and perform field mapping, converting it to LeRobotDataset format. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Independent PCA/STAIG training for each part results in an uncomparable embedding space, leading to failure in cross-part retrieval (recall rate of 14-29%) Solution: Use base models (scGPT gene FM, UNI2 visual FM). These models apply the same pre-trained weights to all parts, resulting in a naturally comparable embedding space. ScGPT has a recall rate of 100%, with an average SL@50 of 0.416.
Key Insight: Zero-shot cross-part retrieval requires a shared embedding space. Joint PCA/Procrustes/joint training require data from both parts, thus violating the zero-shot constraint. Only pre-trained base models can provide true zero-shot capabilities. Users immediately realized this constraint, avoiding wasting time on infeasible solutions.
2. Failure in handling name ambiguity in scholar databases – Multiple researchers with the same name were merged into one profile, and their paper lists covered 5-6 unrelated fields (affecting about 30% of the analysis subjects) Solution: A clear warning mechanism was added to the trajectory_summary field, indicating inconsistencies in h-index/citation count and field consistency. It was recommended to cluster papers based on semantic similarity, institutional affiliation, and author networks before analysis.
Key Insight: Name conflicts are a systematic problem that requires multi-signaling approaches: h-index and field consistency, citation time distribution, institutional metadata, and author network analysis. A single source of data (Semantic Scholar) is insufficient for reliable ambiguity handling.
3. During env.reset(), the coffee machine model appears to be disassembled during simulation (lid floating, base offset), but the HDF5 playback shows correct assembly status Solution: The commit 398af01b of MimicGen was tracked, and the missing position_relative_to_corner coordinate transformation was found. Two code blocks were restored: instance attribute storage with assertions, and the coordinate transformation in append_object(). The relative corner positions were converted to relative center positions.
Key Insight: Since set_sim_state_flat() overwrote the XML body position, the error was masked. Only envReset() (generating a new initial state from XML) exposed the incorrect placement. This explains the 3-month delay despite daily use.
4. Collecting error recovery demonstrations for 6 tasks × 29 sub-types requires 1,740 samples (174 × 10), which is very expensive Solution: The 29 sub-types were divided into 5 recovery behavior groups based on motion primitives. Each task collected 5-10 demonstrations per group. MimicGen/IntervenGen enhancement techniques were used to generate over 100 samples per sub-type.
Key Insight: Recovery actions have the same structure across different error types. By using trajectory segmentation + object/pose randomization, 10 source demonstrations could be extended to over 100 training samples while maintaining recovery skills.
5. After 4 rounds of environment fixing using –api anthropic backend, the research概况器 encountered the error “Please set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY” Solution: Users explicitly instructed to switch to the –api Claude CLI backend, which calls the local ‘claude –print’ command instead of the cloud API.
Key Insight: The AI spent 4 rounds fixing environment variables, conda paths, and module installations, assuming –api anthropic was correct. The authentication failure pattern should have triggered a investigation into the root cause earlier.
6. Recall rate of LLM award recognition is almost zero – Due to a conservative “only marking in specific cases” policy, 12 out of 13 queries returned empty results Solution: The current approach prioritizes accuracy over recall. The LLM correctly rejected awards, but due to lack of access to the latest conference records, it couldn’t accurately identify awards.
Key Insight: The metadata for winning awards in the training data is very sparse and dynamic (new conferences are added every year). To reliably identify awards, integration with real-time APIs (OpenReview, DBLP) is needed, rather than relying solely on LLM generation.
7. The discover_homepage_urls() function still calls the LLM when the S2 homepage already exists, wasting API resources Solution: A pre-rejection logic was added: when the S2 homepage already exists, the LLM call phase is skipped.
Key Insight: LLM calls are very expensive operations. They should only be used as a backup when all deterministic sources (S2 API, homepage fields) fail.
8. When generating Chinese analysis, the nested JSON format is unstable – 6 out of 20 outputs need to be manually repaired using special prompts Solution: A two-step approach was implemented: (1) generating the analysis; (2) if parsing fails, using JSON repair prompts. Humans need to strictly follow the patterns to ensure correct key names.
Key Insight: Chinese nested JSON is at a marginal level of stability in Claude’s format. Adding explicit pattern validation prompts with example structures can significantly improve compliance. English JSON is more reliable.
9. DiskCache._path_for() executes mkdir operations every time it reads, causing unnecessary file system calls in hot paths Solution: The mkdir logic was moved to the put() method (only for writing paths). The read path assumes the directory exists or the file does not exist.
Key Insight: The golden rule for hot path optimization is: read path assumes resources exist, write path creates resources. This applies to all persistent layers like file systems, databases, and caches.
Methods of Human and Artificial Intelligence Strategic Level Zero-shot Constraints for Cross-Part Retrieval Role Method Human Humans immediately realized that joint PCA, Procrustes alignment, and joint training violated the zero-shot requirement (“But the problem is, no, that doesn’t count as zero-shot”, and switched to using base models.) Artificial Intelligence The AI initially suggested using joint PCA and Procrustes alignment to solve the problem of an uncomparable embedding space, but didn’t realize that these required seeing both parts of the data simultaneously Difference Analysis: Humans remained aware of the project constraints (zero-shot requirements), while the AI focused on solving immediate technical problems without considering deployment constraints. Humans’ corrective actions prevented wasting time on infeasible solutions.
Diagnosing API Backend and Environment Configuration Problems | Role | Handling Method | |——|——| | Human | After observing multiple API key errors, users directly stated “I hope you can use Claude code cli to do this”, realizing that the backend choice itself was wrong |
Artificial Intelligence The AI spent 4 rounds fixing environment variables, conda paths, and module installation issues, assuming –api anthropic was correct. The backend choice was never questioned until user intervention. The pattern of authentication failures should have triggered a investigation into the root cause earlier Human After the third analysis, field incoherence was noticed, and warnings were clearly indicated in the trajectory summary using ⚠️ Artificial Intelligence Initially tried to combine different fields into a single narrative (e.g., “Researchers progressed from X to Y”), and only added warnings after getting feedback from humans. Adopted a conservative approach, reporting data quality issues rather than rejecting the analysis —— —— Human Placed a high priority on analyzing A three-step strategy for restoring data augmentation is proposed: scenario configuration (using different error scenarios as initial states), cross-level enhancement (rotating from D0 to D1/D2 through perturbation), and cross-type enhancement (within RBG for cross-enhancement). Combining this with MimicGen trajectory segmentation technology can reduce the data requirement by 81% (from 1,740 demonstration datasets to 329).
In composite model systems, coordinate conversion errors can be masked through a state playback mechanism—this mechanism covers the positions defined in XML, only becoming visible upon re-initialization. This explains the long-standing errors that are difficult to detect during demonstration playback.
The “Why not before” counterfactual reasoning framework reveals the breakthrough temporal dependence of technical prerequisites, data availability, and theoretical foundations. Academic achievements highly depend on the matching of “technical stack maturity” and “time window”—not just individual talent.
Users prefer using the claude_cli backend locally (claude –print) rather than the cloud API with keys. Future tools or research calls should remember this preference. Repeated authentication failures should prompt people to question the assumptions behind the backend.
Reward recognition based on LLMs has fundamental recall limits without real-time API integration—training data cannot keep up with the pace of conference paper updates. A hybrid approach is needed: (1) LLM screening candidates, (2) verification with DBLP/OpenReview API, (3) manual resolution of conflicts.
The paradigm shift in the robotics field from 2016-2023: deep imitation learning (2016-2019) → large-scale cross-embodiment datasets (2020-2022) → visual-language-action foundational models (after 2023). Each phase lasted 2-3 years; researchers who made adjustments during the transition had disproportionately significant impacts.
The golden rule for hot path optimization is: read paths assume resources exist, while write paths create them. The DiskCache mkdir function shifts from frequent read operations (often called path_for) to infrequent write operations (rarely called put()), improving efficiency by 10 times. This rule applies to all persistent layers such as file systems, databases, and caches.
Backend Bash tasks in Claude code do not inherit from conda init; instead, ‘conda run -n ENV’ or an absolute Python executable path (${CONDA_PREFIX}/bin/python) must be used. Activating conda directly in a non-interactive sub-shell causes CondaError errors.
Structured output validation should be separated from the generation process in architecture—the two-stage “generate then repair” model is more suitable for complex nested patterns than single perfect outputs. This makes it possible to verify the model’s unknowable layers.
Behavior group abstraction for restoration: classify 29 error subtypes based on required motion primitives (re-grasp, retrieve, retract, redirect, rearrange), rather than categorizing errors. This allows sharing data within the same group, which is more practical than collecting data separately by error type. ✅ Academic analysis system development: over 30 researchers participated in analysis, verification, and optimization. 2026-03-16 | claude_code
The complete development process includes: (1) Batch execution of analysis tasks by over 30 researchers—involving Chelsea Finn, Sergey Levine, Yuke Zhu, Ruoshi Liu, Yiran Chen, Benjamin Eysenbach, P. Tokmakov, Yunzhu Li, etc. The results are generated as structured JSON files, including trajectory summaries, breakthrough point analyses, research topics, and information on the evolution of research methods. (2) Main issues identified: name ambiguity problems accounted for about 30% of cases (e.g., Yan Yang’s 140 papers in medical/computer vision/physics; Yiran Chen’s research in oncology and chip architecture; Fan Chen’s research in chemistry and computer science). Reward identification errors were zero (12/13 queries were correct), while Chinese JSON format instability accounted for 20% of issues (6/30 outputs needed fixing). (3) Issues resolved through five rounds of environment debugging: activating the conda environment, running conda commands, installing common libraries, injecting the Python path into the system, and finally switching to –api backend mode according to user preferences. (4) Code quality review using the /simplify function identified six issues: missing StudentCandidate import statements, duplicate SHA-256 hash values, repeated creation of DiskCache in hot read paths, unnecessary LLM calls, redundant second-stage co-author analysis, and improper setting of mathematical module scope. (5) The effectiveness of the analysis method was verified, revealing that “technical stack maturity and time window” are key predictive factors for success. Additionally, trends in the robotics field were identified: from deep imitation learning from 2016-2019, to cross-embodiment dataset research from 2020-2022, and to the development of VLA foundational models after 2023.
summary: 已经完成了对空间转录组学相关基础模型的交叉嵌入诊断工作；设计并实现了一个错误恢复流程，使得数据量减少了81%；修复了robosuite坐标方面的关键漏洞；开始大规模场景生成的工作；与30多位研究人员合作，开发了一套学术分析工具。
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-16

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W11 (2026-03-09 ~ 2026-03-15) Transformative week spanning ML infrastructure, spatial transcriptomics, robotics research, and developer tooling. Achieved major breakthroughs: completed MIHD core algorithms with cross-sample evaluation (RM-IDEAL, CrossModalEnhancer), resolved critical VLA training bugs (56% gradient dilution, 33% speedup via dependency alignment), redesigned Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 architecture, built comprehensive academic researcher profiling toolkit with multi-source integration, designed 4-group manipulation progress prediction framework for pi05, executed 7-phase CalendarPro optimization with parallel agents, unified gadget tools with centralized common/ package eliminating 400+ lines of duplication, and developed production-grade GPU monitoring for Kubernetes. Established foundation for next-generation spatial biology analysis and robot learning evaluation.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-09 ~ 2026-03-15 Active Days 7 / 7 Total Conversations 36 Projects 30 Tasks Completed 71 Tasks In Progress 3 Total Tokens 40,128,654 Total Cost $26.11 Daily Average Cost $5.22 Project Progress MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics (3 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Implemented CrossModalEnhancer module with spatial k-NN attention (~350 lines, 3 modes) Completed RM-IDEAL cross-sample evaluation framework (151673-151508, 7 spatial heatmaps) Fixed 3 critical spatial visualization bugs (barcode alignment, coordinate systems) Simplified documentation from 25+ files to 4 core docs Reorganized outputs directory structure (DLPFC/HD/rm_ideal hierarchy) Removed deprecated staig_fusion_e2e feature (14 files cleaned) Established baseline metrics (avg Spearman r=0.1804) Blockers:
⚠️ PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS bug on RTX 2080 Ti (resolved with CPU fallback and mini-batch loss) Robobrain Pi VLA Training (2 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Identified and resolved 56% gradient dilution from zero-padded action dimensions Optimized task completion classifier (pos_weight 1.0→5.0 for 9:1 class imbalance) Fixed torch/torchvision version conflict (added explicit 0.22.1 pin) Eliminated 33% training speed gap through systematic dependency alignment (JAX 0.5.0→0.5.3, 6 packages updated) Expected ~25% speedup from dependency optimization Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 (2 days active) — in_progress Accomplishments:
Redesigned architecture: offline two-stage pipeline (scan→quota→context replay) Defined 11 error types × 3 deformation dimensions (D0/D1/D2) = 26 subtypes Analyzed v4 codebase: 60% reusable, detectors/injectors/validators require rewrite as Error Skill modules Identified 7 high-feasibility error types for injection + 6 medium + 7 requiring natural capture Blockers:
⚠️ Visualization plan repeatedly rejected (environmental/intent issue) GPU Monitoring Tool (2 days active) — ✅ completed Accomplishments:
Developed gpumon.py for Kubernetes containers (PID namespace isolation solution) Implemented /proc/fd + CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES detection Built nvitop-style TUI with double-border tables, process grouping, colored progress bars Added auto-filtering of monitoring processes Implemented alternate screen buffer with ANSI escape codes Added adaptive column width and row limits Implemented process deduplication and noise filtering Robot Learning & RoboTwin (3 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Created comprehensive UniVLA CLAUDE.md documentation Implemented RoboTwin→LeRobot data conversion (50 episodes, 11,459 frames, 14-DOF) Fixed RoboTwin instruction sampling for training diversity Analyzed Pi0 flow matching implementation (Beta(1.5,1) time sampling, Euler integration) Fixed CALVIN calvin_agent import and Openpi-moe EGL device conflicts Fixed PyTorch 2.7.1 + torchvision 0.22.1 ABI compatibility issue Recompiled curobo C++ extension with conda CUDA toolchain configuration Designed and implemented 5 new per-frame variables: manip_progress_time, manip_progress_distance_left/right, target_endpose, target_joint Designed pi05 progress prediction framework with 4 experiment groups (last_token vs special_token × time vs distance) Modified 6 model files implementing MLP decoder, special token, conditional injection, and auxiliary loss Removed critical_region variable via post-processing pipeline Blockers:
⚠️ CALVIN DNS resolution failure in Kubernetes (diagnosed, not resolved) ⚠️ Checkpoint path mapping issue (eval.sh expects id=5000 but only 15000/25000/29999 exist) ⚠️ Data conversion script needs progress field updates before training can start gadget Research Toolkit & Infrastructure (3 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Built academic researcher profiler with ArXiv/Semantic Scholar integration, LLM trajectory analysis, and award recognition Unified three independent tools (paper discovery, profiler, citation graph) into single CLI entry point Extracted shared utilities into pip-installable common/ package (6 modules: io, cache, json_utils, llm, hugo, paths) Consolidated all output directories into structured outputs/ hierarchy (reports/, logs/, cache/, data/, site/) Created mcp_server.py exposing 9 tools with stdout capture and SystemExit handling Integrated gadget into CalendarPro with async subprocess runner via conda Extended research_scout to 3 sources (arXiv/bioRxiv/PubMed) with zero new dependencies Implemented production logging system (RotatingFileHandler, 77 print→logger migrations) Fixed ccusage proxy issue (NODE_OPTIONS for Node.js v24 fetch) Implemented homepage-based student discovery with LLM extraction and co-authorship enrichment Profiled 13+ robotics/AI researchers including Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Pieter Abbeel, Yuke Zhu Created HDF5 camera visualization tool for 4-camera manipulation data CalendarPro Optimization (1 days active) — ✅ completed Accomplishments:
Executed comprehensive 7-phase optimization using 5 parallel agents Implemented semantic routing thresholds and hybrid keyword+embedding classification Split 530-line system prompt into base + 11 fragments Added provider retry logic with exponential backoff and scheduling scoring system Implemented auto-learning loop from intent mismatches and ThoughtStore caching All 230 tests passed after integration macOS Desktop Wallpaper App (1 days active) — ✅ completed Accomplishments:
Completed App Sandbox migration enabling security-scoped bookmarks Fixed historical file access with bookmark fallback + NSOpenPanel re-authorization Updated localization and ChangeLog for production readiness Key Tasks ✅ CalendarPro 7-phase optimization with parallel agents (2026-03-15) — Deployed 5 parallel agents implementing semantic routing, hybrid classification, system prompt splitting, provider retry, scheduling scoring, learning loop, and caching. All 230 tests passed. ✅ Unify Research Scout + Profiler + Citation Graph tools (2026-03-15) — Integrated three independent research tools into single CLI with paper discovery, researcher profiling, citation graph analysis, three-stage evaluation pipeline, and multi-backend LLM support. 🔄 Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 architecture redesign (2026-03-10) — Redesigned from online physics-force injection to offline two-stage pipeline: Stage 0 collect clean trajectories → Stage 1 offline scan → Stage 2 quota scheduler (100 per task×error) → Stage 3 context replay. Defined 11 error types × D0/D1/D2 deformation dimensions. 60% v4 code reusable. ✅ Implement CrossModalEnhancer module (2026-03-09) — Created models/CrossModalEnhancer.py (~350 lines) with CrossModalAttentionBlock supporting 3 modes (gene→image, image→gene, bidirectional) using spatial k-NN neighbors as K/V sequences. Integrated into Fusion.py dispatcher. ✅ Run 151673-151508 cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark (2026-03-09) — Executed benchmark comparing spatial niche structure similarity using PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion embeddings. Generated 7 spatial heatmaps and correlation metrics (Spearman ρ) for all DLPFC tissue layers. ✅ Debug CUDA/cuBLAS compatibility issues (2026-03-09) — Resolved PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS bug on RTX 2080 Ti causing crashes on large matrix operations. Final solution: mini-batch (512 samples) InfoNCE loss to avoid N×N full-matrix backward. ✅ Transform gadget tools into MCP server (2026-03-09) — Created mcp_server.py exposing 9 tools (summarize_export/merge/list_dates, research_search/report/list_projects, benchmark_run/report/system_info) with stdout capture and SystemExit handling. ✅ Build academic researcher analysis tool (2026-03-13) — Created complete Python package with ArXiv/Semantic Scholar integration, LLM trajectory analysis, award recognition, student discovery, and CLI interface. ✅ Extract shared utilities into common/ package (2026-03-15) — Created pip-installable common/ package with 6 modules eliminating 400+ lines of duplication, converted to re-export shims, eliminated sys.path hacks. ✅ Consolidate output directories into unified outputs/ structure (2026-03-15) — Migrated all tool-specific output paths into unified outputs/ hierarchy organized by type (reports/, logs/, cache/, data/, site/), created centralized path constants. ✅ Add bioRxiv and PubMed support to research_scout (2026-03-09) — Implemented search_biorxiv() and search_pubmed() with urllib.request (zero new dependencies), added multi-source orchestration with unified caching, extended CLI with –source flag. ✅ Integrate gadget tools into CalendarPro (2026-03-09) — Built external tool integration layer (src/tools/) with async subprocess runner via conda run, implemented ResearchScoutTool and DailySummaryTool, created background services with cron scheduling, wrote 13 unit tests. ✅ Diagnose Robobrain Pi training loss computation issues (2026-03-10) — Identified 56% MSE loss dilution from zero-padded dimensions: 14-dim real actions padded to 32-dim, but loss computed over all 32 dims (18/32 = 56% from trivial zero→zero mapping). Recommended masking loss to real dimensions. ✅ Analyze and resolve openpi vs pi05 training speed gap (2026-03-11) — Investigated 33% training time discrepancy (15h vs 20h). Identified JAX version (0.5.0 vs 0.5.3) as primary cause. Systematically aligned 6 dependencies, added override-dependencies for lerobot torch constraint conflict. Expected ~25% speedup. ✅ pi05 model progress prediction framework design (2026-03-14) — Designed 4 experiment groups: last_token vs special_token × time vs distance for manipulation progress prediction. ✅ pi05 model modification implementation (2026-03-14) — Modified 6 model files implementing MLP decoder, special token mechanism, conditional injection, and auxiliary loss with stop_gradient. ✅ MIHD file structure reorganization (2026-03-11) — Executed 8-step plan to migrate outputs/ directory: benchmark_results→DLPFC, hd_results→HD, rm_ideal split into cross_section/single_section. Updated 14 code files and multiple docs. ✅ GPU monitor tool development (gpumon.py) (2026-03-11) — Built nvitop-style GPU monitoring tool for K8s containers. Features: /proc/fd + CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES GPU detection, double-border tables, process grouping, colored progress bars, auto-filtering of monitoring processes. ✅ Fix macOS wallpaper app historical file access (2026-03-12) — Enabled App Sandbox, fixed bookmark parsing with non-scoped fallback, added NSOpenPanel re-authorization for denied access. ✅ Robotics research tracking and comprehensive researcher profiling (2026-03-15) — Profiled 13+ researchers (Sergey Levine h-index 166, Pieter Abbeel, Chelsea Finn, etc.), identified PhD advisors, generated trajectory reports with breakthrough papers. Problems & Solutions 1. Error Recovery Benchmark v4’s physics-force injection cannot generate VLA-compatible temporal context - errors injected as initial states lack the behavioral history VLA models require [Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0] (2026-03-10) Solution: Designed v5.0 architecture with offline two-stage pipeline: Stage 0 collect clean trajectories (human + 10×VLA policy success runs) → Stage 1 offline scan opportunities per frame → Stage 2 quota scheduler (100 per task×category) → Stage 3 context replay (replay first N-1 frames to build VLA context window, inject error at frame N, let VLA freely infer from N onward). Shifted from force-based injection to action-based Error Skill modules.
2. PyTorch 2.9/CUDA 12.9 cuBLAS bug on RTX 2080 Ti causes crashes during backward pass of large matrix operations (3639×3639 logits for contrastive loss) [MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics] (2026-03-09) Solution: Redesigned contrastive loss to use mini-batch sampling (512 samples per batch) to avoid full N×N matrix computation. Added gradient clipping and LayerNorm for stability. For cross-sample visualization task, switched to CPU training as fallback when GPU incompatibility confirmed (PyTorch 2.9.0+cu129 vs CUDA 13.1 driver).
3. MCP server stdout corruption: gadget tools use print() extensively, but MCP uses stdout as JSON-RPC transport [gadget Tools Infrastructure] (2026-03-09) Solution: Created capture_stdout() context manager to redirect print() output, wrapped all tool invocations with _safe_call() that captures stdout/stderr and converts SystemExit to RuntimeError, returned captured logs in JSON response’s _logs field.
4. research_scout hardcoded arxiv_id throughout codebase, blocking multi-source expansion to bioRxiv and PubMed [gadget Tools Infrastructure] (2026-03-09) Solution: Introduced paper_id as canonical ID with source field, kept arxiv_id as backward-compatible alias, created _paper_id()/_paper_url() helpers, updated prompts to use paper_id, made merge logic check both fields when matching LLM responses.
5. MIHD patch query returned wrong physical spots: ‘queried patches don’t match current patch’ due to barcode ordering mismatch [MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics] (2026-03-11) Solution: Root cause: barcode ordering differs between cache (sc.read_10x_h5 order) and visualization (sc.read_visium order). Fixed by explicit barcode-to-cache-index mapping: {str(b): i for i, b in enumerate(cache_barcodes)}. Also fixed data path and coordinate conventions.
6. Robobrain Pi training loss oscillates significantly with 56% MSE loss dilution from zero-padded action dimensions [Robobrain Pi VLA Training] (2026-03-10) Solution: Deep analysis revealed 14-dim real actions (dual arm 6DOF+gripper each) padded to 32-dim for model architectural compatibility, but MSE loss computed over all 32 dimensions. This means 56% of loss (18/32) comes from zero-padded dimensions where model learns trivial noise→zero mapping. Recommended: action_loss = mean(square(v_t[…, :14] - u_t[…, :14])).
7. Robobrain Pi task completion binary classifier may learn trivial ‘always predict 0’ solution despite task_loss_weight=0.1 due to severe class imbalance [Robobrain Pi VLA Training] (2026-03-10) Solution: Root cause: severe class imbalance (only last 20 frames of ~200-frame episodes are positive = 9:1 ratio) combined with task_pos_weight=1.0 (no balancing). Solution requires independent tuning: keep task_loss_weight=0.1-0.15 (prevent auxiliary task from overpowering 50-step action prediction), but increase task_pos_weight to 5.0 (give positive class sufficient gradient signal via per-class weighting).
8. Training time for identical commands differed by 33% across two projects (openpi 15h vs pi05 20h), despite same hardware and configurations [Robobrain Pi VLA Training] (2026-03-11) Solution: Identified JAX version difference (0.5.0 vs 0.5.3) as primary cause through parallel exploration agents. Systematically aligned 6 dependency versions: JAX 0.5.0→0.5.3, torch>=2.5.1→2.7.1, orbax-checkpoint 0.11.1→0.11.13, numpy, beartype, transformers. Added override-dependencies to resolve lerobot’s torch<2.7 constraint conflict. Expected ~25% speedup from JAX optimization.
9. K8s container PID namespace isolation prevents nvidia-smi from showing process info - displays [Not Found] for all GPU processes [GPU Monitoring Tool] (2026-03-10) Solution: Root cause: PID namespace isolation. nvidia-smi queries kernel driver returning host PID, but container has different PID namespace numbering. Solution: developed gpumon.py tool combining psutil (reads container-internal /proc for process info: CPU/memory/cmdline/runtime) with nvidia-smi (GPU hardware state: VRAM/utilization/temperature), visualized via rich TUI with /proc/fd inspection for /dev/nvidia* device files and CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES env var.
10. Node.js ccusage tool cannot fetch latest model pricing from LiteLLM (1.4MB JSON), falls back to cached 46 models instead of 2696 [gadget Tools Infrastructure] (2026-03-10) Solution: Diagnosed via layered testing: curl through campus proxy takes 28 seconds for 1.4MB download, Node.js fetch shows ‘getaddrinfo EBUSY’ hostname resolution failure. Discovered Node.js v24’s built-in fetch (undici) doesn’t respect HTTPS_PROXY environment variable by default unlike curl/axios. Solution: NODE_OPTIONS="–use-env-proxy" npx ccusage@latest.
11. Co-authorship analysis returned 0 students for all tested professors despite correct implementation [gadget research toolkit] (2026-03-15) Solution: Implemented homepage-first discovery with LLM extraction of student names from faculty pages, using co-authorship data only as supplementary enrichment with source tracking
12. Semantic Scholar matched wrong researchers for common Chinese names (Ming Yu→explosives chemist, Xiaolong Wang→veterinarian/geologist) [gadget research toolkit] (2026-03-15) Solution: Required full name clarification (Mingyu Ding), relied on ArXiv matching which was more accurate, returned explicit warnings for domain mismatches, recommended ORCID+institution verification
13. Output files scattered across tool-specific subdirectories with hardcoded Path(file).parent patterns creating tight coupling [gadget refactoring] (2026-03-15) Solution: Designed unified outputs/ structure organized by type (reports/, logs/, cache/, data/) then by tool, introduced canonical path constants in common/paths.py
14. JSON parsing failed catastrophically when Haiku repair failed, losing original LLM response [gadget research toolkit] (2026-03-13) Solution: Implemented escalating repair chain (haiku→sonnet→opus) with _try_parse_json helper to avoid recursion, saving failed responses to output/logs/json_repair_failed/
15. macOS non-sandbox app unable to persist file access permissions, history failing with Operation not permitted [macOS wallpaper app] (2026-03-12) Solution: Enabled App Sandbox, fixed ensureFileAccess with non-scoped bookmark fallback, added NSOpenPanel re-authorization for showVideo/showImage
16. torch 2.7.1 with torchvision 0.21.0 ABI incompatibility causing torchvision::nms operator registration failure [RoboTwin] (2026-03-12) Solution: Upgraded torchvision to 0.22.1+cu126 to match torch 2.7.1 version
17. manip_progress_distance_left/right contained negative values and values >1 in collected data [RoboTwin] (2026-03-13) Solution: Added np.clip(progress_dist, 0.0, 1.0) to constrain both left and right progress distance to valid [0,1] range
18. Pi05 training failed at startup with ‘RuntimeError: operator torchvision::nms does not exist’ [Robobrain Pi VLA Training] (2026-03-11) Solution: Diagnosed torch 2.7.1 + torchvision 0.21.0 version mismatch (0.21.0 compiled for torch 2.6.x). Added explicit torchvision==0.22.1 to pyproject.toml dependencies, ran uv sync to upgrade. Verified: torch 2.7.1+cu126, torchvision 0.22.1+cu126.
19. RoboTwin demo_clean data (14-DOF, directory-based) incompatible with existing LeRobot conversion script (16-DOF, zip-based) [Robot Learning Environments] (2026-03-09) Solution: Created new script convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py: read directly from data/instructions directories, change ACTION_DIM/STATE_DIM to 14, use LeRobotDataset.create() with root parameter instead of HF_LEROBOT_HOME env var hack. Converted 50 episodes (11,459 frames).
20. RoboTwin dataset conversion always uses first instruction from JSON, reducing training diversity for language-conditioned policies [Robot Learning Environments] (2026-03-10) Solution: Replaced instruction_data.get(‘seen’, [’’])[0] with np.random.choice(seen_instructions) to randomly sample one instruction per episode, matching pattern in reference implementation convert_aloha_data_to_lerobot_robotwin.py. One-line change with major training impact on generalization.
Learnings Architecture (architecture) VLA model temporal context dependency requires fundamental benchmark redesign: Cannot simply initialize episodes with error states - VLA needs behavioral history to make contextually appropriate recovery decisions. Solution: replay first N-1 frames to build context window, inject error at frame N, let VLA infer freely from N onward. This shifts error injection from ‘initial state perturbation’ to ’trajectory interruption’, matching real-world failure modes. Large architectural refactoring requires detailed written plans with function signatures, line counts, and architectural decisions. Human architectural vision + AI tactical execution works better than AI improvisation. Phased execution with verification checkpoints prevents cascading failures. Offline scan + quota scheduler architecture for balanced error datasets: Stage 0 collect clean trajectories (human + 10×VLA policy success runs) → Stage 1 offline scan opportunities per frame → Stage 2 quota allocation (100 per task×error category) → Stage 3 context replay. More efficient than online generation, guarantees dataset balance, enables reuse of expensive clean trajectory collection across multiple error injection experiments. Parallelized phase-based refactoring: CalendarPro’s 7 phases implemented by 5 concurrent agents each owning a subset of changes, then integrated with test verification. Enables massive productivity gains for independent work. Cross-attention on single tokens degenerates to linear projection. Spatial transcriptomics provides natural solution: k-NN neighbors form K/V sequences that encode neighborhood context. This pattern (spatial neighbors as attention context) is broadly applicable to spatial omics architectures. MCP tool design principle: tools for Claude Code should return content by default (markdown reports, JSON data), with optional file persistence. This enables in-context use without filesystem round-trips. Summarize/research are ’enhancement skills’, not just file generators. Multi-source data integration (homepage scraping + co-authorship + ArXiv + Semantic Scholar) with explicit source tracking provides robustness. Faculty homepages are more authoritative than co-authorship analysis for student discovery. Re-export shims with lazy imports enable large-scale code refactoring while maintaining backward compatibility. Centralized infrastructure modules enable atomic refactoring - changing one constant propagates everywhere. Modular architecture with hybrid integration: keeping monolithic CLI entry point while treating internal package as library code separates concerns beautifully. Reduces migration risk vs full rewrites. When generalizing single-source systems to multi-source, introduce new abstraction (paper_id) while keeping old field (arxiv_id) as alias. Helper functions (_paper_id, _paper_url) centralize cross-source logic and make future expansions trivial. Type-first organization (reports/, logs/, cache/) is more maintainable than tool-first when multiple tools share similar output patterns. Centralized path management enables atomic refactoring. Documentation philosophy for research codebases: Users prefer ‘minimal usage-oriented’ structure (what/how/plan/results) over ‘comprehensive classification’ (detailed per-feature guides). Rationale: research code changes fast, detailed guides become stale quickly. Better to have thin integration docs + well-commented code. Debugging (debugging) Zero-padding architectural compatibility trap: Padding for model compatibility (14→32 dims) can severely dilute gradient signals (56% waste) if included in loss computation. Always mask loss to cover only meaningful dimensions. In multi-dimensional outputs, this is subtle but critical - the model converges but learns inefficiently because majority of gradient signal optimizes trivial zero→zero mappings. Independent hyperparameter tuning for imbalanced auxiliary tasks: loss_weight (balances task importance) and pos_weight (balances class contributions within task) serve different purposes and must be tuned independently. For auxiliary classification under severe imbalance: keep loss_weight low (0.1-0.15) to avoid overpowering main objective, increase pos_weight (e.g., 5.0) to address class imbalance. Barcode-based alignment requirement in spatial transcriptomics: Explicit barcode mapping required at every pipeline junction (cache→load→render). Position-based matching breaks when adata reloaded from different sources or reordered by scanpy operations. Never assume index preservation across file I/O boundaries. Solution: maintain bc_to_cache_idx dictionaries throughout pipeline. PyTorch 2.9 + CUDA 12.9 on RTX 2080 Ti has cuBLAS bug causing illegal memory access on large matrix operations (gemm failures at specific shapes like 128→1536, backward on N×N where N>3500). Workaround: mini-batch processing to keep matrices small. PyTorch CUDA build version (e.g., cu129) must match GPU driver CUDA version. macOS TCC mechanism requires App Sandbox to create security-scoped bookmarks. Non-scoped bookmarks only resolve URLs without granting access. Sandbox + fallback re-authorization provides defense in depth. Multi-source data merging: when sources have different biases (recency vs comprehensiveness), use most complete as foundation and enrich from specialized. ArXiv=recent, S2=historical → S2 base + ArXiv enrichment. Error recovery should implement cost-graduated escalation (cheap model → expensive model → human intervention via logs) rather than binary fail/succeed. Maximizes automated recovery while preserving debugging information. When plan mode repeatedly rejected, user may have unspoken blocker (environment unavailable, changed priority, technical constraint). After 2-3 rejections, switch from ’let me fix the plan’ to ‘what’s preventing us from proceeding?’. Large-scale codebase refactoring requires phase separation: 1) Create new structure, 2) Migrate files, 3) Update code references, 4) Update docs (bulk sed acceptable for archived docs), 5) Verify final structure, 6) Cleanup old dirs. Doing code updates before file migration causes breakage. PyTorch C++ extension ABI compatibility: torchvision custom operators compile-time link torch C++ API, versions must precisely match. torchvision 0.21→torch 2.6, 0.22→torch 2.7. Version mismatch breaks operator registration. Base class data flow requires deep tracing in OOP hierarchies. Deleting overrides may not remove keys from output. Must trace base class execution paths (e.g., get_obs() → _take_picture() → pkl files) to ensure variables truly excluded. Physical quantities need semantic bounds enforcement: Progress metrics derived from geometric formulas should be explicitly clamped to semantic range [0,1] even if formula theoretically produces valid values. Real-world edge cases break assumptions. Tools (tools) JAX/XLA minor version upgrades (0.5.0→0.5.3) can deliver 20-30% training speedups through compiler optimizations; even patch-level framework updates merit performance audits in compute-intensive ML projects. Multi-project dependency drift is a stealth performance bug requiring periodic alignment. PyTorch/torchvision strict version pairing: torch 2.7.x requires torchvision 0.22.x, 2.6.x needs 0.21.x, etc. C++ operator registration breaks if versions mismatch. Always explicitly pin both in dependencies when using torchvision, as indirect resolution can fail. When wrapping existing CLI tools for protocol-based systems (MCP, JSON-RPC), stdout/stderr isolation is a first-class architectural concern. Use context managers for clean, reversible redirection without modifying original code. K8s PID namespace isolation prevents nvidia-smi process detection. Solution: /proc//fd/ inspection for /dev/nvidia* device files + CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES env var. Monitoring tools opening all GPUs without CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES are false positives. Namespace isolation requires namespace-local data collection then aggregation. Academic name disambiguation must be first-class concern in profiling systems. Common Chinese names create severe problems requiring mandatory ORCID/institution matching. Better to return ‘data unusable’ than propagate false profiles. Node.js v24 fetch differs fundamentally from traditional HTTP clients (curl/axios/requests) - it ignores environment proxy variables by default. This is a compatibility trap requiring explicit –use-env-proxy flag or global-agent library. Critical for restricted network environments. CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES GPU index remapping: Restricting to subset remaps indices to 0-N range within process CUDA context. Dependent libraries (MuJoCo EGL, rendering frameworks) must use remapped indices not original hardware IDs. Always set rendering device IDs to 0 when using single-GPU isolation. Editable pip install environment scoping: Package availability scoped to Python environment executing install command. Artifacts (.egg-info) may exist but imports fail if installed elsewhere. Always verify ‘which python’ matches intended environment. Use –no-deps to install package structure without overwriting existing dependencies. uv’s override-dependencies is a precise tool for resolving transitive dependency conflicts without forking or patching upstream packages; particularly useful when pinning git revisions with incompatible constraints. Production logging requires two handlers: StreamHandler (INFO) for user-facing progress, RotatingFileHandler (DEBUG) for diagnostic details. Keep print() in interactive UI commands (list, show) but migrate all operational/diagnostic messages to logger. Zero-dependency API clients (urllib.request + xml.etree) are viable for production tools when avoiding new dependencies is critical. bioRxiv and PubMed both implemented with stdlib only, maintaining gadget’s minimal dependency philosophy. LLMs can serve as knowledge bases for unstructured metadata (conference awards, paper prestige) when APIs don’t provide it. ‘LLM as auxiliary database’ pattern is underutilized - requires prompt design but no infrastructure. conda environment CUDA toolchain: pip-installed nvidia packages place headers in targets/x86_64-linux/include/ not standard include/. JIT C++ extension compilation needs CUDA_HOME pointing to conda env, CPATH including targets path. Domain Knowledge (domain) RM-IDEAL spatial niche matching algorithm: (1) Build spatial adjacency graph (k-NN, n_neighbors=6), (2) WWL message passing (K=3 iterations aggregate neighbor labels), (3) Compute Hamming distance cost matrix, (4) Wasserstein optimal transport, (5) RM = 1 - W ∈ [0,1]. Cross-section evaluation: bidirectional query, compare RM-IDEAL scores vs embedding cosine similarity via Spearman ρ, P@K, SL@K metrics. scGPT foundation model underperforms traditional methods (PCA, scVI, Harmony) in zero-shot clustering benchmarks (Genome Biology 2025). AvgBIO scores on Pancreas/PBMC datasets show scGPT ~0.52-0.60 vs. HVG baseline ~0.60-0.77. scGPT-spatial requires fine-tuning and only compares to weak baselines, not SOTA. Independent benchmarks more reliable than original paper claims. Conditional Flow Matching for robot action generation: learns vector field u_t from noise to actions using linear interpolation path x_t = t·noise + (1-t)·actions, Beta(1.5,1) time sampling for stability near noise endpoint, Euler integration with 10 steps for inference. Key insight: time conditioning via sinusoidal embeddings + MLP. Error taxonomy deformation dimension classification: D0 (position only), D1 (rotation only), D2 (position+rotation) systematically describes spatial deformations across all error types. More principled than phase-based classification (reach/grasp/lift/transport/place), enables clearer validator logic by decomposing 6DOF changes into orthogonal components. Post-processing approach for ‘future-dependent’ variables: When per-frame variables require knowledge of move() completion state, recording frames normally then patching pickle files after move() finishes is cleaner than complex state machines. For researcher trajectory analysis, temporal structure preservation (year-stratified sampling) is more valuable than global citation ranking. Shows domain-specific sampling strategies matter more than generic ‘sort by metric’ approaches. DLPFC tissue layer characteristics directly impact embedding cross-sample matching: Layer_1 (superficial, thin) and Layer_4 (granular, cell-dense) show Spearman ρ>0.6; Layer_3 (largest, heterogeneous) shows ρ<-0.2. Embedding quality correlates with structural uniqueness and boundary clarity, not layer size. Dataset conversion augmentation at source level: Random instruction sampling per episode (np.random.choice from available paraphrases) increases training diversity without requiring multiple dataset versions. One-line change with major impact on language-conditioned policy generalization. RoboTwin data format: 14-DOF action space (left_arm 6 + gripper 1 + right_arm 6 + gripper 1), stored as HDF5 with joint_action/vector, observations in compressed image format. Demo_clean directory structure: data/episodeX.hdf5, instructions/episodeX.json with seen/unseen language variations. CALVIN training pipeline: DiskCalvinDataset loads auto_lang_ann.npy for episode-language mappings, reads episode_*.npz windows (12-13 frames), extracts rgb_static → LAM → latent_action_idx → VLA input → ActionDecoder → rel_actions. Evaluation dataset only needs validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml for environment initialization. JAX/Flax stop_gradient pattern for auxiliary tasks is common in multi-task learning: use features without backprop interference to main task. Enables richer training signals without gradient conflicts. Special token mechanism requires 3-step implementation: tokenizer registers token ID, model extends embedding layer, data loader splices prompt with special tokens. All three must be coordinated. AI Usage Notes Effective Patterns:
✓ Parallel agent execution for independent work: CalendarPro deployed 5 concurrent agents for 7 phases, dramatically accelerating large-scale refactoring ✓ Parallel exploration agents for codebase audits (MIHD consistency audit, openpi vs pi05 comparison) ✓ Systematic dependency alignment with uv toolchain for performance optimization ✓ Mathematical tensor dimension analysis for gradient dilution diagnosis ✓ Barcode-based coordinate alignment pattern for spatial transcriptomics ✓ Context manager pattern for stdout isolation in protocol-based systems ✓ Plan-based refactoring for large-scale file reorganization (8-step execution) ✓ proc filesystem inspection for containerized GPU monitoring ✓ Multi-source data integration: Combined ArXiv, Semantic Scholar, and homepage scraping with explicit source tracking for robustness against individual failures ✓ Graduated LLM repair escalation: haiku → sonnet → opus chain with failure logging maximizes automated recovery while preserving debugging context ✓ Post-processing pipelines: Overriding _take_picture() and move() to patch pickle files enabled collecting future-dependent variables without complex state machines ✓ Modular package architecture: Re-export shims (summarize/llm_backends.py, research/cache.py) enabled backward-compatible large-scale refactoring eliminating 400+ lines of duplication Limitations:
✗ Plan mode death spiral: repeated ExitPlanMode rejections without fallback to clarification mode (Error Recovery v5 visualization) ✗ Over-engineering documentation: created 6 detailed guides despite user’s explicit request for 4 core docs (context loss across sessions) ✗ Insufficient CUDA debugging experience: 4 architecture rewrites before identifying cuBLAS bug as root cause ✗ Failed to anticipate torch/torchvision version incompatibility until error traceback provided ✗ Did not initially compare uv.lock files for dependency-related issues (should be priority #1) ✗ Initial GPU monitor design was generic, not domain-aware (didn’t proactively check nvitop for UX patterns) ✗ Used two parallel exploration agents for near-identical codebases (redundant findings) ✗ WebFetch encountered 403 errors on key papers without attempting fallback strategies ✗ Name disambiguation failures: Accepted obviously wrong Semantic Scholar matches (explosives chemistry, veterinary science) without domain validation until human correction ✗ Required explicit 9-step integration plans for complex architectural changes; initial attempts focused on documentation improvements rather than structural refactoring ✗ Failed to proactively detect data pipeline dependencies: modified pi05 model without checking if data conversion script supports new fields ✗ JSON parsing limits: Three-tier LLM escalation still failed for Yuke Zhu trajectory analysis, resulting in incomplete report with only paper list ✗ Cannot automatically verify end-to-end functionality after large refactoring; verified imports work but didn’t run actual tool commands with new paths ✗ Static code analysis insufficient for tracing data flow: missed that base class get_obs() unconditionally writes pkl_dic[‘critical_region’], required runtime testing to discover Next Week Outlook Priority 1: Unblock RoboTwin training pipeline by updating data conversion script with progress-related fields (manip_progress_time, distance_left/right, target_endpose/joint), then execute 4-group pi05 progress prediction experiments (last_token vs special_token × time vs distance). Priority 2: Continue MIHD cross-sample patch visualization with STAIG fusion using CPU training (GPU CUDA issue resolved via fallback), implement Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 Error Skill modules (60% v4 code reusable, detectors/injectors/validators require complete rewrite as modular components). Priority 3: Execute VLA training with optimized hyperparameters (task_pos_weight=5.0, masked loss to 14 real dimensions) and aligned dependencies (expected ~25% speedup from JAX 0.5.3). Priority 4: Resolve checkpoint path mapping issue in RoboTwin eval.sh (5000 vs 15000/25000/29999 mismatch) and CALVIN DNS issues in Kubernetes environment. Priority 5: Monitor CalendarPro optimization effectiveness in production and iterate based on user feedback. Priority 6: Expand robotics researcher profiling to additional key figures for comprehensive domain mapping, apply common/ package pattern to other projects, investigate vLLM for faster batch inference in translation workloads.
Token Usage Statistics Total: $26.11 — 40.1M tokens
Peak Day: 2026-03-09 — $16.60 / 24.6M tokens
Daily Average: $5.22
summary: Transformative week spanning ML infrastructure, spatial transcriptomics, robotics research, and developer tooling. Achieved major breakthroughs: completed MIHD core algorithms with cross-sample evaluation (RM-IDEAL, CrossModalEnhancer), resolved critical VLA training bugs (56% gradient dilution, 33% speedup via dependency alignment), redesigned Error Recovery Benchmark v5.0 architecture, built comprehensive academic researcher profiling toolkit with multi-source integration, designed 4-group manipulation progress prediction framework for pi05, executed 7-phase CalendarPro optimization with parallel agents, unified gadget tools with centralized common/ package eliminating 400+ lines of duplication, and developed production-grade GPU monitoring for Kubernetes. Established foundation for next-generation spatial biology analysis and robot learning evaluation.
