⚠️ GPU acceleration for RM-IDEAL Optimal Transport planned but not implemented ⚠️ STAIG fusion architecture limitation affects interpretation of all published results VLA Training & Error Recovery Benchmark (FLARE/Phoenix) (7 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Fixed 4 cascading BC-RNN evaluation bugs (demo_len, trigger_step, transport detection, RP baseline) making metrics trustworthy Launched FLARE D0×4 training on 4 GPUs (coffee, stack, stack_three, threading - stable 6.3s/step, ~63h/task) Monitored 10 concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA training jobs: an49 38% complete (~37h remaining), an53 27% (~46h remaining) Optimized Phoenix data loading from 5% to 80%+ GPU utilization via HDF5 preloading (uint8 saves 4× storage) Designed and implemented critical region annotation system for 10 manipulation tasks using distance-based heuristics Generated 191 multi-task error scenarios across 6 tasks + 9 visualization videos Created comprehensive external dependencies inventory (65+ GB, 14 categories) Resolved LLaVA base model download bottleneck via offline HF-to-original format conversion Built BC-RNN baseline and Pi0.5 LoRA evaluation pipeline with multi-task support Synchronized error_recovery_benchmark documentation: 6,474 episodes, 53 framework files, 122 tests Established zhaoganlong 6-stage training pipeline plan for 9 MimicGen tasks on 8× A800 GPUs Redesigned core pipeline architecture showing three parallel error scene sources Separated Phoenix and FLARE projects with shared dependency management (78GB→37MB per project, 99% savings) Blockers:
⚠️ LLaVA MPM/MCM training halted due to severe dataset scale estimation error (160K→1M, 13.5 days vs hours expected) ⚠️ Pi0.5 base model misconfiguration: used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base, requires retraining verification ⚠️ an53 node crashed with NODE_FAIL due to resource overload ⚠️ Symlink removal refactoring planned but not executed (65 symlinks mapped) Desktop Video macOS App (4 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Fixed critical security-scoped bookmark system: eliminated 100% restoration failure rate (Code 257) Implemented complete WKWebView webpage wallpaper functionality for arbitrary videos (YouTube/Bilibili) Resolved 4 major bugs: missing entitlements, errorLog concurrency, log spam (1550+ entries), double window open Implemented liquid glass screensaver clock with .withinWindow blend mode for video transparency Fixed screensaver visual glitches: ghost artifacts, animation smoothing, no-video mode support Fixed sandbox network blocking, window layer interaction, scrollbar transparency issues Implemented browse mode framework with ESC listener and dynamic window level switching Fixed volume sync and mute controls for all content types (video/image/web) Enhanced CLAUDE.md documentation with localization workflow and bilingual entry structure Blockers:
⚠️ Browse mode window layer adjustment incomplete (.desktopWindow non-interactive, need .normal layer) gadget toolkit (5 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Built research scout system from scratch (~750-line core + two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline) Implemented three search modes: keyword, conference, author with differential caching strategies Added language configuration, reverse initialization, arxiv_id deduplication with early-stop Achieved Windows/UNIX cross-platform Hugo deployment support Batch-generated 20+ days of February historical reports (Feb 1-Mar 6) Generated February 2026 monthly summary via LLM aggregation Manually fixed JSON truncation issues in merge phase for 13 historical dates CalendarPro (3 days active) — ✅ completed Accomplishments:
Completed 31-goal autonomous butler upgrade (16 new services, 21 file modifications, 68 tests) Fixed critical context loss bug (three-layer issue: keyword pre-check, timing, missing history parameter) Implemented context-aware intent classification (context_dependent keywords, session management) Resolved 16 dangerous silent exception handlers and 3 incomplete implementations Created 1674-line comprehensive Chinese tutorial (60% user guide, 40% developer guide) All 360 tests passing after systematic fixes RoboTwin (2 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Documented two-phase data collection pipeline (seed discovery + deterministic replay) Fixed SAPIEN rendering errors in Docker via Vulkan/EGL environment configuration Generated 9 visualization videos from error benchmark scenes Key Tasks ✅ Fix Desktop Video security-scoped bookmark system (2026-03-02) — Root cause: empty entitlements file → bookmark creation failed silently → Code 257 on restore. Fixed by adding bookmark.app-scope permission + non-scoped fallback parsing + lifecycle tracking ✅ BC-RNN evaluation pipeline 4 bug fixes (2026-03-07) — Fixed demo_length→demo_len field name, trigger_step reading from wrong source, transport stage detection logic, and missing RP=1.0 for successful episodes. All 127 unit tests passed, GPU validation confirmed metrics trustworthy ✅ CalendarPro Personal Butler System comprehensive upgrade (2026-03-05) — Executed 31 sub-goals across 5 phases: autonomous intelligence engine, deep thought processing, comprehensive life management, learning adaptation, multi-agent orchestration. Modified 21 core files, created 16 new services, validated with 319 passing tests ✅ Desktop Video WKWebView webpage wallpaper complete implementation (2026-03-07) — Created WebNavigationHandler.swift, extended ContentType enum, implemented showWeb/cleanup/bookmark functions, updated UI with URL input, added sandbox network permissions, resolved interaction and transparency issues ✅ research scout system implementation from scratch (2026-03-07) — Implemented ~750-line core script with project CRUD, arXiv search, three-backend LLM support, two-stage evaluation pipeline (fast screening → deep analysis), weekly reports, Hugo deployment, caching, and comprehensive documentation 🚫 Identify Pi0.5 base model misconfiguration (2026-03-03) — Discovered LoRA fine-tuning used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base. Requires verification and potential retraining on all tasks ✅ Design and implement critical region annotation system for VLA (2026-03-08) — Designed distance-based heuristics with gripper-state filtering for 10 manipulation tasks, modified 11 files to inject critical_region labels and ee_target_distances into observations, enabled empirical threshold tuning ✅ Implement scripts/benchmark_rm_ideal.py (2026-03-03) — Created 781-line cross-sample benchmark with WWL graph kernel + Wasserstein distance evaluation framework ✅ RM-IDEAL cross-sample benchmark: 151673↔151508 bidirectional evaluation (2026-03-05) — Executed PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion evaluation ~3-4 hours per direction. Layer_1/5 showed best performance (ρ 0.42-0.66), Layer_3/6 exhibited negative correlation (ρ -0.21 to -0.36), established layer-specific performance patterns ✅ Debug Openpi-moe training normalization issue (2026-03-05) — Discovered apply_tree(strict=False) silently skipped normalization for missing prev_actions key, causing scale mismatch in trajectory VAE. Fixed by modifying compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect and compute statistics ✅ MIHD CLAUDE.md precision update and overview.md creation (2026-03-07) — Added Section 3.2 implementation details (5 subsections), corrected statistics, updated evaluation data. Created 676-line overview.md with research positioning, clinical vision narrative, 4 core questions, 3-stage roadmap, 10 fusion strategy rankings ✅ scGPT/scGPT-spatial and PCA performance systematic investigation (2026-03-07) — Retrieved papers through 4 parallel agents, demonstrated zero-shot scGPT clustering fundamentally underperforms PCA (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, 54% gap) due to cross-tissue generalization vs variance maximization objectives ✅ Diagnose and fix CalendarPro context loss bug (2026-03-06) — Identified three-layer issue: keyword pre-check executing before AI sees history, overly broad keywords, AI provider calls not receiving history parameter. Designed context-aware fix removing pre-check bypass and passing full conversation history ✅ Resolve SAPIEN rendering error in Docker container (2026-03-06) — Fixed ‘Render Error’ blocking evaluation. Downloaded matching driver (535.104.12), extracted libraries, configured VK_ICD_FILENAMES and LD_LIBRARY_PATH for Vulkan/OpenGL stack in Docker ✅ CalendarPro context-aware intent classification system (2026-03-07) — Split general_keywords into always_general and context_dependent to resolve ambiguous queries like ‘几点了’. Implemented session-aware conversation history management with auto-compression and token budget control. All 360 tests passed ✅ Create external dependencies inventory for error_recovery_benchmark (2026-03-02) — Built structured markdown inventory cataloging 14 categories: conda envs, datasets (~18.4GB), checkpoints (~47GB), configs, tools ✅ zhaoganlong Self-Reflection Framework training setup (2026-03-04) — Created 7-task implementation plan for 6-stage training pipeline (Diffusion Policy, LLaVA MPM, MCM, Lifelong Learning) across 9 tasks on 8× A800 GPUs ✅ Update error_recovery_benchmark documentation with actual implementation (2026-03-04) — Aligned project overview with codebase reality: 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, 20 error classifiers, 6,474 episodes (83% complete) ✅ Redesign error_recovery_benchmark core pipeline architecture (2026-03-04) — Updated pipeline to show three parallel error scene sources: Demo Dataset injection, VLA rollout injection, Natural failure capture ✅ MIHD project description multi-round optimization (2026-03-05) — Human-led narrative architecture with AI language optimization. Final version starts from clinical vision (patch query cross-patient knowledge transfer), emphasizes zero-shot necessity, highlights gap filling, each sentence compressed to dual short clauses ✅ CalendarPro code quality audit and critical bug fixes (2026-03-05) — Systematically audited codebase, discovered incomplete executor loop, stub get_progress(), unimplemented meal preferences, 16 dangerous silent exception handlers. Fixed all issues with proper logging and error handling. 319 tests passing after fixes ✅ Write CalendarPro comprehensive Chinese tutorial (2026-03-06) — Created 1674-line docs/TUTORIAL.md covering installation, features, CLI/Discord commands, architecture, and developer guide. 60% user-facing content, 40% developer documentation ✅ Separate Phoenix and FLARE projects with shared dependency management (2026-03-06) — Restructured into two independent projects with shared 9-package dependency directory using symlinks. Reduced disk usage from 78GB to 37MB per project (99% savings) by symlinking large data/checkpoint directories ✅ Design Phoenix/FLARE 9-task training plan (2026-03-06) — Designed complete training infrastructure for Phoenix diffusion policy and FLARE retry framework across all MimicGen tasks, including resource optimization strategy and checkpoint reuse planning 🔄 FLARE D0×4 task training launch (2026-03-07) — Running coffee_D0, stack_D0, stack_three_D0, threading_D0 Pi0.5 LoRA warmup training on an53 GPU 1,2,4,7. Fixed PYTHONPATH issue, running stably at 6.3s/step (~63 hours per task) ✅ Phoenix data loading optimization (2026-03-07) — Modified TrajectoryIndexSpeedDataset to preload all HDF5 data to memory (uint8 saves 4× storage), changed to numpy slicing, set num_workers=8 prefetch_factor=2. Resolved DataLoader bottleneck causing 5-6% GPU utilization 🔄 Monitor Pi0.5 LoRA training progress (2026-03-02) — Tracked 10 concurrent training jobs: an49 38% complete (~37h remaining), an53 27% (~46h remaining) using Slurm cluster commands 🔄 Diagnose GPU utilization bottleneck in VLA evaluation pipeline (2026-03-02) — Identified root cause: Pi0.5 action chunking (50-step outputs) reduces inference to ~8 calls per 400-step trial = 10% GPU utilization ✅ Run scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments (2026-03-02) — Evaluated 5 fusion methods × 11 sections. QFormer achieved best ARI 0.370 (vs 0.170 gene-only, +118%). Confirmed STAIG ignores gene encoder ✅ gadget summarize Windows cross-platform deployment support (2026-03-07) — Created website/update.ps1 PowerShell equivalent, added run_hugo_update() cross-platform function with automatic platform detection and fallback logic ✅ Add language configuration system to research_scout.py (2026-03-07) — Implemented full language support with _DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, _language_instruction() helper, {language_instruction} in 3 prompt templates, parameter threading through pipeline, CLI flag, config.json integration ✅ Implement author search feature for research scout (2026-03-08) — Added search_arxiv_author() function mirroring conference search pattern, updated cache layer and CLI parsers. Research tool now supports three search modes: keyword, conference, author 🔄 GPU acceleration RM-IDEAL Optimal Transport planning (2026-03-05) — Current CPU EMD calculation is bottleneck (~4000 WWL+Wasserstein per spot). Launched Plan agent to design GPU Sinkhorn approximation using GeomLoss, targeting hours-to-minutes reduction ✅ QueST cross-sample query evaluation methodology research (2026-03-04) — Extracted evaluation framework: WWL Graph Kernel ground truth, Best Match Accuracy, Overall Similarity Accuracy metrics ✅ Fix zero-embedding detection and auto-recompute logic (2026-03-03) — Discovered 151676’s STAIG fusion embeddings collapsed to zeros. Added detection + automatic retraining (300 epochs, ~50s) ✅ Multi-task BC-RNN error scenario generation (2026-03-07) — Generated 191 error scenarios using injection+natural_capture modes for 6 tasks’ BC-RNN models. Added –task parameter for automatic checkpoint/dataset/task_config parsing ✅ Implement arxiv_id deduplication and early-stop in search (2026-03-07) — Added _load_known_arxiv_ids() to scan cache, modified search_arxiv() with consecutive-known counter (stops after 5 consecutive cached papers), threaded known_ids through pipeline 🔄 Plan symlink removal for error_recovery_benchmark (2026-03-08) — Mapped 65 symlinks and created comprehensive refactoring plan to eliminate backward-compatibility technical debt with careful import rewrites ✅ Generate visualization videos for error benchmark (2026-03-08) — Created 9 MP4 videos from coffee and stack task error scenes using MuJoCo EGL rendering, covering natural failures (grasp_miss, premature_release, overshoot) and injection errors (tip_over) ✅ RM-IDEAL visualization script authoring (2026-03-05) — Wrote viz_rm_ideal_151673_151508.py generating 3-panel comparison: Ground Truth annotations, RM-IDEAL score heatmap, Embedding cosine similarity heatmap for intuitive spatial topology assessment ✅ Fix scGPT cache metadata and re-evaluate baseline (2026-03-02) — Added cache_version and metadata_json fields to 11 scGPT .npz files. ARI improved from 0.114 to 0.170 (+49%) ✅ Write docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md methodology documentation (2026-03-03) — Created 459-line Chinese documentation covering 10 chapters: spatial graphs, WWL kernel, Wasserstein distance, evaluation metrics ✅ Fix video switching bug (bookmark URL mismatch) (2026-03-03) — Added guard in ensureFileAccess to detect bookmark-resolved URL vs requested URL mismatch for NSOpenPanel cases ✅ Fix BC-RNN evaluation numpy dtype error (2026-03-03) — Modified policy_adapter to filter numpy scalar types (float64) that robomimic TensorUtils cannot handle ✅ Investigate an53 node failure (NODE_FAIL at 08:30) (2026-03-03) — Analyzed Slurm logs, determined crash caused by resource overload (8 GPUs 100% util), not AI operations (ended 3h earlier) Problems & Solutions 1. Desktop Video: 100% failure rate restoring videos/images after app restart with Code 257 ‘Operation not permitted’ [Desktop Video] (2026-03-02) Solution: Four-part fix: (1) Add com.apple.security.files.bookmarks.app-scope to entitlements (was empty dict), (2) Add fallback in restoreFromBookmark for non-scoped bookmarks, (3) Track active URLs in activeSecurityScopedURLs dict with lifecycle management, (4) ensureFileAccess() auto-resolves bookmarks before file I/O
2. BC-RNN RP(0.003) abnormally lower than Random RP(0.020), evaluation results completely unreasonable [ErrorRecovery/BC-RNN] (2026-03-07) Solution: Code review found 4 cascading bugs: demo_len=0 broke SPL, trigger_step=0 further broke SPL, transport always False capped RP at 0.5, successful episode RP not 1.0. Fixed field names, changed to read from scene.dataset, added _get_target_pos() runtime dynamic target position retrieval, added if success: rp=1.0
3. MIHD: STAIG two-stage fusion ignores gene encoder embeddings, making scGPT+UNI2+STAIG equivalent to PCA+UNI2+STAIG [MIHD] (2026-03-02) Solution: Traced through models/Fusion.py and pipeline/runner.py: staig_fusion uses staig_gene_feat (raw HVG matrix from cache) instead of gene_emb parameter. Documented as architectural limitation affecting interpretation of all STAIG results
4. Openpi-moe training didn’t error when norm_stats.json lacked prev_actions key, leading to scale mismatch in trajectory VAE (unnormalized hist_actions concatenated with normalized actions) [VLA Training] (2026-03-05) Solution: Root cause: apply_tree() uses strict=False by default, silently skipping normalization for missing keys. Fixed by modifying compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect prev_actions in batches and compute its statistics
5. CalendarPro bot fails to understand follow-up messages like ‘你安排在几点了呀’ (What time did you schedule?) due to context loss [CalendarPro] (2026-03-06) Solution: Identified three-layer issue: (1) general_keywords pre-check executes before AI sees conversation history, (2) pre-check uses overly broad keywords like ‘几点了’, (3) AI provider calls don’t receive history parameter. Designed fix to remove pre-check bypass and pass full conversation history to AI
6. SAPIEN ‘Render Error’ in headless Docker container - RuntimeError: failed to find a rendering device [RoboTwin] (2026-03-06) Solution: Container had CUDA compute libraries but not rendering stack. Downloaded matching NVIDIA driver version 535.104.12, extracted libraries to user directory, configured VK_ICD_FILENAMES and LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variables
7. Browse mode WKWebView cannot receive mouse/keyboard events; .desktopWindow layer causes window non-interactive [Desktop Video] (2026-03-07) Solution: Set window.level=.normal in enterBrowseMode (not on top but interactive), restore CGWindowLevelForKey(.desktopWindow) in exitBrowseMode. macOS .desktopWindow layer doesn’t receive input events at system level
8. VLA Evaluation: Low GPU utilization (~10%) in Pi0.5 evaluation pipeline despite 100% utilization during training [VLA Training] (2026-03-02) Solution: Root cause identified: Action chunking architecture where Pi0.5 outputs 50-step sequences reduces inference calls to only ~8 per 400-step trial (8 × 2.5s = 20s GPU work / 200s total = 10% utilization). Solution planning requested via batched evaluation implementation
9. SSH access denied on GPU nodes an49/an53 due to PAM slurm_adopt rejecting connections with ’no active jobs on this node’ [VLA Training] (2026-03-02) Solution: Bypassed SSH restriction using Slurm’s srun command with –jobid flag to execute commands directly in allocated job context: srun --jobid=45628 bash -c 'command'
10. LLaVA base model download blocked by proxy bandwidth bottleneck (~50KB/s, downloading 14GB model would take weeks); GPU node no DNS cannot direct connect [LLaVA Training] (2026-03-07) Solution: Discovered cluster had nyw’s HF format llava-1.5-7b-hf (safetensors), wrote _convert_hf_to_llava.py to convert to original LLaVA pytorch_model.bin format (including vocab truncation fix 32064→32000), completed locally without network
11. Phoenix diffusion policy training GPU utilization only 5-6%: DataLoader num_workers=0 caused GPU to wait for CPU random reads from NFS HDF5 files [Phoenix Training] (2026-03-07) Solution: Preload all HDF5 data to memory once in init (uint8 saves 4× storage), close file handles, then all getitem slices from numpy arrays. Changed DataLoader to num_workers=8, pin_memory=True. h5py file handles cannot be shared across fork child processes
12. SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial showed fixed color instead of transparent glass over video [Desktop Video] (2026-03-03) Solution: SwiftUI material uses .behindWindow blend mode (samples desktop) not .withinWindow (samples same window content). Replaced with NSViewRepresentable wrapping NSVisualEffectView with explicit .withinWindow mode and .clear backgroundColor
13. 151676’s STAIG fusion embeddings all zeros (embedding similarity all 0, Spearman r is NaN), model completely collapsed [MIHD] (2026-03-03) Solution: Added np.allclose(embeddings, 0) detection in load_fused_embeddings; automatically trigger –recompute_fusion path to reload gene/vision embeddings from cache and retrain STAIG (300 epochs, ~50s); embeddings normal after fix
14. Video switching after NSOpenPanel selection still played old video [Desktop Video] (2026-03-03) Solution: ensureFileAccess resolved per-screen bookmark to old video URL instead of requested new URL. Added guard to compare resolved vs requested URL paths; if different, return original URL (which has temporary sandbox permission from NSOpenPanel)
15. BC-RNN policy evaluation crashed with ‘Cannot handle data type <class ’numpy.float64’>’ [VLA Training] (2026-03-03) Solution: robomimic’s TensorUtils.to_tensor() cannot handle numpy scalar types. Modified _to_robosuite_obs to filter _raw_obs dict and only pass keys with array/image values to robomimic policy
16. P100 GPU (sm_60) CUDA kernel incompatible with PyTorch (requires sm_70+); torch.cuda.is_available() returns True but operations fail asynchronously [MIHD] (2026-03-03) Solution: Added CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" environment variable for forced CPU fallback logic. User switched to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75) for successful execution
17. Screensaver clock text update left 0.2s ghost of previous digits [Desktop Video] (2026-03-03) Solution: CALayer has implicit ~0.25s animation for contents property changes. Wrapped blurView.layer?.mask updates in CATransaction with setDisableActions(true) to force immediate replacement
18. LLM-generated JSON truncated (…truncated marker), causing daily_summary.py parsing failure for 2026-02-14 and other reports [gadget] (2026-03-03) Solution: Sent truncated raw text as independent prompt to AI, requesting only fix/extract valid JSON portion; AI successfully restored complete JSON structure
19. Documentation-code mismatch in error_recovery_benchmark: 项目全景总结.md described outdated pipeline, missing implementation details, incorrect statistics (726 episodes vs actual 6,474) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-04) Solution: Deployed parallel exploration agents to thoroughly read error_framework/, scripts/, configs/, outputs/ directories, then systematically updated document with verified data: detector/injector/validator/classifier implementations, actual M14 evaluation numbers, real code statistics
20. Pipeline diagram didn’t reflect user’s multi-source vision: only showed demo-based error injection, missing VLA rollout injection and natural failure capture paths [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-04) Solution: Rewrote Section 3.1 pipeline as three parallel paths (Demo注入, Policy Rollout注入, 自然失败捕获) all converging to ErrorSceneDatabase. Added explanations of each path’s purpose and complementary relationship
21. zhaoganlong data preparation scripts only had coffee_d1 task enabled (8 other tasks commented out), blocking multi-task training pipeline [zhaoganlong Framework] (2026-03-04) Solution: Systematically uncomment all 9 tasks in TASK_MAPPING dictionaries across 4 scripts, remove pdb.set_trace() debug breakpoints that would halt batch execution
22. CalendarPro intent classification ambiguity due to general_keywords pre-check interception: ‘你安排在几点了呀’ → ‘几点了’ matches general_keywords → forced GENERAL → error [CalendarPro] (2026-03-07) Solution: Split general_keywords into always_general (always force GENERAL, like ‘天气’) and context_dependent (force GENERAL only without active session, like ‘几点了’). Added confirmation_words with _is_confirmation_input() fast path. Rewrote _process_input() as 6-step flow
23. Current research scout paper evaluation processes all 50 papers at once, wasting tokens and lacking depth [gadget research scout] (2026-03-07) Solution: Designed two-stage pipeline: Stage 1 fast screening of all papers (only title+abstract first 1000 chars, classify high/low relevance), Stage 2 deep analysis of high-relevance papers (max 20 papers, extract 3 highlights + detailed scoring + direction suggestions)
24. Pipeline couldn’t read overview.md due to project.json ID (‘robot-manipulation’) not matching directory name (’error-recovery-benchmark’) [error_recovery_benchmark] (2026-03-07) Solution: Updated project.json id field to match directory name; pipeline uses project[‘id’] to construct overview.md path. Also fixed section headers to match OVERVIEW_TEMPLATE format and removed numbered prefixes
25. FLARE norm stats calculation failed: import openpi loaded zhaoganlong’s installed version, couldn’t find tangzijia’s new config name [FLARE Training] (2026-03-07) Solution: Set PYTHONPATH=/HOME/…/tangzijia/shared_deps/openpi/src:$PYTHONPATH in script to prioritize loading tangzijia’s openpi source copy, bypassing old version in conda env
26. services/init.py eager imports caused circular dependency (services ↔ core.scheduler), tests couldn’t import any service modules [CalendarPro] (2026-03-05) Solution: Changed all imports to lazy import, keeping only all list and comments, requiring users to directly import (e.g. from src.services.calendar_service import CalendarService), avoiding circular triggers during package initialization
27. 16 dangerous except Exception: pass swallow all errors, causing incomplete state but system appears to run normally [CalendarPro] (2026-03-05) Solution: Replaced with except Exception as e: logger.error/warning(f"descriptive info: {e}"), preserving error information for diagnosis, distinguishing error (critical functionality) and warning (secondary functionality)
28. All critical_region labels were 0 after data collection, indicating threshold (0.10m) was too conservative [VLA Critical Regions] (2026-03-08) Solution: Added get_ee_target_distances() method to record raw EE-to-target distances for all tasks, enabling empirical threshold tuning based on actual data distribution
29. LLM output contained too much English when user expected Chinese; no way to configure output language [gadget research scout] (2026-03-07) Solution: Implemented 3-tier language system: _DEFAULT_LANGUAGE constant, _language_instruction() generator, {language_instruction} in all prompts. Config resolution chain CLI > project.json > config.json > default
Learnings Architecture (architecture) Foundation model embeddings (scGPT 512d, UNI2 1536d) are NOT drop-in replacements for dimensionality reduction (PCA 50d) in clustering tasks. scGPT optimizes for cross-dataset transfer, not within-dataset variance. Zero-shot foundation model embedding fundamentally underperforms PCA: FM optimizes for cross-tissue generalization (pretraining objective is language modeling/contrastive learning), not variance maximization. PCA does task-aligned dimensionality reduction, maximizing variance≈maximizing cluster separability. Validated by Microsoft and 3+ papers + MIHD own data (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, 54% gap). STAIG graph-based fusion completely ignores gene encoder outputs, using raw HVG matrix instead. QFormer achieved 2× ARI improvement by learning gene-vision alignment Action chunking in VLA models fundamentally changes GPU utilization profile: Pi0.5 outputs 50-step sequences, so 400-step trials require only ~8 inference calls (~20s GPU work) vs 200s total time = ~10% GPU utilization. Bottleneck shifts from GPU compute to pipeline serialization (CPU preprocessing, WebSocket I/O, simulation steps) EventBus + BackgroundCoordinator + ContextAssembler combination is powerful pattern for building modular AI assistants: EventBus achieves service decoupling (pub-sub), BackgroundCoordinator unified management of scheduled task lifecycle (CronScheduler+conditional loading), ContextAssembler dynamically loads context layers based on intent type. Three together support 16+ independent services collaboration with proper lifecycle management requiring explicit startup calls in application hooks Context-aware intent classification needs to combine session state (session_id, last_activity_at) and conversation history, pure keyword matching causes ambiguity. Split keywords into always_general (like ‘天气’) and context_dependent (like ‘几点了’ that depends on active session). Unit tests (isolated components, mock dependencies, rapid feedback) and integration tests (real interaction, end-to-end experience) are complementary dimensions: 319 unit tests passing ≠ system usable Two-stage evaluation pipeline mimicking human researcher workflow dramatically improves efficiency and quality: Stage 1 fast screening (title+abstract, high/low relevance classification) → Stage 2 deep analysis (≤20 high-relevance papers, extract 3 highlights + detailed scoring). Avoids wasting tokens on low-value papers while generating literature reading records for future reference Multi-source data collection architectures should be explicit: Error scene generation benefits from diverse sources (demo injection for reproducibility, policy rollout injection for realism, natural failure capture for authenticity). Pipeline diagrams must show all sources as first-class citizens Symlink-based dependency sharing can reduce disk usage by 99% (78GB→37MB) for large research projects while maintaining independent project structure. Critical to symlink data directories rather than copy, use relative paths for portability. However, creates complex dependency graphs requiring comprehensive mapping before refactoring (65 symlinks × multiple import patterns = high breakage risk) Debugging (debugging) Evaluation metric distortion is often not a single bug but cascading chain of multiple bugs. BC-RNN case: demo_len=0 broke SPL, trigger_step=0 further broke SPL, transport always False capped RP at 0.5, successful episode RP not 1.0 caused baseline deviation. Need end-to-end validation not isolated unit tests. RP ceiling of 0.5 revealed transport stage judgment failure, zeros revealed field propagation errors. When diagnosing anomalous data, prioritize suspicion of code bugs over rationalizing results. User inferred evaluation problems based on normal behavior observed during training, AI tended to explain ’error recovery is inherently difficult’. User’s intuition more accurate, ultimately discovered 4 cascading bugs Training-vs-evaluation mismatch debugging checklist: When policy performs well in training but fails in evaluation, systematically check: (1) observation dictionary keys, (2) environment initialization parameters, (3) action space bounds, (4) data normalization, (5) camera rendering setup. BC-RNN 0% SR on normal tasks + 0.28% on error recovery suggests env mismatch, not policy failure Data preprocessing pipelines need explicit validation that all expected features have normalization statistics. Silent skipping (strict=False) can propagate scale mismatches through entire training runs without errors. apply_tree’s lenient behavior allows scale mismatches in concatenated features (unnormalized prev_actions + normalized actions). Always validate expected keys have norm stats, especially for concatenated features Context loss requires tracing complete call chain: data collection (conversation_history.json storage) → data passing (provider call signatures) → data usage (prompt construction). Missing any layer breaks context. Effective code audits require multi-dimensional analysis: syntactic (TODO/FIXME/NotImplementedError), behavioral (exception handling patterns, empty loops, stub methods), structural (missing test coverage, unused exports), operational (service registration, startup hooks) Dangerous silent exception handling (except Exception: pass) makes system appear to run normally but state incomplete. situation_monitor’s energy/meals/exercise/thoughts/reminders could all be empty (Schrödinger’s state). Should always log exceptions (logger.error/warning), distinguish critical functionality (error) and secondary functionality (warning), preserve diagnostic information. Python circular import solution: package-level lazy import (only all), module-level function-internal import, test patch source module not call site Log archaeology (reading full log files) can reveal patterns invisible in code: Desktop Video’s ‘saved non-scoped bookmark’ repeated 20× across sessions exposed missing entitlements. Concurrent write bugs (errorLog FileHandle race) silently destroy diagnostic information. High-frequency timer logs (879 updateClockLabels) obscure real errors → need state-based logging, not tick-based Documentation synchronization pattern: Use parallel exploration agents (one per major directory), verify all numbers from actual files (summary.json, filesystem stats), then systematically update document section by section. Don’t trust document claims - always check code/data ground truth. Large-scale refactoring should incrementally validate critical integration points (service startup, event wiring, data persistence) rather than implementing all features first then testing Tools (tools) macOS window layer controls not only visual Z-order but event routing permissions: .desktopWindow layer (below desktop icons) fundamentally doesn’t receive user input events at system level, must use .normal or higher layer to implement interactive ‘browse mode’. This is system-level constraint not API configuration issue. ignoresMouseEvents=false ineffective for .desktopWindow layer. .normal(level=0) is lowest interactive layer that doesn’t cover other apps macOS sandbox security-scoped bookmarks require both compile-time entitlement declaration (in .entitlements file) AND runtime lifecycle management (startAccessingSecurityScopedResource/stop). Empty entitlements → silent fallback to non-scoped bookmarks → 100% restore failure. Bookmark system has no visible error channel when entitlements are missing NSVisualEffectView supports two blending modes: .behindWindow (samples desktop/wallpaper) vs .withinWindow (samples same window’s content); SwiftUI .material uses .behindWindow and cannot sample video content. CALayer properties have implicit ~0.25s animations by default; use CATransaction.setDisableActions(true) for instant updates In GPU cluster environment, prioritize searching existing model copies and performing format conversion rather than downloading through proxy. LLaVA base model conversion from HF format to original format (including vocab truncation fix 32064→32000) saved weeks. HF model format conversion isn’t just renaming keys, involves semantic differences like vocab size. Container had CUDA compute libraries but not rendering stack - Docker containers with NVIDIA GPUs need both CUDA runtime AND Vulkan/OpenGL libraries for graphics-based simulation, driver version must exactly match kernel module h5py file handles cannot be shared across fork child processes (known PyTorch DataLoader limitation). Only safe solution is main process preload to numpy then fork. NFS random reads far slower than sequential reads, one-time loading 10× faster than on-demand. Preload all HDF5 to memory (uint8 saves 4× storage), close file handles, slice from numpy arrays in getitem. Change DataLoader to num_workers=8, pin_memory=True. Resolved 5-6% GPU utilization bottleneck to 80%+ Slurm’s srun with –jobid flag can bypass SSH PAM restrictions (like slurm_adopt) by executing commands directly in allocated job context. When GPU nodes run 100% utilization workloads, administrative commands via srun experience significant latency (60+ seconds) → design monitoring scripts to handle timeouts gracefully with lightweight fallback commands (nvidia-smi over nvitop) PyTorch CUDA compatibility trap: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks if CUDA driver/library exist, not GPU compute capability compatibility (e.g., P100 sm_60 vs PyTorch requires sm_70+). CUDA operations fail asynchronously, Python try/except cannot catch errors. Most reliable fallback: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" environment variable or switch compatible GPU Language configuration for LLM pipelines requires injection at prompt level, not post-processing. Different parts need different rules (JSON keys stay English, content in target language). Config resolution chain: CLI flag > project.json > config.json > hardcoded default. Parallel agent execution dramatically accelerates independent module development. Four background agents completed complex enhancements concurrently (context_detector, checkin_service, learning_service, energy_projection) while main thread worked on coupled modules. Wall-clock time ~140s vs sequential ~560s (4x speedup) Multi-user shared conda environment import priority conflict minimal invasive solution: set PYTHONPATH=/path/to/user/src:$PYTHONPATH at script execution, prioritize user’s own source copy, doesn’t affect other users. Cross-platform script porting: Windows use Get-Item .LastWriteTime instead of find -newer, Start-Job instead of & parallel, PowerShell and bash differ in flow control and error handling. WKWebView transparency: drawsBackground=false makes entire rendering layer transparent, CSS on transparent layer still transparent, fix must be at rendering layer not CSS. App Sandbox blocks all outbound network by default When designing ML data collection heuristics, always implement observability (log raw features) before committing to threshold-based labels. Empirical tuning requires actual data distribution visibility. arXiv API supports author queries via ‘au:“Author Name”’ syntax with AND/OR operators, uses relevance-based sorting for author searches. research_scout.py supports three search modes with different caching strategies: keyword/category (date-sorted, lookback-filtered), conference (relevance-sorted, comment-filtered), author (relevance-sorted, no post-filter). Consecutive-known-paper counter effective heuristic for early-stop Domain Knowledge (domain) RM-IDEAL cross-sample evaluation complete workflow: (1) load string labels aligned by cache barcode, (2) load fusion embeddings from Phase 2 outputs or recompute, (3) compute RM-IDEAL scores using WWL graph kernel + Wasserstein distance, (4) compute embedding cosine similarity (niche centroid vs all spots), (5) evaluate correlation with Spearman r / Precision@K / Same-label rate, (6) generate spatial heatmap visualization. WWL kernel: K rounds of WL message passing, shared hash tables for label encoding, POT library ot.emd2() solves Wasserstein distance (10x speedup) RM-IDEAL Spearman correlation exhibits significant directional asymmetry: 151508→151673 Layer_4 ρ=0.657, but reverse only 0.275. Reveals fusion embedding space quality differences in structural representation of same niche label across different samples, possibly related to spot density, noise level or annotation quality. Middle layers (Layer_3/6) show negative correlation (ρ -0.21 to -0.36) possibly confused with adjacent layers in embedding space, causing embedding-based similarity ranking opposite to topology-based RM-IDEAL ranking. Boundary/central layers easier to distinguish than middle transition layers. Cross-sample niche query evaluation metric interpretation: Spearman r≈0.44 (p<1e-150) indicates moderate positive correlation between fusion embedding cosine similarity and RM-IDEAL topology scores. P@K close to 0 indicates large head differences (embedding similarity and topology measure different dimensions). Low same-label rate suggests embedding spaces across sections not fully aligned QueST evaluation framework: when no ground truth niche labels exist, construct synthetic ground truth using Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman Graph Kernel over cell type annotations, then validate model’s similarity rankings with Pearson correlation. Metrics: Best Match Accuracy (top-1 correctness) and Overall Similarity Accuracy (correlation with WWL ground truth) VLA systematic failures at task-critical phases (imprecise pressing, failed grasping/placing) can be addressed by training binary classifier to predict ‘critical regions’ using distance-based heuristics with gripper-state filtering. Enables cls token classifier training for high-stakes regions requiring extra caution. Must verify actual dataset size, per-step time and total steps before launching large-scale training to avoid resource waste. Phoenix MPM case: dataset scale estimation error (160K→1M) caused 13.5-day training vs expected hours Multi-task ML pipelines built via incremental prototyping (start with 1 task) leave scattered single-task configurations (commented-out task lists, hardcoded paths). Scaling to production requires systematic audit of: data prep scripts, dataset mapping JSONs, checkpoint directories, and evaluation configs Project description narrative strategy: external communication should start from application scenario target audience cares about (clinical patch query knowledge transfer), then explain technical path and key differentiators (zero-shot vs end-to-end training), finally elevate to domain value. Technical details serve narrative logic, not vice versa. Control each sentence within dual short clauses to reduce cognitive load. In spatial transcriptomics, adata.X can be either normalized values (scanpy pipeline) or raw counts (STHD pipeline) - must check code rather than assume from data type Checkpoint inventory best practices: Record full path, size, last modified date, and usability status for all model checkpoints. Large checkpoint directories (180GB) indicate many saved steps - document which step to use for evaluation. Date-based naming helps track training timeline but needs explicit step-to-date mapping AI Usage Notes Effective Patterns:
✓ Parallel exploration agents for large codebase analysis: one agent per major directory, then consolidate findings for documentation synchronization ✓ Parallel multi-agent exploration for rapid codebase understanding: launched 3-4 independent agents to explore conversation history management, intent classification, and AI provider call chains simultaneously ✓ Wave-based parallel execution for large-scale refactoring: launched 4 independent background agents (context_detector, checkin_service, learning_service, energy_projection) while main thread worked on coupled modules, achieving 4x speedup ✓ Systematic debugging via log archaeology: reading full 4438-line log files revealed ‘saved non-scoped bookmark’ pattern exposing missing entitlements ✓ Systematic code tracing through multiple layers: traced data flow through 3+ files (data_loader.py → transforms.py → pi0_moe.py) to find root cause in utility function 4 layers deep ✓ Multi-dimensional code auditing: used specialized exploration agents for TODO/FIXME search, git analysis, exception auditing, test gap detection to discover hidden bugs systematically ✓ Two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline: fast screening (title+abstract, relevance classification) → deep analysis (≤20 papers, extract highlights) mimicking human researcher workflow ✓ Web-based research for paper methodology extraction: arxiv HTML versions more machine-readable than PDFs ✓ Filesystem-based verification: using du -sh, wc -l, stat to verify actual statistics vs documentation claims ✓ Progressive problem isolation: from symptom (0% SR) → hypothesis (env mismatch) → verification checklist (obs keys, action bounds, normalization) ✓ Background task execution with filesystem monitoring: used run_in_background for hour-level computation, monitored progress through cache file timestamps and completed sub-task counts rather than stdout ✓ Iterative empirical validation: logged raw features (ee_target_distances) before committing to threshold-based labels, enabling data-driven tuning ✓ Cross-platform deployment with automatic detection: run_hugo_update() auto-selects Windows PowerShell vs Unix bash with fallback logic Limitations:
✗ Failed to question task assumptions: didn’t verify whether STAIG fusion uses gene encoder before running expensive experiments; user’s casual observation led to architectural insight discovery ✗ Lack of proactivity: didn’t propose visualization or methodology documentation until explicitly requested; didn’t learn user’s execution style preference after multiple ExitPlanMode rejections ✗ Cannot infer system-level architecture from individual script capabilities: missed multi-source pipeline design (demo injection, policy rollout injection, natural capture) until user explicitly corrected ✗ AI didn’t verify actual dataset size, per-step time and total steps before Phoenix training launch, causing severe training duration estimation error (13.5 days vs expected hours), lacking upfront validation awareness of resource consumption ✗ When diagnosing BC-RNN RP anomaly, AI initially inclined to rationalize anomalous data (’error recovery inherently difficult’) rather than prioritizing suspicion of evaluation code bugs. Only after user insisted did AI systematically audit code and discover 4 cascading bugs ✗ Insufficient understanding of OS window layer system: initially thought removing .floating would maintain desktop layer, didn’t realize .desktopWindow fundamentally cannot receive input events, causing browse mode completely unusable ✗ When designing paper evaluation workflow, AI didn’t spontaneously adopt human researcher’s two-stage reading strategy (fast screening→deep analysis), instead inclined toward one-time full processing engineering approach ✗ AI chose critical region threshold (0.10m) based on code patterns without empirical validation, resulting in all labels being 0. Required iterative correction to add distance logging for proper threshold tuning ✗ Agent timeouts on large codebase exploration: three parallel agents timed out (>1100 seconds) when exploring zhaoganlong framework, returning 0 tokens ✗ Didn’t prioritize high-probability failure modes in BC-RNN debugging: started broad exploration instead of checking most likely culprits (observation key mismatch mentioned earlier by user) ✗ Cannot directly parse PDF binary content from WebFetch tool; heavily reliant on HTML versions for arxiv papers ✗ Failed to recognize repetitive batch processing pattern: processed 13 historical dates one by one without suggesting automation or progress recovery mechanism ✗ When batch generating reports, AI repeatedly generated incomplete or syntax-error JSON (quote escaping errors, missing closing brackets), requiring manual intervention to repair. Insufficient reliability in generating ultra-long structured output. Truncated JSON generation in multi-segment merge: LLM-generated ultra-long JSON had truncation risk; needed manual repair prompt instead of automatic detection and retry ✗ AI didn’t proactively identify language configuration gap until user explicitly complained about English output; should have noticed Chinese comments in codebase and suggested bilingual support earlier ✗ Over-triggered plan mode: user rejected entering plan mode multiple times in Claude Code guide authoring task, showing AI failed to self-adapt to user’s preference for direct execution work style ✗ AI created complete Phase 1-5 plan (31 sub-goals) but implementation only completed Phase 1-3. Phase 4’s 10+ file modifications and Discord Bot intent integration were not completed. User needed to explicitly inquire about progress to expose this gap ✗ Failed to verify critical integration point during implementation: BackgroundCoordinator never started in setup_hook() or main.py, meaning all 7 new background services never actually run. Wasn’t discovered until post-implementation audit phase ✗ AI repeatedly tried different patch paths when fixing test fixtures, didn’t understand correct patch strategy after lazy import (must patch source module) in one go, causing multiple edit-test loops Next Week Outlook Priority 1: Resolve BC-RNN 0% success rate anomaly (if persists) and verify Pi0.5 base model misconfiguration (pi0.5_libero vs pi0.5_base) - critical for baseline validation before further LoRA fine-tuning. Priority 2: Monitor and complete FLARE D0×4 training (~63h/task, should finish by week end), then replan and launch Phoenix/LLaVA training with corrected dataset scale and resource estimates to prevent 13.5-day miscalculation recurrence. Priority 3: Continue monitoring Pi0.5 LoRA training progress (currently 38-72% complete, estimated 17-46h remaining per task) and prepare evaluation pipeline for completed checkpoints. Priority 4: Complete GPU acceleration for RM-IDEAL Optimal Transport to reduce hour-level computation to minutes via Sinkhorn approximation using GeomLoss. Priority 5: Implement GPU utilization optimization for VLA evaluation pipeline using batched inference with timer-based request collection to address 10% utilization bottleneck caused by action chunking. Priority 6: Execute symlink removal refactoring for error_recovery_benchmark (65 symlinks mapped, implementation plan ready) to eliminate backward-compatibility technical debt. Priority 7: Complete Desktop Video browse mode by finalizing .normal window level implementation to enable interactive webpage wallpaper functionality. Priority 8: Continue VLA critical region annotation data collection with empirically-tuned thresholds based on logged ee_target_distances to enable high-stakes region classifier training. Priority 9: Execute zhaoganlong 6-stage training pipeline (Stage 0-5: data prep, Diffusion Policy, LLaVA MPM, MCM feedback, LLaVA MCM, Lifelong Learning) across 9 MimicGen tasks on 8× A800 GPUs - multi-week campaign requiring tmux session management and checkpoint monitoring. Priority 10: Begin CalendarPro deployment testing with real Discord bot interaction following 360 passing unit tests. Priority 11: Expand research scout with additional search capabilities and evaluation refinements based on first week of production usage. Add batch processing mode to gadget/summarize tool with date-range parameter, progress recovery, and truncation detection/auto-repair for future historical data processing.
Token Usage Statistics Total: $266.78 — 387.3M tokens
Peak Day: 2026-03-07 — $90.64 / 109.1M tokens
Daily Average: $44.46
summary: Intensive multi-project research and development week spanning spatial transcriptomics evaluation infrastructure, robotics training pipelines, personal automation systems, research tooling, and application development. Major breakthroughs: resolved critical BC-RNN evaluation reliability crisis (fixed 4 cascading bugs making metrics trustworthy), completed MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark revealing layer-specific performance patterns and STAIG architectural limitations, achieved Desktop Video production-grade stability (eliminated 100% bookmark restoration failures + implemented webpage wallpaper functionality), optimized Phoenix GPU utilization 5%→80%+ via HDF5 preloading, and built CalendarPro autonomous butler system with context-aware intent classification (360 tests passing). Established large-scale training infrastructure monitoring 10+ concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA jobs while launching FLARE D0×4 training. Developed research scout tool from scratch with bilingual support and two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline. Systematically addressed technical debt through comprehensive code audits, documentation synchronization, and testing.
title: Weekly Summary 2026-W10

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-08 Daily Overview Completed Work: Expanded the research paper discovery tool, added author-based search functionality, generated visual charts for error-based benchmarks, designed and implemented a critical region annotation method for VLA training, covering 10 manipulation tasks, fixed errors in RoboTwin rendering, and planned to remove and refactor symbolic links. Completion Methods: Implemented a new arXiv search function with caching support, used MuJoCo EGL rendering for batch video generation, designed a distance-based heuristic algorithm, considered the influence of gripper states on critical regions, configured the Vulkan/EGL environment for headless GPU rendering, and mapped the dependencies of 65 symbolic links. Impact: The research tool now supports three search modes (keywords, conferences, authors), created 9 auditable benchmark videos, introduced a new VLA training method to address systematic failure modes, resolved issues with RoboTwin’s data collection pipeline, and improved the maintainability of error_recovery_benchmark. TzJsDesktop Completed Work: Implemented author search functionality for research_scout.py and updated the project documentation to reflect the current state. Completion Methods: Added the search_arxiv_author() function, mimicked conference search mode, updated the cache layer and CLI parser, corrected line count statistics and missing functional documentation in CLAUDE.md. Impact: Users can now search for papers by specific researchers, and the documentation accurately reflects all functions of the 2024 code base. tianhe Completed Work: Generated visual charts for error-based benchmarks, designed and implemented the VLA critical region annotation system, fixed RoboTwin’s rendering infrastructure, and planned to remove and refactor symbolic links. Completion Methods: Batch-generated MP4 files from existing.npz scene data, implemented critical region annotation for 11 files, designed a distance-based heuristic algorithm, added Vulkan/EGL environment configuration, and fully mapped the dependencies of 65 symbolic links. Impact: Created 9 auditable videos for different types of errors, provided a complete critical region annotation system with empirical tuning capabilities, resolved blocking rendering errors, and developed an actionable refactoring plan. Expanded the research tool with author search functionality, updated documentation, generated visual charts for error-based benchmarks, designed and implemented the critical region annotation system for VLA training, and fixed RoboTwin’s rendering infrastructure
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Design a critical region annotation method for VLA — Designed a distance-based heuristic algorithm, considering the influence of gripper states on critical regions, to annotate critical regions for 10 RoboTwin manipulation tasks. ✅ Implement the critical region annotation system — Modified base_task.py and 10 task files, injecting the critical_region label and ee_target_distances into the observation data. ✅ Implement author search functionality (–author flag) — Added the search_arxiv_author() function, updated the cache function to support author parameters, modified cmd_search() and cmd_report() functions to adapt to author search mode, and added CLI parameters to search and report sub-parsers. 🔄 Plan to remove symbolic links from error_recovery_benchmark — Mapped 65 symbolic links and developed a comprehensive refactoring plan to eliminate backward-compatibility technical debt. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Generate visual videos for error-based benchmarks — Created 9 MP4 videos from error scenarios in coffee and stacking tasks, covering natural failures (grasp_miss, premature_release, overshoot) and injected errors (tip_over). ✅ Update the research CLAUDE.md document — Corrected line count statistics (from approximately 1870 lines to approximately 2000 lines), included the –language flag, added an early stop feature for deduplication (known IDs + 5 consecutive known IDs), and updated chapter numbering. ✅ Fix RoboTwin rendering device errors — Added Vulkan/EGL environment variables in collect_data.sh to enable headless GPU rendering. ✅ Locate the episode_num configuration — Identified the episode_num field that controls the number of data collections in the task_config YAML file. ✅ Review the CLAUDE.md document — Confirmed that the existing CLAUDE.md meets production quality standards and does not require modifications. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. The VLA model always fails at certain task stages (incorrect pressing in click tasks, failure to grasp/place in manipulation tasks) Solution: Designed a distance-based heuristic algorithm, considering the influence of gripper states on critical regions, to annotate binary “critical regions” during data collection, thereby supporting the training of the cls token classifier. Key Insight: By training a binary classifier, it is possible to predict areas where agents need special attention, thus addressing systematic VLA failures at critical task stages.
2. All critical_region labels become 0 after data collection, indicating that the threshold (0.10 meters) is too conservative Solution: Added the get_ee_target_distances() method, recording the original EE-to-target distances for all tasks, allowing for empirical threshold adjustment. Key Insight: When designing machine learning data annotation heuristics, it is important to record the original features before determining the label thresholds.
General Problems 3. The SAPIEN renderer encounters “RuntimeError: failed to find a rendering device” errors in headless environments Solution: Added three Vulkan/EGL environment variables (VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, LD_LIBRARY_PATH) to collect_data.sh, pointing to the nvidia_libs directory. Key Insight: Headless GPU servers require explicit Vulkan ICD and EGL vendor configurations to find NVIDIA rendering drivers, even if nvidia-smi works properly.
4. When running visualization scripts from a remote shell, the SSH command returns “No such file or directory” errors Solution: Changed the relative paths of the project root directory and Python scripts to absolute paths, ensuring that the working directory is correctly set before execution. Key Insight: SSH does not inherit the working directory; you must use “cd /absolute_path&&command” or provide the absolute paths of all files.
Human-AI Methodology Strategic Level Selection of critical region annotation thresholds Role Method Human The user found that the 0.10 meter threshold was too small after collecting data, requesting records of distances to adjust the threshold based on actual data distribution. AI The AI selected the 0.10 meter threshold based on the existing pre_grasp_dis code pattern, without empirical verification Difference Analysis: Humans adopt an iterative empirical approach, making decisions based on actual data; AI makes theoretical choices based on code patterns. Humans correctly identified the need for observable data before finalizing heuristic parameters (recording distances).
Implementation plan for author search functionality | Role | Method | |——|——| | Human | Provided a detailed implementation plan, including specific line numbers, function signatures, mutual exclusion checks, and validation commands.| | AI | Initially wanted to explore the code base first, then posed clarifying questions through AskUserQuestion, and then launched the Plan agent to design the functionality |
Difference Analysis: Humans prefer a top-down approach and develop detailed specifications; AI prefers a bottom-up exploratory approach, using problem-driven methods for discovery.
Scope of symbolic link removal and refactoring Role Method Human The user requested the removal of all 65 symbolic links to eliminate technical debt and focus on achieving a clear structure. AI AI fully mapped the dependencies of symbolic links and developed a detailed refactoring plan, minimizing disruption risks by carefully rewriting import statements Difference Analysis: Humans focus on strategic goals (eliminating technical debt); AI focuses on tactical execution (safe migration paths). Both are necessary—humans set the direction, and AI ensures safe implementation.
Limitations of AI Key Limitations AI initially selected the critical region threshold (0.10 meters) based on code patterns, but without empirical verification, resulting in all labels being 0. It requires iterative correction, adding distance records to adjust the threshold based on actual data distribution. General Limitations AI needs to use sub-agents multiple times to fully map the dependencies of symbolic links (65 symbolic links distributed across the root directory, script directory, and nested output directories, with complex import patterns). Failed to correctly handle SSH working directory context, requiring multiple attempts to build commands with correct absolute paths. Lessons Learned Key Lessons For VLA training in manipulation tasks, distance-based heuristics and filtering of grasping states can be used to systematically handle failure modes at critical task stages (pressing, grasping, placing). The arXiv API supports author queries using the “au:” syntax, which can be combined with keyword filters using AND/OR operators, and relevance sorting can be used for author searches. When designing machine learning data collection heuristics, observability should be achieved before using threshold-based labels (record original features). Empirical adjustments require visibility into the actual data distribution. research_scout.py now supports three different search modes with different caching strategies: keywords/categories (sorted by date, with backoff time), conferences (sorted by relevance, with comments filtered), authors (sorted by relevance, without post-processing filtering). Backward-compatibility with symbolic links creates complex dependency graphs, requiring a comprehensive mapping before refactoring (65 symbolic links × multiple import patterns = high risk of disruption if not carefully planned). Practical Lessons MuJoCo visualizations can be generated from pre-saved.npz scene data without rerunning the entire strategy, enabling efficient post-production video generation. SAPIEN’s headless GPU rendering requires three Vulkan/EGL environment variables: VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH, pointing to the NVIDIA driver library directory. Dialogue Summary gadget/research ✅ Implemented author search functionality and updated documentation 03:25:22.536 | claude_code The user provided a detailed implementation plan to add the –author flag, allowing for searching for papers by specific researchers. The assistant implemented the search_arxiv_author() function, updated the cache layer to support author parameters, created a dedicated cache file, modified the search and reporting commands to handle author mode, and performed mutual exclusion checks to prevent conflicts with –conference. Additionally, the three previously implemented functions (initializing –from-overview, renaming from weekly to daily, searching for deduplication) were verified, and the CLAUDE.md was updated, correcting line counts (from 2024 to 1870), adding the –language flag documentation, documenting early stop deduplication functionality, and fixing chapter numbering. All changes were checked for grammar accuracy.**
error_recovery_benchmark ✅ Generated visual effects and reviewed CLAUDE.md 01:15:43.352 | claude_code The user asked if rendering visualizations could be provided. The assistant found the existing visualizations from the old pick_place task, but none in the current 6 task runs. It was discovered that there was scene data available (150-743 scenes per task), which could be rendered immediately. After resolving the SSH working directory issue, 9 MP4 videos were successfully generated, covering different error types in coffee and stacking tasks, using MuJoCo EGL rendering on GPU6. The existing CLAUDE.md was also reviewed, confirming its quality for production use without modification. Subsequently, the user requested the removal of all 65 backward-compatible symbolic links; AI mapped the dependencies and developed a comprehensive refactoring plan.**
RoboTwin VLA ✅ Design and implementation of critical region annotation 04:18:09.279 | claude_code The user pointed out systematic failures in VLA at critical task stages (pressing in click tasks, grasping/placing in manipulation tasks). They requested heuristic annotation of “critical regions” to train the cls token binary classifier. AI thoroughly explored the RoboTwin process, analyzed 10 task environments, designed a distance-based heuristic algorithm, and incorporated gripper state filtering. The entire system was implemented in 11 files: modified the base_task.py file, added helper methods (_ee_dist, get_critical_region_label, get_ee_target_distances), injected the critical_region and ee_target_distances into get_obs(), and replaced all methods in the 10 task files with task-specific heuristics. After collecting data, all labels became 0; AI added distance records for empirical threshold adjustment. Additionally, the collect_data.sh rendering errors were fixed, and VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH were exported, and the episode_num configuration was located in task_config/*.yml files.**
summary: 扩展的研究工具，包括作者搜索功能以及更新的文档资料；能够生成错误情况的可视化展示；设计并实现了用于VLA训练的关键区域标注系统；同时还修复了RoboTwin的渲染基础设施问题。
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-08

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-07 Daily Overview Completed Work: Parallel development in the MIHD, ErrorRecovery, DesktopVideo, gadget, and CalendarPro projects. Technical documentation was written, training processes were initiated and fixed, applications were developed, and research infrastructure was built. How It Was Done: Documentation was generated through parallel proxy code libraries, multi-GPU training tasks were started, errors in evaluation metrics were fixed, a browsing mode function for WKWebView web wallpapers was implemented, historical reports were generated in bulk, a context-based intent classification system was built, and a research paper management system with LLM enhancements was constructed from scratch. Impact: Complete MIHD technical specifications and research positioning were established. It was proven that zero-shot scGPT performs significantly worse than PCA. Reliable FLARE/BC-RNN training and evaluation processes were achieved. A complete Desktop Video solution was provided for any web wallpaper. The work in February was completed in over 20 days. The intent classification accuracy of CalendarPro was significantly improved. An automated paper discovery and evaluation infrastructure with bilingual support and efficient search functions was established. DCC Completed Work: Accurate update of MIHD CLAUDE.md was completed. Research positioning documentation overview.md was created. scGPT/scGPT-space performance was systematically studied. Cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark tests were performed. How It Was Done: Parallel proxy code libraries were used to extract APIs and statistics for incremental document patches. Four parallel proxies were started to retrieve arXiv papers and benchmark data. Python benchmark test scripts were used to analyze Spearman correlations. Impact: Project documentation evolved from descriptive outlines to precise technical specifications. A differential narrative comparing zero-shot and STAIG based on clinical perspectives was established. It was proven that zero-shot scGPT clustering performs significantly worse than PCA (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, a difference of 54%). MacBook Completed Work: Complete WKWebView web wallpaper functionality for Desktop Video was implemented (for YouTube/Bilibili, etc.). Interaction issues during browsing mode, scroll bar transparency issues, and volume synchronization errors were fixed. Automatic Hugo deployment for Windows/UNIX platforms was implemented for the gadget summary tool. Some historical reports were generated in bulk. How It Was Done: TheContentType enumeration type was expanded to include the.web type. WKWebView was used to control playback. Sandbox network permissions and JS injection techniques were utilized. The problem of interacting with the window_level=.normal layer was resolved by removing drawsBackground. A update.ps1 PowerShell script and run_hugo_update() cross-platform detection function were created. Impact: Desktop Video achieved a complete solution for playing any web video as a desktop wallpaper (solving sandbox network blocking, non-interactive window layers, and scroll bar transparency issues). The automatic deployment of the gadget tool worked properly on both Windows and UNIX platforms. TzJsDesktop Completed Work: Gadget report tools were processed in bulk, covering more than 20 dates from early February to early March. Tests were conducted on multiple devices. A context-based intent classification system for CalendarPro was implemented (passed 360 tests). A research paper management system was developed from scratch (approximately 750 lines of core script + two-stage evaluation process), and then production-level optimization was carried out (language configuration, reverse initialization, deduplication, error fixing). How It Was Done: daily_summary.py export and merge commands were executed repeatedly, processing different dates. Context-based keyword classification and conversation-aware dialogue history management were implemented. arXiv search was performed using the summarize/LLM backend pattern. A two-stage LLM evaluation process was implemented. Hugo Extended was installed using winget. research_scout.py was optimized using an LLM-driven approach, including language configuration for three-layer prompts, deduplication based on arxiv_id, and early termination logic. Issues related to project ID/directory name mismatches were also fixed. Impact: Comprehensive work from February to early March was archived, ensuring historical traceability. CalendarPro completely solved the problem of inaccurate intent classification caused by keyword pre-checking (such as “What time is it?”). An automated research paper discovery and evaluation infrastructure was established for 3-5 active projects, supporting bilingual output, efficient search, and reverse project initialization functions. tianhe Completed Work: FLARE D0×4 training was started (stable runtime: 6.3 seconds/step). Errors in four BC-RNN evaluation metrics were fixed (demo_len/trigger_step/transport/RP). Testing of 191 multi-task error scenarios was completed. Parallel training of LLaVA MPM/MCM began. The loading efficiency of Phoenix data was optimized (from 5% to over 80%). The bottleneck problem in downloading LLaVA base models was solved through offline format conversion. How It Was Done: PYTHONPATH was used to isolate and solve openpi configuration conflicts. Distortion issues in BC-RNN metrics were fixed through code review. get_target_pos() function was added to dynamically retrieve target positions during runtime. Six tasks shared generation scripts were implemented through task_registry dynamic routing. LLaVA base models were obtained through offline safetensors→pytorch_model.bin format conversion. Training bottlenecks were solved through ZeRO-3→ZeRO-2+HDF5 preloading. Impact: FLARE 4 D0 tasks ran smoothly on an53 GPUs (each task took approximately 63 hours). BC-RNN evaluation metrics were reliable (all 127 unit tests passed, GPU verification showed RP up to 0.800). The 191 error scenarios for six tasks laid the foundation for subsequent training. The training time for LLaVA MPM/MCM was reduced from estimated 13.5 days to about 2 days. However, there were errors in estimating the dataset size, requiring re-planning. The optimization plan for Phoenix GPU utilization was ready. Parallel Execution on Four Devices: MIHD documentation system and scGPT research were completed. FLARE/Phoenix training was started, and four BC-RNN evaluation errors were fixed. Desktop Video web wallpapers with WKWebView were implemented. Historical reports for more than 20 days in February were generated in bulk. CalendarPro context-based intent classification was completed. A research paper management system with language configuration, reverse initialization, and deduplication functions was constructed from scratch. Tasks Architecture and Strategy - ✅ Fixed Errors in BC-RNN Evaluation Process 4 — Corrected the field name error in collector.py from demo_length to demo_len. Fixed the logic for reading data from scene_dataset in trigger_step. Fixed the detection logic in the transport stage (added get_target_pos() function for dynamic target retrieval). Added if success: rp=1.0 condition in collector.py. All 127 unit tests passed. GPU verification confirmed demo_len=605, trigger_step=55, and RP up to 0.800. ✅ Complete Implementation of Desktop Video WKWebView Web Wallpaper — Created WebNavigationHandler.swift file. Expanded theContentType enumeration type. Implemented showWeb/cleanupWebView/saveWebBookmark functions. Updated AppDelegate playback state management. Updated UI design of SingleScreenView (URL input field). Updated history, thumbnail, localization settings, and ChangeLog. Added sandbox network permissions. Set customUserAgent to modern Chrome user agent. ✅ Develop Research Scout System from Scratch — Developed research/research_scout.py script from scratch (approximately 750 lines of core code). Included project CRUD operations, arXiv search (using arxiv package), support for three LLM backends (anthropic/openai/claude_cli), paper evaluation scoring, direction suggestion generation, weekly report output (JSON+Markdown format), Hugo deployment, and caching mechanism. Two-stage paper evaluation process was designed: first stage for quick screening of all papers (title+abstract, determining relevance); second stage for in-depth analysis of highly relevant papers (no more than 20 papers, including 3 highlights: insights/ motivations/ experiments/limitations). Requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md, and TUTORIAL.md Chinese tutorials were written. ✅ Accuracy Update of MIHD CLAUDE.md — Added implementation details for section 3.2 (including detector/injector/verifier/classifier/coordinator, five subsections). Corrected VLA model type (Pi0-FAST→Phoenix/Flare). Updated code statistics (number of scripts increased from 18 to 22, number of test cases increased from 109 to 122). Updated M14 actual evaluation data (726 cases became 6474 cases). Added distribution information for database stage/severity. Process configuration structure was explained in detail. Cache directory structure was described in detail. ✅ Create MIHD Research Positioning Document — Written 676 lines of overview.md document, including a one-sentence positioning description (systematic evaluation of zero-shot embedding of basic models in space-temporal transcriptomics multi-modal fusion clustering), 4 core questions, 3-phase technology roadmap, ranking of 8 fusion strategies, 8 open questions, search keywords, literature note template, project structure quick reference, quick start command. ✅ Systematic Study of Performance of scGPT/scGPT-spatial and PCA — Retrieved scGPT-spatial papers, zero-shot limit papers, BioLLM framework papers, and internal results obtained by MIHD through four parallel proxies. Summary analysis showed that zero-shot scGPT clustering performed significantly worse than PCA (PCA ARI=0.261 vs scGPT=0.170, a difference of 54%). 🔄 Implementation of Desktop Video Browsing Mode and Window Layer Fixes — Implemented browsing mode framework (WallpaperWindow dynamically can BecomeKey, ESC listening, exit via control panel buttons). The window layer was adjusted several times, and it was found that the.documentsWindow layer could not receive any mouse/keyboard events. It was confirmed that the.normal layer was needed, but it was not fully implemented yet. ✅ Implement CalendarPro Context-Aware Intent Classification System — Implemented context-aware intent classification system (divided general_keywords into always_general and context_dependent to avoid incorrect classification due to vague queries like “What time is it?”). Implemented conversation-aware dialogue history management (automatic compression, session reset, token budget control). Completed A/B/C/D four-phase plan, added 41 special tests, and all 360 tests passed. ✅ Fixed Errors in Recovery Benchmark Project Configuration — Fixed three key errors: ID and directory name mismatch in project.json, chapter titles in overview.md not conforming to process regular expression patterns, and lack of automatic append markers. 🔄 Launch of FLARE D0×4 Task Training — Ran coffee_D0, stack_D0, stack_three_D0, threading_D0 Pi0.5 LoRA warm-up training on an53 GPUs 1,2,4,7. Each task had approximately 36K steps (about 63 hours). Due to PYTHONPATH issues, the first attempt failed, but it was successfully restarted after fixing. Now each task runs for 6.3 seconds. ❌ Launch of LLaVA MPM/MCM Parallel Training — Confirmed that the paper had not published the LLaVA LoRA checkpoint. The cluster lacked a complete llava-v1.5-7b base model. MPm training data llava_motion.json (460MB, 14M lines) was ready. Base model was obtained through offline safetensors→pytorch_model.bin format conversion. Modified lora.sh to use ZeRO-3→ZeRO-2 to avoid gradient division issues. MPm/MCM ran parallel on an53 GPUs 3,5, but the training time estimation was severely incorrect (13.5 days vs expected hours). Dataset size estimation was incorrect (160K→1M), requiring users to re-plan. ✅ Optimization of Phoenix Data Loading — Modified TrajectoryIndexSpeedDataset in trajectory_data.py. Added preload_all_data() function to preload all HDF5 data into memory (uint8 type saved 4 times the storage space). Changed getitem to numpy slicing. Set the number of parameters for get_dataloader to num_workers=8, prefetch_factor=2. Solved DataLoader bottleneck issue, allowing GPU utilization to reach only 5-6%. ✅ Support for Cross-Platform Deployment of Gadget summarize Windows — Created website and update.ps1 file (equivalent to update.sh in PowerShell). Added run_hugo_update() cross-platform function in daily_summary.py (Windows preferred update.ps1, otherwise Git Bash used; UNIX handled update.sh). Replaced three hardcoded bash calls in daily_summary.py, and one call in monthly_summary.py. ✅ Generate Bulk Historical Reports for February (Over 20 Days) — Used gadget/summarize tool on TzJsDesktop to generate historical reports for more than 20 days from 2026-02-01 to 2026-03-06. Reports covered key aspects of multiple projects such as MIHD, ErrorRecoveryBenchmark, CalendarPro, gadget, and DesktopVideo. Dialogue data across devices (DCC, MacBook, tianhe, TzJsDesktop) was processed through export/merge process. ✅ Add Language Configuration System to research_scout.py — Implemented full language support: _DEFAULT_LANGUAGE constant, _language_instruction() helper function, {language_instruction} in three prompt templates. Language parameters were passed throughout the process. –language command line flag, and integration with config.json. ✅ Implement init –from-overview Function — Added LLM-driven reverse initialization function: created project_from_overview() function, OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT for parsing, command line parameters (–from-overview, –api, –timeout). ✅ Generate Multiple Task BC-RNN Error Scenarios — Generated error scenarios for six tasks using injection and natural capture modes in the training environment. Added –task parameter in 1c_generate_from_policy.py to automatically parse checkpoints/datasets/task configurations. Fixed is_natural protection and load_demoActions extension in 2_visualize_scene.py. A total of 191 error scenarios were generated. ✅ In-depth Evaluation of 3D-MVP Papers on Robot Control — Conducted in-depth analysis of arXiv 2406.18158v2 (3D-MVP: 3D multi-view pre-training for robot control).
