Three key points were identified: ① 3D perception pre-training significantly improved downstream generalization ability, verified with large-scale Objaverse datasets; ② Modular separation of visual encoder and action decoder made pre-training more flexible; ③ Multi-view input provided powerful 3D scene understanding capabilities, achieved through implicit triangulation. Output included ratings of relevance=4/novelty=4/inspiring=4, and suggestions for benchmark design. - ✅ Implement arXiv_ID Deduplication and Early Stopping in Search — Added load_known_arxiv_ids() function to scan cache. Modified search_arxiv() function to use continuous known paper counting mechanism (stopped after 5 consecutive cached papers). Passed known paper IDs through cmd_search and cmd_report. Implementation and Fixes### Key Limitations of Artificial Intelligence### General Limitations When planning three new features, the AI created a plan document, but the user immediately rejected it and said “implement according to the following plan”. The AI should have asked “should I implement now or just create a plan” before writing the plan document. Lessons Learned Key Lessons Zero-shot baseless models perform significantly worse than PCA in clustering tasks: FM is designed to optimize generalization across tasks (pre-trained goal is language modeling/contrastive learning), not to maximize variance. PCA performs task-related dimensionality reduction, and maximizing variance means maximizing cluster separation. This conclusion is confirmed by Microsoft’s research and several papers, as well as MIHD’s own data (PCA’s ARI is 0.261, while scGPT’s is 0.170, a difference of 54%). Evaluation metric bias often results from multiple errors rather than a single mistake: In the BC-RNN demo_len=0, trigger_step=0, and transport always False scenario, the successful episode RP dropped to the expected 0.5 due to four errors, but actually only reached 0.003. End-to-end validation is needed, not isolated unit tests. The macOS window layer not only controls visual Z order but also event routing permissions: The.documentsWindow layer (located below desktop icons) doesn’t receive any user input events. True interactive “browsing mode” requires using the higher-level.normal layer. This is a system-level constraint, not an API configuration issue. In a GPU cluster environment, it’s better to prioritize searching for existing model copies and performing format conversions (e.g., from HF format to original LLaVA format), rather than downloading through proxies. This can save weeks of time. Successful conversions from HF format to original LLaVA format (including vocabulary truncation, from 32064 to 32000) prove this. When designing automated paper management tools, it’s important to follow human researchers’ cognitive processes: quick filtering (relevance + novelty + source authority) → in-depth analysis (core insights + problem definition + experimental design + limitations). Don’t try to evaluate everything at once. h5py file handling cannot be shared between forked subprocesses (this is a limitation of PyTorch DataLoader). The only safe solution is to load the main process into numpy before forking. NFS random reads are much faster than sequential reads. Loading everything at once is 10 times faster than loading on demand. Using uint8 can reduce memory usage by 4 times. Before starting large-scale training, it’s necessary to verify the actual dataset size, step time, and total number of steps to avoid wasting resources. In the Phoenix MPM training case, an incorrect estimate of dataset size increased it from 160,000 to 1 million, resulting in a 13.5-day delay in training time. This proves the importance of early verification. Context-aware intent classification needs to combine session state (session_id, last_activity_at) and conversation history. Simple keyword matching can lead to ambiguity (e.g., “What time is it?” could refer to querying the time or asking about a schedule). CalendarPro solves this problem with context-dependent keyword classification and quick confirmation. The research_scout.py architecture consists of a single 1871-line file, divided into 0-10 numbered sections. It has a two-stage LLM process (filtering → in-depth analysis) and three backends (claude_cli, anthropic, openai). The parameter parsing chain is CLI > project.json > config.json > default. Cache validation is done using keyword hashing. Command line tool parameter configuration should use a hierarchical parsing strategy: CLI flags > specific project configurations > user-level global configurations > hard-coded defaults. This provides flexibility while maintaining reasonable default values. Batch document generation tools need a two-stage architecture: the export stage parses raw data locally on each device, and the merge stage aggregates data across devices. When there are a large number of daily sessions, a “range merging” step is needed to remove duplicates and consolidate multiple outputs from the same day. The solution to conflicts in conda environment import priorities for multi-user sharing is to set PYTHONPATH=/path/to/user/src:$PYTHONPATH during script execution. This prioritizes loading user’s own source files without affecting other users, and no separate environment needs to be created. Language configuration for LLM processes needs to be injected at the prompt level, not later in the process. Different parts of the output require different rules (e.g., JSON keys should remain in English, and content should be in the target language). Extracting structured data from documents along with an LLM works well. OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT can reliably parse overview.md, extracting titles, keywords, categories, and questions. Pipeline regular expression patterns must match exactly. “## 3. Current Technical Approach” won’t match “##\s*Current Method and Progress”. The template-driven architecture needs to strictly follow the format. arXiv search results are returned in reverse chronological order. A continuous known paper counter is an effective early stopping heuristic because it indicates that the processed time range has been reached. Practical Experience Key adjustments for cross-platform script migration: Windows uses Get-Item .LastWriteTime instead of find -newer, Start-Job instead of & parallel, Push-Location/Pop-Location instead of cd, [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText ensures UTF-8 without BOM, -replace instead of sed/perl. PowerShell and bash differ greatly in terms of process control and error handling mechanisms. Dialogue Summary MIHD ✅ Updated CLAUDE.md and created a research positioning document for overview.md + a systematic survey of scGPT performance 20:29:42.417 | claude_code Explored 13 key files in the code library through parallel proxies, accurately updated CLAUDE.md (added pipeline configuration structure, cache directory details, code statistics), and then created a 676-line overview.md with a one-sentence positioning, 4 core questions, technical roadmap, ranking of 10 fusion strategies, and 8 open questions. Launched 4 parallel proxies to retrieve scGPT-related papers, zero-shot limit papers, BioLLM framework papers, and MIHD internal results, demonstrating that zero-shot scGPT performs significantly worse than PCA in clustering tasks (PCA ARI is 0.261, while scGPT’s is 0.170, a difference of 54%). Created a patch query to distinguish clinical vision-driven zero-shot from STAIG.
ErrorRecoveryBenchmark 🔄 BC-RNN evaluation process fixed 4 errors and generated 191 error scenarios + launched LaVA training 04:22:21.377 | claude_code Located and fixed 4 errors that caused low evaluation metrics in BC-RNN RP: incorrect field name for demo_len, source error in triggering step reading, transmission phase always False (added get_target_pos() for dynamic target position retrieval), successful episode RP not being 1.0. Adjusted noise parameters for three error classifiers. Completed all 127 unit tests, GPU verification confirmed demo_len=605, trigger_step=55 non-zero, and highest RP was 0.800. Completed 6 tasks × (number of injections + naturally captured) totaling 191 error scenarios. Launched LaVA MPM/MCM parallel training (ZeRO-3 → ZeRO-2, offline format conversion of base model), reducing training time from expected 13.5 days to about 2 days. However, discovered that incorrect estimation of dataset size (increased from 160,000 to 1 million) was prevented by the user. 🔄 FLARE D0×4 training launch and Phoenix data loading optimization
03:35:40.921 | claude_code
After launching 9 MimicGen tasks, calculated 3 missing standard statistics. LoRA training reduced to 4 D0 tasks per user request (coffee/stack/stack three types/threads). Resolved openpi configuration loading conflicts through PYTHONPATH override. 4 D0 tasks ran smoothly on GPUs 1, 2, 4, 7, with each step taking 6.3 seconds (about 63 hours per task). Also achieved preloading memory optimization to solve Phoenix HDF5 I/O bottleneck (loaded all data into numpy at once, set num_workers of DataLoader to 8).
DesktopVideo 🔄 Complete implementation and error fixing of WKWebView web wallpaper 07:14:20.499 | claude_code
Created WebNavigationHandler.swift file, extendedContentType enum to include.web type, implemented showWeb/cleanupWebView/saveWebBookmark functions, updated AppDelegate’s playback status management and SingleScreenView’s UI design. Fixed sandbox network permission issues (added com.apple.security.network.client), browsing mode interaction issues (window.level=.normal), scroll bar transparency issues (removed drawsBackground), volume synchronization issues (removed isWebContent guard), and low UA prompt issues (set customUserAgent to modern Chrome). Implemented ability to play any web video as a desktop wallpaper, but encountered problems with the browsing mode window layer: .desktopWindow couldn’t receive events, requiring the use of the.normal layer.
gadget (Summary) ✅ Implementation of cross-platform deployment support for Tool Windows 07:14:20.499 | claude_code
Created a website and updated.ps1 script (equivalent to the full PowerShell version of update.sh). Added a cross-platform function run_hugo_update to dailySummary.py (Windows prefers using update.ps1 script, otherwise Git Bash; UNIX systems use bash). Replaced 3 hardcoded bash calls in dailySummary.py and 1 call in monthlySummary.py. Also updated outdated line count and import list in CLAUDE.md.
✅ Batch-generated historical AI dialogue reports for February (more than 20 days) + Windows environment configuration 11:35:59.703 | claude_code
Ran the gadget/summarize/dailySummary.py tool repeatedly on Windows systems, analyzing historical session records from February 2026-02-01 to March 2026-03-06 (from DCC/MacBook/tianhe three devices), covering MIHD multimodal fusion, error recovery benchmark test scenario generation, VLA integration, QueST project documentation, CalendarPro upgrade, DesktopVideo development, report tool development, etc. Generated structured JSON reports and completed multi-day summary merging. Encountered the issue of Hugo command not found during merging, solved by installing Hugo.Hugo.Extended 0.157.0 and restarting shell via winget. Finally, created a complete research archive from February to early March.
CalendarPro ✅ Context-aware intent classification and session-aware conversation history management 02:02:53.360 | claude_code
Implemented context-aware intent classification system (divided general_keywords into always_general and context_dependent to avoid incorrect classification due to vague queries like “What time is it?”). Also implemented session-aware conversation history management (automatic compression, session reset, token budget control). Completed A/B/C/D four-phase plan: token tool, session-aware history reconstruction, intent classification reconstruction, token budget context building. Added 41 special tests, all 360 tests passed. Fixed multiple pytest parameter errors, compression logic assertion errors, Settings patch range errors, and other testing issues. Fully resolved the issue of inaccurate intent classification caused by keyword pre-checking.
gadget (Research Scout) ✅ Implemented research scout paper management system from scratch until production-level optimization 20:26:51.124 | claude_code
Analyzed the gadget’s code library through 3 Explore sub-proxies, designing a two-tier system: project overview document (overview.md + project.json) + automated search tool (research_scout.py). Developed approximately 750 lines of core script, including project CRUD operations, arXiv search, LLM three-backend support, paper evaluation scoring, direction suggestion generation, weekly report output, Hugo deployment, caching mechanism, etc. Designed a two-stage paper evaluation process: first stage quickly filters all papers (title + first 1000 characters of abstract, determining relevance levels); second stage deeply analyzes highly relevant papers (≤20, including 3 highlights: insights/ motivation/ experiments/limitations). Completed writing requirements.txt, CLAUDE.md, TUTORIAL.md Chinese tutorial. Tests passed: successfully created test projects, searched for 50 cs.RO+cs.LG papers (30-day review), cached correctly. Then made improvements: generated comprehensive CLAUDE.md file for research_scout.py, fixed 3 critical errors in the error-recovery-benchmark project configuration (ID mismatch, header file format, missing markers), implemented full language support (_DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, _language_instruction(), configuration parsing chain), added LLM-driven reverse initialization (init –from-overview with OVERVIEW_EXTRACT_PROMPT), renamed “weekly report” to “daily report”, implemented arXiv_id deduplication, and used early stopping strategy.
Robot Control Research ✅ In-depth evaluation of 3D-MVP papers 23:23:34.598 | claude_code
Conducted in-depth methodological analysis of arXiv 2406.18158v2 (3D-MVP: 3D multi-view pre-training for robot control). Extracted three key highlights and design motivations: 3D perception pre-training solved the problem of ignoring task geometry in 2D pre-training; modular design supported standard self-supervised pre-training; multi-view fusion improved spatial reasoning robustness through implicit triangulation. Output included a score of 4/Relevance=4/Insight=4, and proposed benchmark test design suggestions based on 3D-MVP and test directions for multi-view robustness.

title: Bug Journal 2026-03-06

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-05 Daily Overview Completed Work: Multiple projects were executed in parallel on 4 devices: MIHD RM-IDEAL evaluation infrastructure, device documentation system, machine learning training process debugging, and CalendarPro autonomous intelligent upgrade. Completion Methods: DCC ran long-running background computing tasks (RM-IDEAL cross-sample benchmark tests), and wrote visualization scripts; MacBook performed parallel research agents to generate documentation; Tianhe was responsible for environment setup and deep code analysis to find machine learning vulnerabilities; TzJsDesktop implemented a comprehensive personal housekeeper system with an event-driven architecture, including multi-agent coordination and systematic code quality audits. Impacts: RM-IDEAL evaluations revealed differences in the quality of specific layers (Layer_1/5 performed exceptionally well with ρ values of 0.42-0.66, while Layer_3/6 showed negative correlations with ρ values of -0.21 to -0.36), providing guidance for subsequent optimizations; the device received a monthly review mechanism and a 676-line introductory guide; VLA training returned to normal, Openpi-moe standardization errors were fixed, avoiding scale mismatch issues; CalendarPro shifted from a passive scheduler to an active self-housekeeper, with 319 tests passed, but critical technical debts found that needed immediate fixing. DCC Completed Work: MIHD project RM-IDEAL evaluation infrastructure: Cross-sample benchmark tests from 151673 to 151508, writing visualization scripts, optimizing project descriptions to emphasize zero-sample/patch query functions, and planning GPU acceleration. Completion Method: Conducted long-running WWL+Wasserstein calculations (3-4 hours per direction), wrote three-panel visualization scripts, iteratively optimized project descriptions, emphasized the necessity of zero-sample requirements, and distinguished it from STAIG end-to-end training. The Plan agent was initiated to design GPU Sinkhorn implementation plans. Impact: Completed bidirectional 7-label evaluations for 151673↔151508, finding the highest Spearman ρ value of 0.657 (only for Layer_4), and identified a key issue where intermediate layer embeddings were inversely correlated with topological rankings. A narrative framework was established from a clinical perspective (patch queries across patient knowledge transfer). MacBook Completed Work: Device toolkit documentation: February monthly data analysis and merging, writing a comprehensive Claude Code guide. Completion Method: Used LLM to analyze the entire month’s AI dialogue daily report JSON data to generate structured summaries. Three parallel research agents were initiated to collect Claude Code information. Research results were read in plan mode, and then a 10-chapter, 676-line guide was written. Impact: Established a systematic review mechanism for historical dialogue data. The Claude Code guide covered installation/functionality/integration/best practices/pricing, providing a standard reference document for the team. TzJsDesktop Completed Work: Comprehensive upgrade of the CalendarPro personal housekeeper system (16 new services, 21 file modifications, 68 tests). A systematic code quality audit was conducted, revealing 3 unfinished implementations, 16 dangerous silent exception handlers, and missing BackgroundCoordinator startup. Completion Method: Studied the reference code library (openclaw multi-agent ACP/gateway/plugin SDK, get-shit-done wave execution/context engineering/200K window). The source of the error cannot be found during debugging. Solution: Replace it with except Exception as e: logger.error/warning(f"Description: {e}"), retaining error messages for diagnosis, distinguishing between errors (critical functions) and warnings (minor functions).
Key Insight: Never use empty pass to handle exceptions; if ignoring is necessary, at least record the logs; silent failures in state tracking can lead to “Schrödinger’s state” — unknown whether it is truly empty or a loading failure.
4. How to efficiently implement a large-scale upgrade plan with 31 goals without creating sequential bottlenecks? Solution: Use parallel background agent execution mode: Start 4 independent agents to execute context_detector, checkin_service, learning_service, and energy_projection enhancement functions, while directly modifying 8 other files. All agents successfully completed without conflicts.
Key Insight: Wave-based parallel execution can significantly accelerate large-scale refactoring when dependencies are correctly isolated. The key is to identify independent work units that can run concurrently.
5. AI repeatedly tries to enter plan mode, but users prefer to execute directly. Solution: Users rejected the ExitPlanMode tool, providing direct execution content for AI to perform. AI read the results of previous research agents in plan mode and then directly wrote the documentation without re-planning.
Key Insight: Users’ work style prefers rapid iteration rather than long-term planning. AI should adaptively reduce the frequency of plan mode triggering, or learn this preference after multiple user rejections.
6. CalendarPro has 6 completely untested source files, 12 modified files containing untested new methods, and BackgroundCoordinator is implemented but never started. Solution: A systematic testing infrastructure was created: conftest.py for sharing test fixtures, creating 20 new test files based on existing patterns (pytest, AsyncMock, patch decorator), fixing the on_ready() function to actually call coordinator.start_all(), and adding 9 missing Discord intent handlers.
Key Insight: Large-scale features usually come with incomplete integration. Background services require explicit startup calls, not just class definitions. The system’s testing requires shared test fixtures and consistency in all test files.
7. The patch path in test fixtures is incorrect (patch to call point instead of definition point), causing simulation failures after lazy loading, with multiple tests reporting AttributeError errors. Solution: All patch functions for singleton factories (get_settings/get_calendar_service, etc.) must be located in the source module (src.config/src.services.calendar_service), not at the call point (e.g., srcservices.gap_analyzer), because lazy loading delays name binding.
Key Insight: The golden rule for unittest.mock.patch is to patch at the location where it is searched, not at the definition point. Lazy loading means the “search” location is always the source module, not creating new bindings at the import location.
8. How to discover hidden errors and incomplete implementations after major refactoring in the large-scale code library? Solution: Multiple dedicated exploration agents were initiated: TODO/FIXME searcher, git status analyzer, test gap detector, and exception auditor. The system’s multi-faceted audit revealed key issues: incomplete executors, stub get_progress(), unimplemented diet preference learning, and over 50 dangerous silent exception handlers.
Key Insight: Code auditing requires multi-dimensional searches: static analysis (TODO, NotImplementedError), behavioral analysis (exception handlers, empty loops), and integrated analysis (service registration, startup hooks). Single-angle audits may overlook certain types of errors.
Common Issues 9. RM-IDEAL computations are very time-consuming (3-4 hours per direction, approximately 3500-4000 locations × 7 tags × WWL+Wasserstein) Solution: Use run_in_background=true to submit background tasks, regularly check progress through TaskOutput, and cache files (completed layers and file modification timestamps) to avoid blocking interactions.
Key Insight: Long computation tasks should run in the background, and progress should be monitored through the file system status, not through stdout, as stdout may fail due to redirection issues and is unreliable.
10. Users mistakenly thought the DLPFC part ID 151508 was 151608 Solution: AI actively confirmed the valid range of part IDs (151508-151510 and 151669-151676), asked the user if they meant 151508, and got confirmation before proceeding.Key insights: AI should actively verify obviously unreasonable inputs and provide friendly corrections instead of blindly executing them.
11. The path to the output file of the background task does not exist (/tmp/claude-1549010/…bvwfk6yeq.output) Solution: Although the output file reading failed, the calculation results of RM-IDEAL were correctly saved in the outputs/rm_ideal_benchmark/ directory (summary.csv, detailed_results.json, rm_cache/*.npy). It is possible to read data directly from the result file.
Key insights: The actual output of the background task (persistent file) is more reliable than the temporary stdout log. It is better to check the final results rather than the intermediate logs.
12. The curobo compilation failed, with the message “cuda_runtime_api.h: No such file or directory”, even though the CUDA toolkit was installed in the RefineVLA’s conda environment. Solution: The CUDA header file is located in a non-standard location (targets/x86_64-linux/include instead of include/). This issue was resolved by setting the CPLU_INCLUDE_PATH and C_INCLUDE_PATH environment variables before pip installation.
Key insights: The CUDA toolkit installed using Conda may place the header files in sub-directories of a specific architecture instead of the standard path. Always use the find command to check the actual location of the header files before assuming the standard path.

Lessons Learned Main Lessons Learned - The combination of EventBus, BackgroundCoordinator, and ContextAssembler is a powerful pattern for building modular AI assistants: EventBus implements service decoupled communication (publishing-consumeing), BackgroundCoordinator manages the lifecycle of scheduled tasks (CronScheduler + conditional loading), and ContextAssembler dynamically loads context layers based on intent types (avoiding full data transfers every time). Together, they support the collaboration of more than 16 independent services. The Spearman correlation of RM-IDEAL shows significant directional asymmetry: The correlation coefficient ρ between 151508 and 151673 in Layer_4 is 0.657, while the reverse relationship between 151673 and 151508 is only 0.275. This indicates that there are differences in the fused embedding space for the same label across different samples, which may be related to sample point density, noise level, or annotation quality. The topological structure of intermediate layers (Layer_3/6) in the embedding space shows negative correlations (ρ ranges from -0.21 to -0.36): This may be because these layers are confused with adjacent layers (Layer_2/4/5) in the embedding space, resulting in the order of embedding being opposite to the order of RM-IDEAL based on topology. This indicates that boundary layers (Layer_1/WM) and central layers (Layer_5) are easier to distinguish, and intermediate transition layers require stronger discriminative features. Data preprocessing processes need to be clearly verified to ensure that all expected features have standardized statistical information. Silent skipping (strict=False) may lead to scale mismatch issues throughout the training process, without causing errors. For large-scale refactoring projects, referring to mature open-source project architectures (such as openclaw’s ACP spawn/Gateway/plugin SDK, get-shit-done’s wave execution/context engineering/200K window) is more reliable than zero design. However, it is necessary to adapt to the conventions of the target language (such as TypeScript→Python’s async/await, singleton factory functions, dataclass), and cannot be directly copied. Unit testing (isolating components, simulating dependencies, providing quick feedback) and integration testing (real Discord bot interactions, end-to-end experience) are two complementary dimensions: the former ensures code correctness (319 test cases passed), while the latter ensures that the user experience meets expectations (29 manual test checks); 68 unit test passes do not mean the system is ready, and real user scenarios are needed for verification. Dangerous silent exception handling (only handling Exception exceptions) is a technical debt tumor: It makes the system appear to run normally, but in reality, the state is incomplete (situation_monitor’s energy/diet/exercise/ideas/reminders may all be empty), and the source of the error cannot be found during debugging (Schrödinger’s state). Exceptions should always be logged (logger.error/warning), with distinction between critical functions (errors) and minor functions (warnings), and diagnostic information should be retained. Large-scale refactoring should gradually verify key integration points (service startup, event connection, data persistence), rather than implementing all functions first and then testing. The ‘implement all functions first and then test’ approach will result in a system that is technically feasible but cannot run properly (services exist but will never start). Project description narrative strategy: External communication should start with aspects that are relevant to the target audience of the application scenario (such as clinical patch query knowledge transfer), then explain the technical path and key differences (zero-shot vs. end-to-end training), and finally elevate to domain value. Technical details serve the narrative logic, not the other way around. Control each sentence within double short clauses to reduce cognitive burden. Background services need to explicitly call the startup function in application lifecycle hooks. Class definitions and dependency injection are not enough - it is necessary to call coordinator.start_all() in on_ready() or similar functions. Solution to Python loop import problem: Use deferred imports at the package level (only using all, not importing immediately, add explanatory comments), import at the module level inside functions (import deferred until the actual need arises), and do not call the site when testing patch source modules (because deferred imports do not create new bindings at the site call); three-layer defense ensures scalability. Parallel agent execution greatly accelerates the development of independent modules. Four background agents (context_detector, checkin_service, learning_service, energy_projection) complete complex enhancement functions simultaneously, while the main thread handles joint modules. The total time is about 140 seconds, while the sequential method takes about 560 seconds (4 times faster). Effective code auditing requires multi-dimensional analysis: syntax level (TODO/FIXME/NotImplementedError), behavior level (exception handling patterns, empty loops, stub methods), structure level (missing test coverage, unused exports), and operation level (service registration, startup hooks). Auditing from a single dimension will overlook problems across the entire category. Creating large test suites benefits from parallel agent execution and shared fixtures. The reusable simulators in conftest.py (mock_settings, mock_notification) make it possible to write consistent tests across more than 20 files. Practical Learning Long-running background task monitoring strategy: For hour-long computing tasks, use run_in_background and monitor progress in the cache file system (file modification time, number of completed sub-tasks), rather than relying on potentially failed standard output redirection. The final result should be read from the persistent output directory, not the temporary log files. UNI/UNI2 standardization mechanism: Both perform L2 standardization at the output layer (F.normalize(features, dim=-1)), ensuring that the L2 norm of the feature vector is 1. The input layer uses ImageNet standardization by default unless skip_normalize=True. This two-layer standardization ensures geometric consistency in the feature space, making it easy to calculate cosine similarity. The CUDA toolkit installed using Conda places the header files in a specific architecture path (targets/x86_64-linux/include), instead of the top-level include/ directory. This fix is backward-compatible (statistical information is only calculated when keys exist). CalendarPro ✅ Designing an Upgrade Plan for a Personal Butler System (Exploring Reference Architectures and Five-Stage Methodology)

It also studied the architecture patterns of openclaw’s multi-agent ACP/Gateway/plugin SDK, get-shit-done’s waveform execution, context engineering, and 200K window architecture. The AI fixed these issues one by one: (1) Deleted unnecessary loops in executor.py:158-161 (only iterating Task objects, but the body is empty, and plan variables are not used); (2) Implemented the get_progress() function, returning a dictionary {plan_id, status: “executing”}; (3) Implemented the learn_from_history() function for learning meal times (requiring more than 3 data points, calculating average time and distribution, updating preferred_range in user_preferences); (4) Replaced 16 silent exceptions with logger.error/warning, involving situation_monitor (5 locations in energy/meals/exercise/thoughts/reminders), autonomous_executor (3 locations in cache invalidation/meal detection/decision recording), agent_registry (4 locations in audit logs/3 EventBus emissions), life_context (2 locations in learning statistics/butler context), scheduler (1 location in meal conflict resolution), energy_projection (1 location in movement relevance); (5) Added import logging and logger declarations in scheduler.py. title: Bug Journal 2026-03-05

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-04 Daily Overview Completed Work: Cross-device multi-project work, including space transcriptomics literature research, documentation improvements, large-scale VLA training infrastructure setup, and comprehensive error recovery benchmark documentation synchronization. Completion Methods: DCC: Web-based Arxiv paper analysis for evaluation method extraction; MacBook: Code library analysis using init commands for documentation auditing; Tianhe: Parallel proxy exploration to align code and documentation, checkpoint inventory through file system traversal, and multi-GPU training coordination planning. Impact: Fully understood the cross-sample ecosystem evaluation framework; improved the introductory documentation for Desktop Video; laid the foundation for a 6-phase VLA training activity over several weeks; synchronized the overview of the error_recovery_benchmark project with actual conditions (6,474 episodes, 53 framework files, 22 scripts, 122 tests). DCC Completed Work: Research on the evaluation method for QueST paper in space transcriptomics. Completion Method: Obtained and analyzed the HTML version (v1) and abstract page (v3) of Arxiv to obtain detailed information about the evaluation framework. Impact: Used WWL graph kernel and Wasserstein distance as benchmarks for ecosystem similarity, clarified the evaluation framework, and used accuracy metrics such as top-1 accuracy and overall similarity accuracy. MacBook Completed Work: Audited and improved the CLAUDE.md documentation for Desktop Video using init commands. Completion Method: Analyzed the code library structure (Swift/SwiftUI/AVFoundation), identified deficiencies in existing documentation, and made three targeted edits: corrected capitalization issues in agents.md, added localization hints for Localizable.xcstrings, and enhanced the inline documentation of ChangeLog format. Impact: Improved the clarity and completeness of the documentation, providing accurate file references and bilingual development workflow guidance for future Claude code examples. Tianhe Completed Work: Discussed during the full-day meeting the training setup of the zhaoganlong framework, updated the error_recovery_benchmark documentation comprehensively, and conducted a performance survey of BC-RNN. Completion Method: Developed a plan for implementing 7 tasks for the 6-phase training process, covering 9 MimicGen tasks; deployed 3 parallel exploration proxies for code library analysis; checked points through file system traversal; systematically edited the documentation based on actual implementation; began comparing BC-RNN training and evaluation. Impact: Laid the foundation for an 8× A800 GPU training activity over several weeks, with checkpoint locations verified; the documentation now matches actual conditions, including accurate statistics, detailed API descriptions, and a three-source pipeline architecture; identified an anomaly with 0% success rate for BC-RNN that needs debugging. Multi-project research and documentation work: studied the cross-sample query evaluation method used in QueST paper for space transcriptomics, improved the CLAUDE.md documentation for Desktop Video, created a comprehensive zhaoganlong self-reflection framework training guide, and synchronized the error_recovery_benchmark documentation to match the actual code library implementation, including a redesign of the pipeline architecture.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Training Setup and Documentation for zhaoganlong Self-Reflection Framework — Created a comprehensive 7-task implementation plan for all 6 phases (Phase 0-5: data preparation, diffusion strategy, LLaVA MPM, MCM feedback collection, LLaVA MCM, lifelong learning), executing 9 MimicGen tasks on 49 8× A800 GPUs. Recorded the complete training method, including checkpoint list: Pi0.5 Phoenix (180GB, 21 steps, last modified on 2026-02-24), basic OpenPI model (about 12GB each), diffusion strategy snapshots (4, each 253MB). Started modifying 4 data preparation scripts to enable all tasks. Created docs/zhaoganlong_training_guide.md, including inference pipeline patterns and complete file path references. ✅ Updated the panoramic summary.md of the error_recovery_benchmark project to match actual conditions — By deploying parallel proxies, aligned the project overview documentation with the actual code library: added detailed implementations of 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, 20 error classifiers, along with API descriptions; updated code statistics (scripts increased from 18 to 22, tests from 109 to 122, evaluation runs from 7 to 11); corrected M14 evaluation data (726 episodes became 6,474 episodes, approximately 83% completed, performance evaluation per strategy); corrected VLA model types (Pi0-FAST to Phoenix/Flare); recorded plugin architecture with registration patterns. ✅ Redesigned the core pipeline architecture of error_recovery_benchmark — Updated Section 3.1 of the pipeline, showing three parallel sources of error scenarios: (1) replaying demonstration datasets and injecting them, (2) VLA/strategy publishing and injecting, (3) natural fault capture—all of which are input into ErrorSceneDatabase. Resolved the issue where the original chart only showed paths based on demonstrations. Added explanations of the purpose and complementary relationships of each path. ✅ Research on QueST cross-sample query evaluation methods — Obtained QueST paper (arxiv 2410.10652) and extracted the complete evaluation framework: used Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman graph kernel to build benchmarks from cell type labels, evaluating with top-1 accuracy and overall similarity accuracy (Pearson correlation with WWL benchmarks). Synthetic query experiments were designed using 9 different cell types and sizes (1-3, 50-200 cells). Users corrected to use v3 instead of v1; confirmed the existence of v3 but no HTML version. 🔄 Investigation into BC-RNN 0% success rate anomaly — Users reported that BC-RNN had a 0% success rate on all normal tasks, while the expected success rate after training release was around 60-70%. Started exploring differences between training and evaluation code (observation keys, environment settings, checkpoint loading), but interrupted. Needed to compare the release of training scripts with benchmark evaluation settings. Users mentioned the previously fixed “object” observation key issue—which might be related to this problem. Implementation and Fixes ❌ Review of ErrorSceneDatabase design — Users raised questions about the current database design. Discussed actual implementation (meta.json index, scene JSON+NPZ pair, deduplication, automatic classification, 4 segmentation strategies). Users felt some parts were “strange,” but abandoned the discussion before identifying the specific issue. ✅ Improvement to Desktop Video CLAUDE.md — Analyzed the Desktop Video code library using init commands, identified three areas for improvement in the existing CLAUDE.md, and implemented fixes: corrected capitalization issues in agents.md, added localization workflow hints (Localizable.xcstrings), enhanced ChangeLog format documentation, and adopted bilingual entry structures and date format descriptions. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Documentation does not match code in error_recovery_benchmark: The panoramic summary.md describes outdated pipelines, lacks implementation details, and incorrect statistics (726 episodes do not match the actual 6,474 episodes) Solution: Deployed parallel exploration proxies to thoroughly check the errorrameworks/, scripts/, configs/, and outputs/ directories, then systematically updated the documentation with verified data: implementations of detectors/injectors/validators/classifiers, actual M14 evaluation data, and real code statistics. Key Insight: In active projects, changes to documentation are inevitable—a systematic alignment process between code and documentation is needed. For large code libraries, using parallel proxies for exploration is an effective way: one proxy corresponds to each main directory, and the results are then integrated.
2. BC-RNN had a 0% success rate on all regular tasks (such as coffee, stack, thread, etc.), although the performance during training was around 60-70% Solution: Started exploration but did not complete it: needed to compare (1) observation dictionary keys between training and evaluation; (2) differences in environment initialization; (3) correctness of checkpoint loading; (4) consistency of action spaces. Users mentioned the fix for the “object” observation key issue—which might be related to this problem.
Key Insight: Mismatches between training and evaluation are common failure modes. BC-RNN had a 0.28% success rate on error recovery tasks (5/1800 successes), but a 0% success rate on regular tasks (0/250 attempts), indicating that the problem might be due to environmental or observation data mismatches, rather than defects in the strategy itself. A systematic comparison list is needed: observation keys, environment configurations, action boundaries, camera settings, normalization, etc.
3. zhaoganlong’s data preparation scripts only enabled the coffee_d1 task (other 8 tasks were commented out), which hindered multi-task training Solution: Systematically uncomment the 9 tasks in the TASK_MAPPING dictionary, removing pdb.set_TRACE() debug breakpoints that would interrupt batch execution.
Key Insight: The existing code library was designed for single-task prototypes; to scale to a multi-task production environment, tasks need to be enabled in batches in the data pipeline, checkpoint paths, and orchestration scripts.
4. The flowchart did not reflect the user’s multi-source visual needs: Only demonstrated error injection based on demonstrations, lacking VLA scrolling injection and natural fault capture paths Solution: Redrew the flowchart in Section 3.1, dividing it into three parallel paths (demonstration injection, strategy scrolling injection, natural fault capture), all of which eventually converge at ErrorSceneDatabase. Added explanations of the purpose of each path and how they complement each other.
Key Insight: Visual charts must conform to the user’s mental model, not just the code structure. Users’ concerns about “VLA scrolling injection during execution” indicate that the function is supported by the code, but overlooked in the documentation—missing documentation is just as bad as incorrect documentation.
5. The Arxiv paper’s PDF version returned binary encoded content, making it impossible to extract methodological details directly Solution: Use the HTML version provided by arxiv.org/html/2410.10652v1, which provides complete text with all technical details.
Key Insight: For Arxiv papers, the HTML version is more readable for machines than the PDF version. Always confirm whether the HTML version is available when checking.
6. zhaoganlong’s framework checkpoints are scattered across multiple locations (Pi0.5 Phoenix alone has about 180GB of checkpoints), making it unclear which ones are available Solution: Conducted a comprehensive file system search: found the checkpoints for Pi0.5 Phoenix (21 steps, total size 180GB, last modified on 2026-02-24), basic OpenPI checkpoints (pi0_base, pi05_base, pi0_fast_base, pi0_libero, each about 12GB), diffusion strategy checkpoints (4 snapshots, each about 253MB, dated 2025-08-22). Confirmed all paths and sizes, and marked the availability status.
Key Insight: Checkpoint lists are crucial for reproducibility. Large checkpoint directories (180GB) indicate that many training steps are saved—this is helpful for analysis, but it’s important to specify the exact steps used for evaluation. Date-based directory structures (2025-08-22, 2026-02-24) help track the training timeline.
General Issues 7. The user initially asked “How does this paper perform cross-sample queries?” This question was vague—it might refer to methodology or evaluation methods Solution: The user clarified that they were referring to the evaluation methodology, not the implementation methods. Rechecked the paper, focusing on evaluation metrics, construction of real values, and experimental design.
Key Insight: Regarding the “how” question of the paper, it might mean (1) technical implementation or (2) verification/evaluation methods—always clarify which aspect the user is interested in.
8. The HTML version of QueST v3 returns a 404 error when accessed directly Solution: v3 is only available in PDF format (no HTML rendering). Use the v1 HTML version for detailed content extraction, then check the v3 abstract page for version differences (no substantial differences).
Key Insight: Not all Arxiv paper versions have HTML rendering capabilities; if subsequent versions only have minor formatting changes, then v1 HTML version may be sufficient.
9. The Desktop Video CLAUDE.md file uses lowercase “agents.md” as a reference file, while the actual file name is “AGENTS.md”, which can lead to file not found errors in case-insensitive file systems Solution: Corrected the reference, using the correct uppercase “AGENTS.md” as the file name.
Key Insight: File references in documentation must match case exactly to ensure reliability in different file system configurations (macOS defaults to case-insensitivity, but can be set to case-sensitive).
Human-AI Approaches Strategic Level Multi-source design pipeline architecture Role Method Human The user immediately realized that the single-source (demonstration dataset) flowchart did not meet their core needs: they wanted to clearly show VLA scrolling injection and natural fault capture as the same source of error scenarios. They saw that the code supported this (1c_generate_from_policy.py --mode injection), but the documentation ignored this AI The AI initially focused on recording the content explicitly shown in the README/notes, treating demonstration-based injection as the main approach. It did not infer from the code capabilities what the user wanted to emphasize about the multi-source architecture Difference Analysis: Humans think about system-level problems: “How many ways can errors occur?” While AI focuses on file-level issues: “What does this script actually do?” Humans identify gaps in the documentation by understanding the problem domain—the diversity of error scenarios requires multiple sources; AI needs explicit instructions to add parallel paths
Interpretation of BC-RNN 0% success rate Role Method Human The user immediately pointed out this was an anomaly: “Why is BC-RNN so poor? It’s completely different from the performance during training.” Based on domain knowledge, they knew BC-RNN should have a 60-70% success rate on normal PickPlace tasks, so a 0% success rate indicated something was wrong, not a defect in the strategy itself AI The AI started exploring the code structure (training scripts, evaluation scripts, configuration files), but did not immediately identify the most likely cause: observation key mismatch (the user previously mentioned fixing the “object” key issue), differences in environment initialization, or problems with data normalization Distinguishing between implementation and evaluation when analyzing papers Role Method Human When the AI first provided implementation details, the user immediately clarified “No, I mean, how does the evaluation proceed?” AI The AI initially interpreted “How does this paper perform cross-sample queries?” as a question about technical methods (such as autoencoders, contrastive learning, adversarial training)" Difference Analysis: Humans know which aspect is important for their research (evaluation framework), while AI provides a comprehensive technical overview. The user’s clarification is crucial for focusing on relevant evaluation metrics.
Granularity of training orchestration Role Method Human The user provided a complete implementation plan, including a dependency diagram for the 6 stages, clear GPU allocation (Stage 1: GPU 0, Stage 2: GPU 1-4, etc.), parallel execution strategies, and tmux session management requirements AI The AI created a TodoList matching plan structure for 7 tasks and started systematically editing each file, reading all files in parallel before editing Difference Analysis: Humans pre-designed a complete orchestration architecture, considering hardware limitations (already 6-7 GPUs available), estimating time for each stage (Stage 1: 2-4 days, Stage 2: 4-8 hours), and specifying failover measures (tmux sessions continue running after disconnection). AI followed instructions, but did not propose monitoring strategies, checkpoint validation, or resource contention handling to handle multi-day training activities.
Method of aligning documentation with code Role Method Human The user explicitly requested: “Based on the content in the folder.” They wanted detailed implementation descriptions, just like before (referring to the earlier ErrorSceneDatabase API summary), prioritizing the actual code over document statements AI The AI deployed parallel exploration proxies to read all code, then systematically updated the documentation. The pattern was: exploration → verification → recording. Used file system commands to get actual values (wc -l, du -sh, stat), rather than relying on document statements Difference Analysis: Both agreed to use real data as a baseline, but humans specified the output style (detailed API documentation embedded in the overview document). AI defaulted to a shorter update frequency—humans wanted comprehensive explanations to prevent future deviations. This indicates that documentation should be treated as an “executable specification”
Implementation Level Versatility of research paper versions| Role | Method | |——|——| | Human | The user explicitly corrected the statement “Do not use v1, there is a v3 version with updates”, showing awareness of the paper’s evolution. | | AI | The AI defaults to using the first available complete text version (v1 HTML), without checking subsequent versions. |
Difference Analysis: Humans keep track of the version history of the papers they are concerned about; AI tends to use the most accessible version (HTML rather than PDF). The user’s intervention ensures that the latest version is used.
Scope of Document Improvement Role Method Human The user initiates the /init command, triggering a comprehensive analysis of the code library and an audit of CLAUDE.md. It is expected that the AI will autonomously identify vulnerabilities and propose improvements. AI The AI analyzed the existing CLAUDE.md and found it to be “already excellent and comprehensive”. Only three simple fixes were made, rather than broader structural improvements. Difference Analysis: Humans use /init as a mechanism to identify potential document issues; AI defaults to a conservative assessment of “already good”, only making obvious corrections (such as case sensitivity, missing parts), possibly ignoring deeper organizational or completeness issues.
Limitations of AI Key Limitations Unable to infer multi-source pipeline architecture from the code: Although 1c_generate_from_policy.py clearly supports --mode injection for policy rolling injection and natural capture, the AI only recorded the paths based on demonstrations until the user explicitly corrected them. It was unable to synthesize the system-level architecture from individual script functions. Proxy timeout in large code library exploration: When exploring the zhaoganlong framework, all three parallel proxies timed out (each exceeding 1100 seconds), returning 0 tokens and requiring a restart. Large directory trees and numerous files can exceed the proxy’s token budget or trigger tool call limits. In BC-RNN debugging, high-priority fault modes were not prioritized: Instead of immediately checking the most likely culprits (observational keys not matching, which the user previously mentioned needed to be fixed), a systematic exploration was conducted but was slow due to lack of debugging heuristics. When modifying create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py, the AI interrupted its work mid-editing, leaving the multi-file data pipeline in a partially modified state. The AI did not save progress or suggest atomic batch operations (modifying all 4 files and then testing them as a unit) to avoid inconsistent intermediate states. Unable to directly parse the PDF binary content returned by the WebFetch tool. For arxiv papers, it relies heavily on the HTML version or preprocessed text. General Limitations Ambiguous query explanations: “How does this paper do X” may refer to technical implementation or evaluation methods. Clarification questions should be raised when ambiguity exists. Unable to identify design issues in ErrorSceneDatabase without specific user information: The user indicated that the design seemed “strange”, but the AI could not determine exactly what was wrong (classification logic, source tracking, query API, architectural design). A clear statement of the problem is required—it is difficult to detect design issues based solely on the code. Without checking for updates to paper versions before using v1 HTML, it is necessary to always verify the latest version when accessing arxiv papers. During the CLAUDE.md audit triggered by /init, the AI showed confirmation bias towards the quality of the existing documentation, concluding that it was “already excellent and comprehensive” after finding the content structure was good, rather than looking for potential vulnerabilities (such as a lack of troubleshooting sections, unclear version requirements, or incomplete architecture diagrams). Lessons Learned Key Lessons - QueST Evaluation Framework: When there are no existing real labels, use the Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman graph to verify synthetic real labels for cell type annotations, and then validate the model’s similarity ranking using the Pearson correlation coefficient. Multi-task machine learning processes built through incremental prototype design (starting with one task) retain decentralized single-task configurations (annotated task lists, hardcoded paths). To scale to production environments, it is necessary to systematically review data preparation scripts, dataset mapping JSON files, checkpoint directories, and evaluation configurations. Document synchronization mode: Use parallel exploration proxies (one for each main directory), verify that all numbers come from actual files (summary.json, file system statistics), and then systematically update the document content section by section. Do not rely on statements in the document—always check the actual situation of the code and data. Debugging checklist for training and evaluation mismatch: If a strategy performs well during training but fails during evaluation, it is necessary to systematically check: (1) observational dictionary keys; (2) environment initialization parameters; (3) action space boundaries; (4) data standardization; (5) camera rendering settings. The BC-RNN had a recovery rate of 0% on normal tasks, but 0.28% on error recovery tasks, indicating an environmental mismatch rather than a strategy failure. Cross-sample label query evaluation requires: (1) obtaining batch-invariant embeddings through adversarial training; (2) using graph kernels to provide real labels when labels are unavailable; (3) using dual metrics to evaluate the highest accuracy and overall ranking correlation. For spatial transcriptomics, the Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman graph kernel can convert discrete cell type labels into continuous label similarity scores by calculating the graph-level Wasserstein distance between subgraph representations. Large-scale HPC training scheduling requires clear dependency mappings (Stage 0 → Stage 1+2 parallelism → Stage 3 → Stage 4 → Stage 5), GPU allocation planning for each stage, and persistent session management (tmux) to handle multi-day training activities and SSH disconnections. The multi-source data collection architecture should be clearly stated in the documentation: Generation of error scenarios requires data from different sources (for reproducible demonstration injections, for realistic policy deployment injections, for realistic natural fault capture). The flowchart must show all sources as first-class citizens, rather than hiding them in script options. The M14 evaluation results showed baseline performance: 6,474 scenarios involving 4 strategies, with a random strategy having a recovery rate of 0% and reproducibility of 1.9%; the BC-RNN had a recovery rate of 0.28% and reproducibility of 0.3%; Pi0 had a recovery rate of 0% and reproducibility of 0.2%; Pi0.5 had a recovery rate of 0% and reproducibility of 0%. The near-zero error recovery rate verified the difficulty of the benchmark tests and provided a basis for future improvements (it is expected that M15 human recovery data and LoRA fine-tuning will show positive improvement effects). Best practices for checkpoint inventory: Record the full path, size, last modification date, and availability status of all model checkpoints. A large checkpoint directory (180GB) indicates many saved steps—it is necessary to record the specific steps used for evaluation. Date-based naming helps track the training timeline, but it is necessary to clearly associate steps with dates. Expansion of the error framework: There are 53 Python files (approximately 1.47 thousand lines of code), including 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, and 20 classifiers, covering 3 domains (operation, planning, progress). The plugin architecture is implemented through DETECTOR_REGISTRY, INJECTOR_REGISTRY, VALIDATOR_REGISTRY, CLASSIFIER_REGISTRY. The cooling time of each detector (20 steps) prevents detecting junk behavior. Practical Experience The /init command triggered a comprehensive analysis of the code library and the generation/improvement of CLAUDE.md, aiming to prepare for future Claude Code sessions. This analysis is still applicable even if CLAUDE.md already exists. Dialogue Summary MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics) ✅ Research on Cross-Sample Query Evaluation Methods for QueST Papers 22:21:12.826 | claude_code The user requested an analysis of the evaluation methods in QueST papers (rather than implementations). The v1 HTML version of the arxiv paper and the v3 abstract were obtained, and the complete evaluation framework was extracted: the Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman graph kernel constructs real labels from cell type labels, and the evaluation is done using the highest accuracy of best match and the overall similarity accuracy of Pearson r-value. The synthetic queries varied in the number of cell types (1-3) and size (50-200). After the user’s correction, the v3 version was used, and it was confirmed that v3 has no HTML version, but the content is quite rich.
Desktop Video ✅ Audit and Improvement of CLAUDE.md Documentation via the /init Command 20:56:25.688 | claude_code The user called the /init command to trigger the analysis of the code library and the generation/improvement of CLAUDE.md. The AI analyzed the structure of the Swift/SwiftUI project, read the existing CLAUDE.md file, and considered it to be “already excellent”. Three targeted fixes were made: correcting the capitalization issue in the agents.md file, adding a prompt to check Localizable.xcstrings when modifying the code, and improving the ChangeLog format documentation. The AI confirmed that there are no Cursor/.github copilot rules, and verified the dead code detection configuration in .periphery.yml.
zhaoganlong Motion-Based Self-Reflection Framework 🔄 Setup of Training Infrastructure and Comprehensive Documentation 22:23:48.001 | claude_code The user requested a complete 6-stage training plan for the zhaoganlong motion-based self-reflection framework. This framework involves 9 MimicGen tasks, running on 49 nodes (8×A800). The AI created a decomposition diagram of 7 tasks, mapping the dependencies between stages (data preparation → Diffusion + LLaVA MPM parallel → MCM feedback → LaVA MCM → Lifelong Learning), and began modifying 4 data preparation scripts by uncommenting the TASK_MAPPING entry and removing pdb breakpoints to ensure all 9 tasks could run properly. The framework directory was explored, and a complete training method covering 6 stages was recorded, including HDF5→5Hz→Speed→LaVA JSON data preparation, training with ResNet18+Transformer+UNet diffusion strategy, LaVA MPM/MCM LoRA fine-tuning, lifelong learning, and OpenPI Pi0/Pi0.5 training. All checkpoints were listed: Pi0.5 Phoenix (180GB, 21 steps, 2026-02-24), base model (about 12GB per model), diffusion strategy (4 snapshots, 253MB each). [[[HYMTPH_4]] was created. During the modification of create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py, the workflow was interrupted. Three parallel exploration proxies initially timed out (each exceeding 1100 seconds), requiring a restart.
Error Recovery Benchmark Test 🔄 Comprehensive Document Synchronization and Architectural Redesign 03:04:05.254 | claude_code
A multi-session meeting was held throughout the day regarding the update of the error_recovery_benchmark document. (1) By deploying parallel proxies, the system aligned the project panorama summary.md with the actual implementation, covering sections such as error frameworks/, scripts/, outputs/. Six new chapters were added, detailing 4 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, and 20 classifiers, along with API descriptions. Code statistics were updated (22 scripts, 122 tests, 11 evaluation runs), M14 evaluation data (6,474 cases, while the document only had 726 cases), and database statistics (649 scenarios, categorized by error type, stage, and severity). The VLA model type and version date were corrected. (2) The user requested changes to the pipeline architecture to demonstrate three sources of design: demonstration playback and injection, VLA/strategy deployment and injection, natural fault capture. Section 3.1 of the pipeline diagram was updated. (3) The user raised questions about the design of ErrorSceneDatabase, discussing the actual implementation (meta.json index, scenario JSON+NPZ pair, automatic classification, deduplication, 4 segmentation strategies), but the discussion was interrupted before identifying the specific issue. (4) Investigation into the 0% success rate of BC-RNN began—the user reported that all normal tasks failed completely, while the expected success rate for training stages should be around 60-70%. Investigation into the differences between training and evaluation code began, but was interrupted midway. Issues such as mismatched observation keys, environment settings, and checkpoint loading need to be checked. (5) The batch evaluation results of Pi0.5 were analyzed: the overall recovery rate was 58.9%. Compared to the BC-RNN baseline (0%) and M14 error recovery results, all recovery rates were close to 0%. It was found that the visualization videos of Pi0.5 were empty directories—the user requested these videos to be regenerated. (6) Methods such as scp/sftp/VS Code/WinSCP were provided to download the document to the local machine.
Token Usage Total usage: $13.83 — 22.3 million tokens
summary: Multi-project research and documentation update: - Studied the cross-sample query evaluation method used in the QueST paper for spatial transcriptomics data. - Improved the Desktop Video CLAUDE.md tool. - Developed a comprehensive training guide for zhaoganlong self-reflection frameworks, including detailed explanations of each checkpoint. - Updated the error_recovery_benchmark document to align with the actual code implementation, including a redesigned process architecture.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04

content:
Task Architecture and Strategy - ✅ Identify Pi0.5 base model configuration errors — Users found that the base number used in LoRA fine-tuning was pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base. Verification is needed for the settings of zhaoganlong, and it may be necessary to retrain all tasks using an49’s 8 GPUs. - ✅ **Implement script/benchmark_rm_ideal.py** — Created a benchmark script with 781 lines of functionality: load_section_string_labels (loading string labels from a commented file and aligning them with cached barcodes), load_fused Embeddings (loading STAIG fused embeddings from the second-stage output or recalculating them), compute_cross_embedding_similarity (computing cross-sectional niche center cosine similarity), evaluate_correlation (Spearman r / Precision@K / identical tag rate), run_cross_section_benchmark (unidirectional sectional evaluation process), generate_summary (formatting output in CSV + JSON + standard output formats) - ✅ **Run Layer_3 benchmark and generate results** — Ran the bidirectional Layer_3 test on RTX 2080 Ti for 151673↔151676. Successfully recalculated the STAIG fused embeddings for 151676 and loaded the RM-IDEAL scores from the cache. Results: Spearman r=0.4356 (p=6.24e-159) for 151673→151676, Spearman r=0.4542 (p=2.66e-183) for 151676→151673, indicating a moderate positive correlation. P@K was close to 0 (with significant differences in rankings), and the identical tag rate was low. - ✅ **Run BC-RNN baseline evaluation (5 tasks × 50 iterations)** — Completed batch evaluations for coffee, stacking three types, threading, and three-piece assembly. The success rate for all tasks was 0%. - 🔄 **Monitor Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning progress** — 8 training tasks ran on 8x A800 GPUs. Progress: coffee (72%), stacking/stacking three types (69-71%), threading/three-piece assembly (50%). The average iteration time for each task was about 6.1 seconds. Each task has approximately 17-31 hours remaining. - ✅ **Summarize Pi0.5 and BC-RNN evaluation results** — Pi0.5 (15th thousand steps): stacking 95-100%, stacking three types 80-90%, coffee/threading/three-piece assembly 15-45%. BC-RNN baseline: success rate for all tasks was 0%. Phoenix comparison (9,999th step): coffee 0%, stacking 12-24%. - ✅ **Fix zero embedding detection and automatic recalculation logic** — Found that the STAIG fused embeddings for 151676 were all zero (model crashed). Added zero embedding detection (np.allclose(embeddings, 0)) in load_fused Embeddings, automatically triggering --recompute_fusion to retrain STAIG (300 cycles, about 50 seconds). After fixing, the embeddings returned to normal. - ✅ **Generate spatial heatmap visualization** — Used sc.read_visium() from scanpy to load DLPFC data (Dataset/DLPFC/151673 path), and used sc.pl.spatial() to create a 2×3 panel graph: each row represents a direction (151673→151676 / 151676→151673), with three columns representing actual values, RM-IDEAL score heatmap, and embedding cosine similarity heatmap. Saved as outputs/rm_ideal_benchmark/rm_ideal_layer3_spatial.png - ✅ **Batch generate historical daily reports from February 7 to February 19, 2026** — Processed 13 dates, involving 4 devices (DCC/tianhe/TzJsDesktop/MacBook) and 5 projects (error-recovery-benchmark/MIHD/CalendarPro/gadget/Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework). Performed approximately 20 export operations and multiple merges. Manually fixed the JSON truncation issue in the report dated February 14, 2026. - ✅ **Fix screen protection program clock transparency issue** — Replaced SwiftUI's.ultraThinMaterial with NSViewRepresentable, and used.nWithinWindow blend mode to display video content instead of the desktop. - ✅ **Launch 2-GPU Pi0.5 LoRA evaluation on an53** — Created the eval_pi05_lora_2gpu.sh script, separating the VLA server (GPU 0) and MuJoCo iteration (GPU 1). Successfully launched 8 tasks × 20 trials on an53. Total runtime is expected to be 60-70 minutes. - ✅ **Explore RM-IDEAL code library and design benchmark testing methods** — Conducted a comprehensive exploration of the RM-IDEAL implementation using 3 exploration agents (RMIdealEvaluator in utils/rm_ideal.py), cross-sample capabilities (compute_niche_scores_cross_section method), and experimental record system. Designed independent script methods (only testing PCA+UNI2+STAIG_FUSION, with target sections being 151673 and 151676), but users rejected ExitPlanMode. - ✅ **Write documentation/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md method paper** — Created a 459-line Chinese method paper, covering 10 chapters: overview (problem definition, two evaluation paths, application scenarios), spatial graph construction (build_grid_connectivity k-NN + symmetry), k-hop subgraph extraction (bfs_k_hop BFS precomputation), WWL graph kernel (message passing iteration, shared hash table), Wasserstein distance (Hamming cost matrix, POT/scipy dual backend), sectional evaluation process (global type mapping, node ID offset), embedding similarity comparison (niche center, cosine similarity), correlation evaluation metrics (Spearman ρ, Precision@K, identical tag rate), experimental results (Layer_3 bidirectional query data), key code path summaries (complete files/functions/data flow diagrams). - ✅ **Implement screen protection program for video-free states** — removed media content protection, created black background windows for screens without wallpapers, and enabled clock display on black screens. - ✅ **Fix video switching errors (bookmark URL mismatch)** — Added a protection mechanism to ensure FileAccess parses bookmarks as old video URLs instead of requesting new ones. If different, returns the original URL (which has temporary sandbox permissions provided by NSOpenPanel). - ✅ **Fix numpy dtype errors in BC-RNN evaluation** — Modified policy_adapter._to_robosuite_obs to filter out numpy scalar types (float64) that robomimic TensorUtils cannot handle. - ✅ **Investigate an53 node failure (NODE_FAIL at 08:30)** — Analyzed Slurm logs (sacct), confirming that an53 crashed due to resource overload (8 GPUs + high CPU load), not caused by AI operations. The AI's srun command failed before the crash. The node restarted at 09:41, and the SSH host key has been changed. Implementation and Fixes - ✅ Fix CUDA compatibility and CPU fallback issues — Initially encountered CUDA kernel incompatibility issues on P100 GPUs, requiring PyTorch version sm_70+. Added try/except to handle torchzeros(1).cuda() exceptions, falling back to CPU if failed. Successfully ran after switching to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75). - ✅ **Fix screen protection program clock text update ghosting issue** — Updated the mask layer in CATransaction.setDisableActions(true) to eliminate 0.2-second ghosting when the time changed. - ✅ **Add --task parameter to BC-RNN evaluation scripts** — Refactored 5_baseline_accuracy.py and visualize_policy_rollout.py to support task registration and resolution per task. - ✅ **Update CLAUDE.md documentation with coding standards and evaluation commands** — Enhanced CLAUDE.md documentation, adding over 35 Makefile targets, incorporating coding standards from AGENTS.md, documenting parallel VLA evaluation workflows, and adding reference to training scripts. - ✅ **Fix gray ghosting issue with screen protection program clock** — Unified the font thickness of ScreensaverClockHighlight and ScreensaverClockMask, eliminating blurriness at the text edges. - ✅ **Create BC-RNN checkpoint symbolic links** — Created symbolic links from checkpoints/bc_rnn/{task} to the actual BC-RNN d0 training directories for 5 tasks. - ✅ **Create website and CLAUDE.md documentation** — Analyzed Hugo blog structure and created a complete CLAUDE.md document including build commands, content sections, and static resource specifications. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial displays a fixed-color glass in videos, rather than transparent glass Solution: SwiftUI materials use the.behindWindow blend mode (displaying the desktop behind the window) instead of the.integralWindow mode (displaying content inside the window). Replace it with NSViewRepresentable using NSVisualEffectView, and explicitly set the.integralWindow mode and clearBackgroundColor in NSHostingView.
Key Insight: SwiftUI’s abstraction hides the details of complex visual effects; for complex visuals like window-level transparency, control needs to be at the NSView level.
2. The STAIG fused embeddings for 151676 were all zero (all embedding similarity values were 0, Spearman r was NaN), causing the model to completely crash Solution: Added np.allclose(embeddings, 0) detection in load_fused Embeddings; if true, print a warning and automatically trigger the–recompute_fusion path to reload the gene/visual embeddings from the cache and retrain STAIG (300 cycles, about 50 seconds on RTX 2080 Ti). After fixing, the embeddings returned to normal (64 dimensions, norm 1.0).
Key Insight: Due to unstable training, the STAIG fused embeddings saved in the second stage might go down to zero; active detection and automatic recalculation are needed in the benchmark testing script to avoid manual intervention.
3. When switching videos after selecting a bookmark in NSOpenPanel, the old video still plays Solution: Ensure FileAccess parses each bookmark as the old video URL instead of requesting a new one. Added a guard to compare the parsed URL with the requested URL; if different, return the original URL (which has temporary sandbox permissions provided by NSOpenPanel).
Key Insight: Bookmark storage conflicts with multiple video histories; a guard is needed to detect that a bookmark points to an expired file.
4. The success rate for the Coffee task’s BC-RNN was 0%, suspected to be an evaluation errorSolutions: The training logs were checked, and it was confirmed that the BC-RNN training for the coffee task achieved 0% SR at all stages (600 cycles of training were performed). This is not an evaluation error; the task indeed failed during training. Key Insights: Before debugging the evaluation process, it is essential to verify the training metrics. A zero value in downstream metrics may indicate a failure in the upstream training process.
Node an53 crashed at 08:30, causing the tmux session to be lost and job 46798 to fail. Solution: An investigation was conducted using the sacct log, and it was determined that the crash was caused by resource overload (8 GPUs reached 100% utilization, with high CPU usage). AI operations were ruled out as the cause (the last completion was at 05:07, 3 hours before the crash). The SSH host key mismatch was resolved using ssh-keygen -R an53.
Key Insights: Slurm reported the details of the crash, but it did not have access to system logs from root.
CUDA kernels on P100 GPU (sm_60) are incompatible with PyTorch (requires sm_70+ versions). torch.cuda.is_available() returns True, but asynchronous operations fail. Solution: Initially, an attempt was made to use try/except to catch the torchzeros(1).cuda() exception, but the asynchronous failures on P100 could not be caught. The user successfully ran the script after replacing it with RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75). Finally, an environment variable CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" was added to the script to implement a forced CPU fallback logic.
Key Insights: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks whether the driver and libraries exist, without checking whether the GPU’s computing power is compatible with current PyTorch. CUDA operations may fail asynchronously, and cannot be caught by Python’s try/except. The most reliable method is to replace the GPU or use an environment variable to force the use of the CPU.
The JSON generated by the LLM is truncated (… truncation marks), causing daily_summary.py to fail to parse on 2026-02-14 and other dates. Solution: The truncated original text was sent to the AI as a separate prompt, with only the required valid JSON portion requested to be repaired/extracted. The AI successfully restored the complete JSON structure.
Key Insights: The extremely long JSON generated by the LLM is at risk of being truncated (especially in scenarios where multiple segments are merged). Truncation detection and automatic JSON repair retries should be added during the merging phase, rather than relying solely on manual discovery and intervention.
The screen saver clock text leaves a 0.2-second smear when the timeout text is updated. Solution: CALayer has a default animation effect of about 0.25 seconds for changes to content properties. In CATransaction, blurView.layer?.mask was updated, and setDisableActions(true) was set to enforce immediate replacement.
Key Insights: CALayer has an animation effect for almost all property changes. Explicit transaction control is needed to achieve immediate visual updates.
The BC-RNN strategy evaluation crashed due to “Unable to handle data type <class ’numpy.float64’>”. Solution: Robomimic’s TensorUtils.to_tensor() cannot handle numpy scalar types. The_to_robosuite_obs was modified to filter out keys in the raw_obs dictionary that only contain array/image values, which are passed to the robomimic strategy.
Key Insights: Robomimic expects a structured obs dictionary containing array values. NumPy scalars from the robosuite (such as gripper width float64) must be filtered before being passed to TensorUtils.
The visualization script failed multiple times: Bashheredoc does not equal escape character != (SyntaxError), file name prefix does not match (151673_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 versus filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5), spatial/data path errors (located in Dataset/DLPFC, not directly in the chapter directory), load_dlpfc_data() return value parsing error (returning 1 instead of 2), read_10x_h5 lacks uns[‘spatial’], causing sc.pl.spatial() to fail. Solution: An independent script/_viz_rm_ideal.py file was used instead of the heredoc method. The glob “*filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5” was used to find the correct file name. sc.read_visium("/path/to/Dataset/DLPFC/151673") was used directly for loading (automatically handling spatial/subdirectories). The problem of unpacking load_dlpfc_data into a single return value was fixed. The load_dlpfc_data function was abandoned, and sc.read_visium was used directly to ensure the presence of uns[‘spatial’].
Key Insights: Bash’s heredoc is sensitive to special character escapes. Complex scripts should be written as separate files. The naming of DLPFC data files includes chapter prefixes. Spatial data is located in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial/ instead of {section}/spatial/. Load_dlpfc_data uses read_10x_h5 instead of read_visium, resulting in the lack of spatial metadata. sc.pl.spatial relies heavily on uns[‘spatial’], so it must be loaded using read_visium.
Common Issues There is a gray ghost shadow on the edges of the screen saver clock text. Solution: The root cause is that the font weights between ScreensaverClockHighlight (.light) and renderTextMask (.regular) do not match, causing the mask to become thicker and exposing the frosted glass edge of hudWindow. This issue was solved by unifying the font weight to .reasonable.
Key Insights: Visual effects layers must achieve pixel-level alignment. Even minor weight differences can expose the underlying content.
Processing historical data over 13 days requires frequent switching of date parameters, and manually confirming the export/merge status is tedious and prone to errors. Solution: The daily_summary.py command export –date YYYY-MM-DD was executed manually, and the merge commands for each date were executed one by one. The integrity of the output files was confirmed through the file system.
Key Insights: The Summarize tool lacks a batch mode (e.g., –date-range 2026-02-07..2026-02-19 automatically iterates). Future improvements should include support for batch processing and progress recovery mechanisms.
When using relative paths in nohup, if the SSH command is executed in the project directory rather than the user’s home directory, problems may occur.
