summary: 先进的空间转录组学分析流程，支持Visium HD技术，并具备全面的RM-IDEAL基准测试能力。已经修复了scGPT模型加载过程中的关键问题，使得聚类指标提升了44%。同时实现了桌面壁纸功能“History”，并解决了多屏幕下的电源管理问题。此外，还建立了VLA评估流程，发现尽管对目标任务进行了训练，但Phoenix模型的性能仍然不佳。
title: Bug Journal 2026-02-28

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-27 Daily Overview Completed Work: Exploration of cluster infrastructure, storage analysis, and review of ML evaluation results for error recovery benchmark testing project Implementation Method: Used Slurm commands for exploration and real-time testing, performed parallel directory scans on large file systems and handled timeout issues, summarizing results from evaluation logs Impact: Created a comprehensive guide to GPU job scheduling, making batch processing possible; identified storage bottlenecks for 18 users in a 7.5PB file system; established model performance baselines and detected severe distribution bias requiring new training methods Explored Slurm cluster infrastructure, analyzed storage usage in the shared file system, and reviewed ML model evaluation results for error recovery benchmarks
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Slurm Cluster Exploration and GPU Node Allocation Tutorial — Explored Slurm commands (salloc, sbatch, srun), found 9 idle GPU nodes in the ai partition, documented three connection methods, and used pam_slurm_adopt constraints ✅ Review of M14 Evaluation Results, Focusing on pi0/BC-RNN Strategy — Obtained and analyzed completed M14 baseline evaluations, showing that success rates in error recovery scenarios were close to zero (BC-RNN at 0.28%, VLA at 0%) ✅ HDD_POOL Storage Analysis, Including All Users — Scanned 18 user directories using parallel execution and timeout mechanisms, identified major storage consumers (5.1TB to 1.4TB), and confirmed 81% file system utilization Implementation and Fixes ❌ Searching for Training Set Evaluation Modification Suggestions — Attempted to find modifications previously discussed for training set evaluations, but no relevant evidence was found in code, memory, or documentation after thorough searches Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. All ML strategies suffered catastrophic failures in M14 error recovery evaluations, while the random baseline had higher recovery progress Solution: Results indicate the need for an M15 phase—fine-tuning Pi0.5 through manual recovery demonstrations to address distribution bias
Key Insight: Models trained only on successful task demonstrations suffered catastrophic failures when errors were injected (success rate <1%), indicating severe distribution bias; error recovery requires specialized training for recovery paths, not just normal task execution
2. Unable to connect directly to GPU compute nodes via SSH (pam_slurm_adopt access denied) Solution: Resources must be allocated via salloc or sbatch first, then connected to active job IDs using srun or SSH; it is recommended to use tmux + salloc workflow for stable interactive sessions
Key Insight: HPC clusters use job-based access control to prevent unauthorized resource usage; direct connection to compute nodes before allocating Slurm jobs is prohibited, requiring a two-step process (allocation → connection)
3. When analyzing HDD_POOL storage, large directory scans timed out for more than 10 minutes (for directories larger than 2TB) Solution: Implemented parallel directory scans with a 600-second timeout per directory, enabling most scans while handling very large directories gracefully
Key Insight: Shared HPC file systems require parallel execution with clear timeout limits for directory traversal operations; some user directories (e.g., miniconda3, VLA_data, cpx1/2) are too large to be scanned using lfs quotas or –max-depth=1
Human and AI Approaches Implementation Level Explanation of Storage Analysis Scope Role Method Human Initially requested extensive storage analysis, later expanded to include all users as AI could only display their own directories AI Initially interpreted the request narrowly as analysis of user’s own directories, requiring explicit instructions to expand to all 18 users Difference Analysis: Humans have a systematic understanding of shared storage from the beginning, while AI defaults to a user-centered interpretation until corrected; this indicates AI tends to narrow scope without explicit instructions
Review of Evaluation Modifications Role Method Human Remembered requesting modification of evaluation to use training set instead of test set in previous conversations AI Conducted thorough searches in code, memory files, and documentation, but found no evidence of such modifications Difference Analysis: Humans rely on conversation memory, while AI relies on searchable documents; either conversations were not preserved, changes were not implemented, or humans remembered incorrectly
Limitations of AI General Limitations For large shared HPC file system directories larger than 2TB, multiple attempts at directory scans timed out despite the 600-second limit, requiring switching to parallel execution, resulting in incomplete results for 5 directories (chenshiyu, cpx1, cpx2, liangzhijia, miniconda3, mozihao) Despite thorough searches of code, memory systems, project documentation, and recent file changes, no evidence of the previously discussed “using training set for evaluation” modification was found, indicating issues with conversation persistence or human memory errors Clear user instructions are needed to understand that storage analysis should include all users, not just their own directories, indicating AI’s default interpretation is too narrow Lessons Learned Key Lessons Severe distribution bias in error recovery tasks: Models trained on successful task demonstrations had almost no success rate (<1%) when errors were injected, while the random baseline had better recovery progress than learned strategies, indicating the need for specific training data collection for error scenarios (M15 phase) For large shared HPC file systems, parallel execution with task-level timeout limits is required as individual user directories may exceed 2TB; sequential scanning is not feasible for production-scale analysis HPC clusters use pam_slurm_adopt to enforce job-based SSH access control—resources must be allocated via salloc/sbatch, then connected to compute nodes via ssh/srun; tmux + salloc provides a stable interactive workflow that can withstand connection interruptions Tianhe-XY cluster has 9 idle A800 GPU nodes (an16, 21, 23, 28-29, 31, 34, 39-40), each with 56 CPUs and 1TB of memory, suitable for tasks ranging from 4 to 72 hours without specifying GPU GRES specifications Conversation Summary Error Recovery Benchmark Testing (Tianhe Cluster) ✅ Slurm GPU Node Allocation and Connection Workflow Tutorial 05:18:36.275 | claude_code Users requested a comprehensive guide to Slurm cluster operations. We explored available commands and found 9 idle GPU nodes (an16-40 series) in the ai partition, discovering that pam_slurm_adopt enforced job-based SSH access. We created a detailed tutorial covering salloc (interactive), sbatch (batch), and tmux-based workflows to achieve stable connections for 4-24 hour GPU tasks. ✅ Analysis of HDD_POOL storage usage for all 18 users
04:28:09.784 | claude_code Users requested storage analysis, initially focusing only on their own 29GB storage space, later expanding to all users. Due to multiple timeout issues with large directory scans, we adopted a parallel scan strategy with a 600-second timeout per directory. The total storage usage was confirmed to be 14.7TB, with the top 5 users using 5.1TB (chenjunye) and 1.4TB (zhaoganlong), with 5 directories exceeding 2TB, making scanning impossible. The total file system capacity reached 7.5PB (81% used, 1.5PB remaining).
Error Recovery Benchmark Testing ✅ Evaluation Results of Pi0/Pi0.5/BC-RNN Strategies in M14 Benchmarks
05:57:24.584 | claude_code Users inquired about previously published evaluation results. M14 results were retrieved from 649 error scenarios, showing that the BC-RNN strategy had a success rate of 0.28% (5 successes out of 1800 attempts), Pi0 strategy was 0%, and Pi0.5 strategy was also 0%. The random baseline strategy achieved higher recovery rates than learned strategies. This indicates significant distribution differences between training phase (normal conditions) and evaluation phase (error injection scenarios), validating the need for manual recovery demonstrations in the M15 phase.
🔍 Attempting to Find Modifications for Training Set Evaluation
07:57:48.342 | claude_code Users again requested modifications to strategies evaluated on the training set. Thorough searches were conducted in the code library, memory system, and documentation, but no evidence of such modifications was found. No research materials were also found, possibly due to delayed conversation recordings or memory confusion.
Token Usage Total: $2.10——4.3 million tokens
summary: The Slurm cluster infrastructure was studied, the storage usage in the shared file system was analyzed, and the evaluation results of the machine learning models were reviewed, in order to understand their performance in error recovery.

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-26 Daily Summary Completed Tasks: Developed a production-grade LaTeX resume on the MacBook. Implemented an image training process based on BC-RNN and adopted a multi-task evaluation framework. Also explored the Pi0.5 VLA policy evaluation infrastructure at Tianjin Hai Laboratory for evaluating the MimicGen task. Completion Methods: Used Jake’s resume template to format the resume professionally on the MacBook. Converted the BC-RNN configuration to an image mode using ResNet18 encoder. Expanded the evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks. Conducted parallel infrastructure exploration at Tianjin Hai Laboratory, mapping Pi0.5 checkpoints, VLA server architecture, and evaluation processes. Impact: Created a LaTeX resume suitable for ATS, which can be used for job applications. Established a reproducible BC-RNN benchmark testing process, solving the 0% success rate issue through task alignment evaluation. Completed the full mapping of the Pi0.5 evaluation infrastructure, making it possible to systematically test trained MimicGen cases. MacBook Completed Task: Created a professional LaTeX resume from a markdown draft using Jake’s resume template. Completion Method: Improved the format through multiple iterations, optimizing the layout of the publication section to ensure compatibility with Overleaf. Impact: Created a concise resume template suitable for ATS, ready for job applications. Tianjin Hai Laboratory Completed Tasks: Implemented the BC-RNN image mode training system. Analyzed the root cause of the 0% success rate. Built a multi-task evaluation framework that supports 5 MimicGen tasks. Explored the Pi0.5 evaluation infrastructure in multiple repositories. Completion Method: Rewrote 5 BC-RNN configurations to use ResNet18 encoder for image observation. Started parallel GPU training. Identified the problem of task mismatch. Created a task registry and extended scripts to include the –task parameter. Fixed the issue with handling _D0 environment observation dimensions. Used parallel exploration agents to map Pi0.5 checkpoints, VLA server startup mechanisms, and evaluation processes. Impact: Established a reproducible BC-RNN benchmark testing process that meets the standards of the MimicGen paper. Solved the critical 0% success rate issue through proper task alignment. Gained a comprehensive understanding of the Pi0.5 evaluation components, making systematic testing possible. Created a LaTeX resume on the MacBook. Implemented a comprehensive BC-RNN image mode training system at Tianjin Hai Laboratory. Explored the Pi0.5 model evaluation infrastructure.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Convert BC-RNN training configuration from low-dimensional mode to image mode — Rewrote 5 BC-RNN training configurations using image observation data (84×84, ResNet18Conv encoder, RNN hidden layer dimension 1000, batch size 16), meeting the requirements of the MimicGen paper. ✅ Implement a multi-task evaluation framework — Created 5 task YAML configuration files, modified 3 evaluation scripts to accept the –task parameter, and updated run_full_eval.sh to support multi-task evaluation. ✅ Solve the problem of observation dimension mismatch — Modified the create_env() function in 4 scripts, imported mimicgen and retained the _D0 environment variant instead of removing the suffix, ensuring consistency between training and evaluation observations. 🔄 Design a Pi0.5 evaluation plan — Developed a detailed implementation plan for running Pi0.5 evaluations on 9 trained MimicGen tasks using the discovered infrastructure. ✅ Diagnose the results of Pi0.5 and BC-RNN training — Analyzed Pi0.5 (completed 99,999 steps, 0% success rate) and BC-RNN (600 cycles, dimension mismatch), identifying the root causes as task distribution mismatch and object state processing issues. 🔄 Start BC-RNN training for 5 tasks — Started parallel BC-RNN image mode training on GPUs 0-4 for coffee, stack, stack three, thread, and three-piece assembly tasks (600 cycles per task). ✅ Explore Pi0.5 training settings — Mapped Pi0.5 checkpoints in zhaoganlong’s directory to understand the structure of checkpoints and available training models. ✅ Explore VLA evaluation infrastructure — Learned about starting the VLA server through start_vla_server.py, supported model types, port configuration, and service architecture. ✅ Explore zhaoganlong’s evaluation process — Understand the evaluation process of the Pi0.5 model based on the motion self-reflection framework in MimicGen tasks. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Create a professional LaTeX resume — Used Jake’s resume template to convert the markdown draft into a professional LaTeX format, gradually improving the formatting of the publication and experience sections. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Pi0.5 completed 99,999 steps but had a 0% success rate. The input dimension of BC-RNN did not match expectations (65 instead of 37). Solution: It was found that Pi0.5 was trained on 5 MimicGen tasks (coffee, stack, thread, assembly, stack three), but the evaluation was done on PickPlace, which was not in the training data. The dimension mismatch in BC-RNN occurred because the create_env() function removed the ‘_D0’ suffix from the environment name, resulting in fewer object observations returned by the base robosuite environment compared to the MimicGen variant. Solution: Imported mimicgen to register the _D0 environment, used the full environment name, and adjusted the training/evaluation tasks. Key Insight: Task distribution mismatch is a critical but often overlooked issue in robot learning evaluation; environment naming conventions can disrupt the consistency of observations between training and deployment.
2. BC-RNN training configurations were in low-dimensional mode (state-based), while the MimicGen paper used image mode (pixel-based) as the main benchmark. Solution: Rewrote all 5 BC-RNN configurations to use image mode: removed ‘object’ from obs.low_dim, added agentview_image and robot0_eye_inhand_image to obs.rgb, configured ResNet18Conv+SpatialSoftmax encoder, increased the RNN hidden layer dimension from 400 to 1000, reduced the batch size from 64 to 16, and set hdf5_cache_mode to ’low_dim’. Key Insight: Image-based BC-RNN requires a completely different architecture (ResNet18 visual encoder, larger RNN, smaller batch size) compared to low-dimensional mode. The official MimicGen benchmark uses image mode, with 600 cycles.
3. The evaluation framework only supported the PickPlace task, making it impossible to evaluate the 5 tasks actually trained by Pi0.5. Solution: Built a comprehensive multi-task framework: created 5 task YAML configuration files containing task-specific objects, stages, and thresholds; created task_registry.yaml to map task names to datasets/checkpoints/prompting; modified 1.generate_scenes.py, 3.collect_data.py, and 4.analyze_results.py to accept the –task parameter and automatically parse it; rewritten run_full_eval.sh to loop through tasks. Key Insight: The evaluation infrastructure needs to be independent of tasks from the beginning; adding multi-task support requires coordinating changes in configurations, scripts, and orchestration layers.
4. It was unclear whether to use the official BC-RNN checkpoints or train independently, and which observation mode to use. Solution: Investigated the MimicGen repository and confirmed that they only provided datasets, not pre-trained checkpoints. Users explicitly expressed their desire to use image mode (pixel-based ResNet18 model, 600 cycles) to match the main results of the paper, rather than low-dimensional mode (state-based model, 2000 cycles). Key Insight: Benchmark papers usually do not provide model weights, only datasets; clarifying the configuration choices (observation mode, training duration) can prevent wasting time on incorrect settings.
5. There was a need to understand the complex multi-repository evaluation infrastructure, including error_recovery_benchmark and Motion-based-Self-Reflection-Framework, which contain interdependent components. Solution: Started 3 parallel exploration agents, mapped training settings, evaluation infrastructure, and flowcharts, and then synthesized the findings.
General Issues 6. The current login node cannot access GPUs (nvidia-smi cannot communicate with the NVIDIA driver). Solution: It was determined that evaluations must run on computing nodes via Slurm schedulers (active tasks with GPU allocations found on the an49 node). Human-AI Approaches Strategic Level Availability of BC-RNN Checkpoints and Training Strategies Role Approach Human Before proceeding with training, actively asked if BC-RNN used official checkpoints and wanted to verify if the MimicGen paper provided pre-trained models. AI Initially implemented the training script without verifying the official checkpoints. After the human raised questions, a thorough investigation was conducted on the MimicGen repository to confirm that checkpoints were not provided. Difference Analysis: Humans are more cautious before investing expensive computational resources by questioning assumptions. AI is eager to implement solutions without verifying the necessity of training first. This shows that AI tends to solve problems without questioning upstream assumptions.
Task Alignment Strategy for Evaluation Role Approach Human Clearly stated the principle: “Train on Task X, evaluate on Task X.” After understanding the root cause of the 0% success rate, confirmed that BC-RNN should follow the same strategy. AI Provided detailed root cause analysis and initially suggested retraining Pi0.5 or evaluating on PickPlace, or evaluating on trained tasks. The human chose the latter approach. Difference Analysis: Humans make architectural decisions based on resource constraints and research objectives. AI provides options but hands over strategic decision-making to humans.
Observation Mode Selection for BC-RNN Role Approach Human When faced with two options, clearly chose the image mode (pixel-based), which aligned with the main results of the MimicGen paper. AI Prepared a trade-off analysis between low-dimensional mode and image mode, but waited for the human to make a decision before providing further options. Difference Analysis: Humans made a wise choice based on the goal of research reproducibility. AI provided technical background but correctly handed over decision-making to humans regarding research methods.
Planning of Evaluation Methods Role Approach Human Requested to directly test “Pi0.5 trained on the Mimicgen test set” — a clearly defined goal-oriented task. AI Interpreted the request as requiring a comprehensive understanding of the infrastructure before execution. Launched a broad exploration phase to map out all components. Difference Analysis: Humans focused on the final result, while AI recognized the need to fully understand the infrastructure to avoid failure during execution. AI’s approach was more conservative and systematic.
Limitations of AI Key Limitations Implemented the BC-RNN training process without verifying if there were official pre-trained checkpoints. Only investigated after the human questioned the method. This shows that AI is eager to solve problems without verifying upstream assumptions. Provided a detailed technical diagnosis when analyzing the 0% success rate, but did not initially raise strategic questions, such as re-training on PickPlace or evaluating on original tasks. This required the human to set the direction. General Limitations After collecting complete information about Pi0.5 evaluation through parallel exploration and file reading, announced a plan to design, but the actual complete implementation plan was not shown in the conversation logs. When creating the resume, made multiple iterative modifications to the format details, rather than understanding user preferences more deeply to achieve a faster final design. Lessons Learned Key Lessons The mismatch between training and evaluation tasks was a serious failure mode, resulting in a 0% success rate. In this case, Pi0.5 was trained on {Coffee, Stack, Threading, Assembly, Stack Three}, but evaluated on PickPlace, leading to a system failure despite successful training. The MimicGen paper only provided demonstration datasets (HDF5 files) and did not provide pre-trained model checkpoints. Researchers had to train the BC-RNN model themselves using robomimic, which is the official benchmark protocol. BC-RNN has two different observation modes, and its architecture is also completely different: low-dimensional mode (state-based, 2000 cycles, larger MLP) and image mode (pixel-based, ResNet18 encoder, 600 cycles, larger RNN). The main results of the MimicGen paper used the image mode. The environment naming convention in robosuite/MimicGen has semantic meaning: removing the ‘_D0’ suffix would change the environment category instantiated, thereby changing the object properties returned. The base robosuite environment returns fewer object states than the MimicGen ‘_D0’ version, resulting in a dimension mismatch (65 → 37 dimensions). The VLA strategy evaluation requires coordination of multiple components: checkpoint locations, strategy servers (based on websocket/TCP), evaluation scripts, conda environment management, and GPU resources allocated through Slurm—any one of these components can hinder the entire process. The multi-task evaluation infrastructure needs to be designed with coordination at different levels: task configuration (YAML), registry mapping (datasets/checkpoints/prompting), script parameters (with the –task flag for automatic parsing), and orchestration (task loops in bash scripts). Later adjustments are much more expensive than designing tasks without them. Image-based robot learning strategies require camera rendering capabilities during both training data collection and evaluation processes. The camera resolution must match the training resolution (84×84 for BC-RNN image mode) to avoid mismatches in input dimensions for the visual encoder. Parallel exploration agents are very effective for understanding multi-component systems—exploring training settings, evaluation infrastructure, and process code separately, and then synthesizing this information to understand the system faster than sequential investigations. The Pi0.5 evaluation stack uses a dual-process architecture: the strategy server (openpi05 conda environment) serves the model through websocket, and the evaluation script (mimicgen_env) runs the network environment and queries the server. Practical Experience Jake’s resume template on Overleaf is a popular LaTeX format suitable for ATS, easy to customize. It uses a simple chapter format and horizontal lines, requiring no special fonts or packages, just a standard LaTeX distribution. Conversation Summary Personal Resume ✅ Created a professional LaTeX resume from a markdown draft 04:33:57.481 | claude_code The user provided a markdown draft of a resume and requested it converted to LaTeX format. The assistant selected Jake’s resume template from Overleaf, as it is suitable for ATS, and implemented the formatting of the resume. After several iterations, the format of the publication section was changed from inline to double-line headings. The final deliverable is a complete .tex file that can be compiled using Overleaf.
Error Recovery Benchmark Test ✅ Implementation of a Multi-Task Evaluation Framework 02:34:46.032 | claude_code The multi-task evaluation infrastructure was implemented as planned. Five task YAML configuration files were created (Coffee, Stack, Stack Three, Thread, Assembly), each containing task-specific objects, stages, and thresholds. The task_registry.yaml file was created to manage the mapping of datasets/checkpoints/prompting. Three evaluation scripts were modified to accept the –task parameter. Five BC-RNN training configuration files were created, and the run_full_eval.sh file was updated to handle task loops. By importing mimicgen and retaining the _D0 environment name, the issue of observation dimension mismatch was resolved. The user inquired about the availability of BC-RNN checkpoints, and the assistant confirmed that MimicGen only provided datasets. The user chose image mode for training.
✅ Implementation of BC-RNN Image Mode Training 04:00:34.795 | claude_code The BC-RNN image mode training process was implemented. Five training configuration files were rewritten to switch from low-dimensional mode to image observation mode (ResNet18 encoder, 84×84 resolution, RNN hidden layer dimension 1000, batch size 16). The evaluation script was modified to enable camera rendering for BC-RNN strategies. Parallel training was initiated on all five MimicGen tasks, running on GPUs 0-4, with 600 cycles per task. Training was confirmed to have started successfully, and the image observation mode was verified.
✅ Analysis of Pi0.5 and BC-RNN Training Status 01:10:41.534 | claude_code The user requested information about the status of the previously started Pi0.5 and BC-RNN training. An investigation revealed that Pi0.5 completed 99,999 steps but had a 0% success rate, while BC-RNN completed 600 cycles but encountered a dimension mismatch issue. The root cause analysis indicated that Pi0.5 was trained on five MimicGen tasks, but evaluation was conducted on PickPlace (task mismatch), while the BC-RNN dimension issue occurred due to the removal of the _D0 suffix, causing loss of object state. The user decided to align the training and evaluation tasks.🔄 Exploration of the evaluation infrastructure for Pi0.5 and MimicGen 04:10:57.207 | claude_code The user requested to test the performance of the Pi0.5 model trained on MimicGen in training cases. The assistant initiated 3 parallel exploration tasks to understand the training settings, VLA server infrastructure, and evaluation process. It was determined that the checkpoint location is within the zhaoganlong directory, the serve_policy websocket architecture was understood, GPU requirements were identified using Slurm, and a implementation plan was started. The complete evaluation infrastructure across multiple repositories was organized, and key files such as evaluate_mimicgen.py and start_vla_server.py were identified.
Token Usage Total: $15.11 — 27 million tokens
summary: These tasks involve work on various devices: creating LaTeX-resumes on a MacBook; building a comprehensive BC-RNN image pattern training system with a multi-task evaluation framework; and exploring the evaluation infrastructure for the Pi0.5 model on the Tianhe platform.
title: Bug Journal 2026-02-26

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-23 Daily Overview Completed Work: Two main research tasks were executed in parallel: (1) Optimization of the MIHD spatial transcriptomics workflow and generalization of the visual encoder; (2) Fixing errors in the benchmark evaluation infrastructure and restarting the Phoenix model training. Completion Method: Systematic optimization methods were applied to both projects: code analysis to identify bottlenecks, targeted vectorization/batch processing to improve performance, architectural restructuring to increase flexibility, comprehensive ablation studies, and a robust error handling mechanism for long-running batch processes. Impact: In terms of MIHD: major training bottlenecks were eliminated (initialization speed increased by 100-500 times, QFormer accelerated by 20-50 times), unlocking architectural flexibility for modern visual encoders and providing empirical evidence for selection criteria for fusion methods. In terms of error recovery: the entire M14 evaluation process was restored (649 scenarios × 4 strategies), Pi0.5 training increased from 3K steps to 100K steps with stable monitoring, and the reliability of M13 classification was quantified. DCC Completed Work: Four MIHD tasks: (1) Optimization of CPU bottlenecks in fusion training; (2) Generalization of the STAIG encoder; (3) Ablation experiments on visual refinement; (4) Large-scale deployment of STAIG in all DLPFC parts and fixing compatibility issues. Completion Method: Vectorization of NumPy/scipy calculations, moving dropout operations to GPU, adding batch processing capabilities for QFormer, restructuring process orchestration using encoder family detection, conducting comprehensive experiments comparing the effectiveness of 8 fusion methods with scan_cluster refinement compared to baseline methods, resolving UNI2 patch size incompatibility issues and NaN handling boundary conditions. Impact: Training bottlenecks were eliminated (QFormer training time reduced from 3.5 hours to 5%, GPU utilization increased by 5%), enabling STAIG to support any visual encoder, finding that refinement is beneficial for weak fusion methods (+0.05-0.09 ARI), but negative for strong fusion methods (-0.05 ARI), and completing full STAIG evaluations in 10/11 parts (average ARI of 0.546). Tianhe Completed Work: Fixed 6 critical errors, started 7 parallel GPU processes (m14_cpu/pi0/pi05 evaluation + Pi0.5 training), completed M13 analysis (kappa=-0.02, discrimination report). Completion Method: Iterative repair and verification cycles were performed on collector.py (error handling), train_pi05.sh (monitoring), evaluate_mimicgen.py (seed generation), and discrimination scripts (type conversion). Slurm jobs 45628 (an49, 4 evaluations) and 46553 (an46, training) were used. Impact: M14 evaluation can now handle fingerprint mismatches (130 natural scenarios), training results can be viewed in real-time (no buffered logs), Pi0.5 training stabilizes at 2.0 seconds/step, and is progressing towards 100K steps. The reliability of M13 classification was also quantified (poor kappa value, requiring VLA data). Through vectorization and GPU batch processing, the MIHD fusion workflow was systematically optimized, with speed increased by 100-500 times. STAIG was extended to support any visual encoder, and comprehensive visual refinement experiments were conducted on 11 slices. Error recovery benchmark evaluation issues were fixed, and Pi0.5 Phoenix training was restarted, along with improved monitoring mechanisms.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Batch QFormer forward pass — The forward_batched() method was implemented, supporting pre-built padding neighbor tensors and key_padding_mask, replacing sequential point processing. ✅ Fixing fingerprint mismatch crashes in evaluation — collector.py was modified, adding scene-level try-except around run_episode(), allowing evaluation to skip 130 incompatible natural capture scenarios instead of crashing at scenario 122. ✅ Vectorizing STAIG edge weight calculations — In STAIGTrainer.py and STAIGTrainerE2E.py, O(n²) Python nested loops were replaced with scipy.cdist and NumPy vectorization for calculating edge weights during initialization. ✅ Conducting ablation experiments on visual refinement — On 151673 slices, 8 fusion methods were compared (series, mean, element sum, attention, basic contrast, adln_attention, llava_mlp, qformer), including cases with and without scan_cluster refinement. 🔄 Restarting Pi0.5 LoRA training and monitoring — train_pi05.sh was updated with PYTHONUNBUFFERED, stdbuf, error catching, and GPU monitoring. Job 46553 was submitted, starting from step 3000 with a step size of 2.0 seconds/step, with a loss of 0.068. ✅ Fixing errors in evaluation scripts (environment seeds, state validation, quaternions) — env.seed() was added to evaluate_mimicgen.py to ensure reproducibility, state dimensions were verified (8D check), and safe quaternion conversion was used (using np.clip instead of in-place mutation). 🔄 Restarting m14 evaluation on GPU (cpu/pi0/pi05) — 3 evaluation processes were restarted on an49 using –resume flag: m14_cpu in 1547 episodes, m14_pi0 in 400 episodes, m14_pi05 in 407 episodes, all processes proceeding smoothly under new error handling. ✅ Extending STAIG fusion to any visual encoder — Hardcoded UNI encoder requirements were removed from process orchestration layer, introducing STAIG_UNI_FAMILY, applying staig_strict preprocessing only when appropriate. ✅ Completing M13 classifier reliability analysis — 7_classifier_reliability.py was run, generating kappa_matrix.csv and reliability_report.md. Fleiss’s kappa value was -0.02 (poor), and 2 non-redundancy violations were found (drop↔grasp_slip kappa=0.71). ✅ Completing M13 error type discrimination analysis — Int(‘False’) errors and scipy.stats.kruskal degradation were fixed, generating discriminability_report.md showing all SR=0 cases, Kruskal-Wallis test not significant. ✅ Generating m14_cpu baseline analysis report — 4_analyze_results.py was run on 726 episodes, generating report.md and summary.json, with Random SR=0% RP=2.3%, BC-RNN SR=0% RP=0.6%, Bootstrap 95% CI, and Fisher exact test. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Deploying STAIG fusion in all DLPFC parts — STAIG fusion was deployed on all 11 slices, using pca and no-gen encoder, while fixing UNI2 patch size incompatibility and NaN handling issues. ✅ Moving adaptiveDropoutAdj to GPU — By directly generating random numbers on edgeAttr devices, GPU↔CPU data transfer was avoided, eliminating GPU↔CPU data transfer. ✅ Updating project documentation to v4.11.1 and v4.12 — Project panorama summary.md was updated, increasing the number of scenarios from 454 to 649, training status was updated (job 46553), milestone progress was updated (M13 analysis completed, M14 in progress), and version history was updated. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. QFormer training is very slow (about 3.5 hours), using only 5% of GPU utilization, as Python loops process 2700 points sequentially. Solution: Batch forward pass was implemented: Pre-calculated padding neighbor index tensors at the start of training, using index_select to build batch context, adding key_padding_mask in transformer, and processing all points in parallel calls to forward on a single GPU.Key Insight: When serializing operations in Python loops, GPU utilization is extremely low—even if the batch semantics are “spatial position” rather than “samples”, batch processing is essential for Transformer models.
2. All 3 GPU evaluation nodes (m14_cpu/pi0/pi05) crashed at scenario 122/649 due to inconsistent XML hash fingerprints of 130 natural capture scenarios, causing EnvironmentMismatchError. Solution: EnvironmentMismatchError import statement was added to collector.py, and scene-level try-except wrappers were added around _run_episode() function to log warnings and skip incompatible scenarios instead of terminating the program.
Key Insight: The database contains two sets of fingerprint data (519 pulses/enhanced scenarios vs. 130 natural VLA-generated scenarios); robust error handling at the iteration boundary can prevent cascading failures, and the work can continue with –resume.
3. STAIG’s edge weight calculation uses O(n²) nested Python loops, which takes 2-5 seconds to calculate 2700 positions, blocking the training process. Solution: Scipy.spatial.distance.cdist was used for distance matrix calculations, scipy.special.softmax(axis=1) for row normalization, and advanced indexing features of NumPy were used to access edge probabilities instead of list comprehensions.
Key Insight: Nested loops containing mathematical operations should always be vectorized—using basic operations of Scipy/NumPy can achieve 100-500 times speedup; the key is identifying O(n²) patterns and finding appropriate library functions (for paired distances, cdist can be used).
4. It’s unclear whether the refinement of visual embeddings (scan_cluster: 1536d→256d) improves multimodal fusion performance or actually reduces it. Solution: Systematic ablation experiments were conducted: All 8 fusion methods were tested on section 151673, and it was found that refinement helps weaker methods (mean increases by 0.056, attention mechanism increases by 0.086 ARI), but negatively affects stronger methods (qformer decreases by 0.054, concat decreases by 0.074 ARI)—the downsampling process requires balancing information to reduce noise.
Key Insight: The value of downsampling depends on the capacity of downstream models: Simple aggregation methods (mean/attention) can benefit from noise reduction, avoiding dimensionality disasters, while complex learnable models like qformer/llava_mlp can utilize rich information in high-dimensional embeddings—there is no universal answer.
5. Pi0.5 training task (job number 46551) unexpectedly exited after about 580 steps due to stdout buffering preventing monitoring, and the exit reason was unclear (code 0). Solution: PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 + stdbuf -oL was added to achieve real-time log output, –eo pipefail + ERR trap was added for error reporting, and health checks on background GPUs were performed every 30 minutes. –resume was used to resume training from checkpoint step 3000.
Key Insight: Buffering of stdout in Python/JAX may hide training failures; no-buffered output, explicit error catching, and regular health checks are crucial for 50-hour training tasks on shared clusters.
6. During STAIG fusion, the pipeline hard-coded visionencoder=‘uni’, limiting our ability to try other encoders (such as HIPT, ResNet50, etc.). Solution: Orchestration layer was refactored: STAIG_UNI_FAMILY setting was introduced to identify encoders that require staig_strict preprocessing (256×256 patches, no ImageNet standardization, frequency filtering), forced encoder coverage logic in run_benchmark.py was removed, extract_planner and evaluation_planner were updated to conditionally apply different variants based on encoder family membership.
Key Insight: Responsibility separation: The model layer (STAIGTrainer) has already processed data of any size through PCA, but the orchestration layer imposes artificial restrictions—good architecture makes this generalization simple.
7. evaluate_mimicgen.py lacks environment initialization, making evaluation results non-replicable between runs. Solution: env.seed(args.seed) call was added before each task’s episode loop to match the reference implementation in LIBERO/Phoenix main.py.
Key Insight: Reproducibility requires clear initialization strategies for RNG and environment RNG; comparing with the reference implementation reveals missing initialization steps.
General Issues 8. adaptiveDropoutAdj transfers edgeAttr to CPU, generates random numbers on CPU, then returns to GPU—this causes synchronization overhead in each cycle (totaling about 1-2 seconds in 1100 cycles). Solution: torch.rand(…), device=edgeAttr.device, dtype=edgeAttr.dtype was used directly on edgeAttr_device to generate random numbers—eliminating all CPU transfers.
Key Insight: Small GPU↔CPU transfers can accumulate significantly over multiple repetitions; always generate random numbers on the same device as the operation tensor; CUDA synchronization overhead cannot be ignored.
9. UNI2 (ViT-H/14, patch_size=14) failed on STAIG’s 256×256 input, with an assertion error stating “256 cannot be divided by 14”. Solution: When the strict mode of STAIG was detected, UNI2VisionEncoder.extract() was automatically adjusted to 224×224 (divisible by 14), and then the patch was passed to the model.
Key Insight: Visual transformers have strict input size requirements; adjusting patches based on size requires checking divisibility constraints; for most ViTs, 224×224 is the safe default size.
10. discriminability script encountered ValueError error when parsing JSONL’s success field: invalid decimal integer value “False”. Solution: String to boolean conversion was added: success_raw = row.get(‘success’, False); success = success_raw if isinstance(success_raw, bool) else success_raw.lower() == ’true’
Key Insight: JSONL serialization inconsistency—success field is stored as string ‘False’; defensive type checking can prevent parsing errors, especially when pattern assumptions are not met.
11. m14_cpu crashed under AssertionError: MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID (0) must be in CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES (5). Solution: MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID was changed from 0 to 5 to match CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=5, thus satisfying the assertion in binding_utils.py regarding device ID strings.
Key Insight: Robosuite’s binding_utils.py requires MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID to be a substring of CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES; when using a single GPU, both must match perfectly.
12. Section 151676 caused STAIG training to produce NaN losses and zero embeddings, making KMeans fail on NaN-filled data. Solution: np.nan_to_num() was added to KMeans backtracking path, replacing NaN with 0 before clustering, and infinity with a larger finite value.
Key Insight: Model crashes (NaN losses) are known failure modes; graceful degradation is needed; input should always be cleaned before passing to external libraries that may not handle NaN gracefully; known failure cases should be documented.
13. discriminability script crashed under scipy.stats.kruskal ValueError due to all success rates being the same (all SR=0 cases). Solution: H, p = scipy_stats.kruskal(*groups) was encapsulated in try-except ValueError, returning H=0.0, p=1.0, significant=False, note=‘all values identical’ in case of exceptions.Key Insight: Statistical tests will fail on degenerate distributions; explicit edge cases and informative messages can prevent silent failures and help interpret results
Differences Between Human and Artificial Intelligence Approaches Strategic Level Strategic optimization planning versus tactical execution| Role | Method | |——|——| | Human | The user pre-analyzed the MIHD code base and provided a detailed three-part optimization plan, including specific code locations, expected performance improvements, and implementation steps—essentially writing specifications for Claude’s execution. | | Artificial Intelligence | Claude strictly followed the provided plan, implementing vectorization using scipy.cdist, random number generation based on GPU devices, and batch processing with padded tensors—focusing on execution rather than strategic planning. |
Difference Analysis: Humans were responsible for strategic analysis and design phases (identifying bottlenecks, selecting algorithms, estimating performance improvements), while artificial intelligence served as an accurate execution agent—a clear delegation of tasks after humans completed complex thinking.
Debugging Strategies for Multi-component Process Failures Role Method Human For error recovery crashes, humans provided detailed context information (649 scenarios, fingerprint groups, crash location scenarios 122), and identified specific repair locations (collector.py lines 299-325), using a try-except pattern. Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence systematically followed the plan but initially focused too much on exploring the code structure. Under human guidance, it directly implemented repairs and verified them through test runs. Difference Analysis: Human domain knowledge allowed for accurate identification of root causes (such as EnvironmentMismatchError, lack of scenario-level handlers); artificial intelligence’s advantage lay in verification work (such as import checks, parallel restarts, progress monitoring), but it required a clear and quick solution plan.
Interpretation of Experimental Results Role Method Human The user requested a comparison of visual refinement without specifying any conclusions—an open-ended analysis request. Artificial Intelligence Claude conducted experiments and then summarized key insights: the value of visual refinement depends on the capacity of downstream models. It helps simplify methods and reduce noise/dimensionality, but it also undermines the effectiveness of complex methods—not just data presentation, but also causal explanations. Difference Analysis: The user requested data, and artificial intelligence provided an explanatory framework. Claude identified balance-of-powers patterns related to capacity, going beyond mere reporting of metrics and demonstrating deeper analytical thinking.
Generalization Range of Visual Encoders Role Method Human The user simply asked if any visual encoder could be used, not specifying UNI—a high-level requirement without specifics on how to implement it. Artificial Intelligence Claude analyzed the system architecture and found that the model layer was flexible, but the coordination layer imposed restrictions. The concept of an “encoder family” was introduced, including conditional preprocessing variants—this went beyond the literal requirements and showed architectural insight. Training Monitoring and Observability Requirements Role Method Human Based on previous failure analysis, humans specified specific monitoring improvements (PYTHONUNBUFFERED, stdbuf, error capture, GPU memory logging). Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence implemented all specified improvements but initially did not realize the importance of non-buffered output. After seeing the note “PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 took effect,” it became aware of this pattern. Difference Analysis: Humans anticipated the observability needs derived from past failure patterns; artificial intelligence learned these patterns through execution feedback rather than active design.
Implementation Level When to Use Agents, When to Use Tools Directly Role Method Human The user let Claude decide when to start exploring with agents—without micromanaging the investigation method. Artificial Intelligence Claude launched multiple exploration agents to understand data loading processes and fusion training cycles before formulating optimization plans. Artificial intelligence actively used agents to explore open code bases. Difference Analysis: Artificial intelligence correctly applied the patterns of using agents for discovery/research tasks and using tools for known operations. Humans trusted this judgment and did not intervene.
Limitations of Artificial Intelligence Key Limitations Before running geneencoder=pca and geneencoder=none, it failed to actively verify whether they would produce the same results in STAIG fusion—wasting computational resources on redundant experiments with no new information. Before designing the training restart plan, it failed to actively verify checkpoint status—assuming “checkpoint 1000” from the plan without checking the current file system status (which was step 3000). This required humans to manually find checkpoint 3000 using the ls command later. It didn’t realize that stdout buffering was a critical failure mode in long-term training until humans explicitly informed it. The previous silent exit code 0 should have raised suspicions about the reliability of logs. General Limitations In the visual optimization experiment, it didn’t notice that the qformer baseline score (0.4832) was actually the best score—only realizing this after completing all comparisons. This issue should have been addressed earlier to adjust experimental priorities. Only realizing the inconsistency between the planned text (“checkpoint 1000”) and the actual file system status during the implementation phase indicates that the initial verifications were insufficient before deciding on the execution path. Only after several rounds of debugging did it discover the incompatibility issue with the UNI2 patch size. If the UNI2 architecture (ViT-H/14) and STAIG’s 256×256 requirements had been known in advance, this issue could have been anticipated. Relying too much on sub-agents for simple state checks (e.g., starting task agents to check M14/M16 states, instead of directly reading files) increased latency and complexity without bringing corresponding value. When multiple sessions referenced the same experiment (visual optimization comparison), Claude did not connect these experiments in chronological order or indicate that they were sequential experiments—this led to confusion in the narrative flow. Lessons Learned Key Lessons Batch processing is crucial for GPU efficiency: Python loops that continuously call small GPU operations (even 2700 calls) can lead to severe resource waste (QFormer only used 5% of GPU resources)—always look for opportunities to combine operations into single GPU kernel calls. The value of dimensionality reduction depends on the capacity of downstream models: It helps simplify aggregation methods (e.g., mean, attention mechanisms), thereby reducing the impact of noise and dimensionality disasters (+0.05-0.09 ARI), but it has a negative impact on advanced learning models that can utilize high-dimensional information (e.g., qformer, llava_mlp) (-0.05 ARI). There is no universal solution; preprocessing methods must be adjusted according to the complexity of the model. Conducting robust error handling at the iteration boundary (scene-level try-except handling) can prevent cascading failures during long-running batch processes, while retaining some results through recovery mechanisms. Vectorizing operations can significantly improve array operation speed: Using scipy/NumPy primitives to replace Python nested loops is always worth it—the key is to identify O(n²) patterns and know the correct library functions. For machine learning training tasks that run for long periods on remote clusters, non-buffered output (PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 + stdbuf -oL) is essential—buffering can hide failures for hours, making debugging impossible. In RL/robotics evaluations, it’s necessary to explicitly initialize strategies RNG and environment RNG—comparing with reference implementations can reveal initialization steps that may be missed in documentation. Achieving flexibility in architecture through conditional logic based on families: From hard-coding assumptions (only UNI) to introducing semantic grouping (encoder family), adopting conditional behavior instead of removing all constraints—this allows for optimization of special cases while maintaining flexibility. Practical Lessons In large-scale applications, eliminating data transfer between GPUs and CPUs is important: Small data transfers may seem insignificant, but they can accumulate over multiple iterations (1100 cycles × multiple dropout calls), totaling 1-2 seconds—perform generation and operations locally on the device. Visual transformers have very strict input requirements: ViTs require input sizes to be multiples of Patch Size (UNI2: H%14=0, W%14=0)—when applying preprocessing methods designed for one architecture to another, it’s necessary to verify size constraints in advance. For most ViTs, 224×224 is a safe default value. Statistical tests (e.g., scipy.stats.kruskal) fail on degenerate distributions; always include edge case handling with informative error messages, rather than allowing library exceptions to propagate. Defensive type checking (isinstance() protection) can prevent parsing errors when serialization format assumptions go wrong; JSONL fields may have inconsistent types across different data sources. Model crashes (NaN losses) require graceful downgrading paths: When there are known failure patterns, add sanitization steps (np.nan_to_num) in the downgrading path and record the issues—producing a downgraded output (ARI=0 and clean data) is better than a crash. When debugging multi-GPU Slurm jobs, MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID and CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES must have precise string matching—substrings in binding_utils.py include checks that require both to align. Dialogue Summary MIHD Space Transcriptomics ✅ CPU Bottleneck Optimization and Architecture Generalization 05:38:48.477 | claude_code Comprehensive optimization of the MIHD fusion training process, addressing three bottlenecks: (1) Using scipy.cdist for vectorized computation of STAIG edge weights, replacing O(n²) loops, which improved speed by 100-500 times; (2) Moving adaptive dropoutAdj to GPU, reducing data transfer time by about 1-2 seconds; (3) Adding forward_batched() method in QFormer for batch processing, improving speed by 20-50 times. By introducing encoder family detection (STAIG_UNI_FAMILY) and conditional preprocessing, STAIG was generalized to support any visual encoder. Comprehensive visual optimization experiments were conducted on 8 fusion methods, finding that optimization helped weaker methods (+0.05-0.09 ARI), but had a negative impact on stronger methods (-0.05 ARI). STAIG fusion was deployed in all 11 DLPFC parts, with UNI2 compatibility fixes, averaging ARI of 0.546 for 10 valid parts.
Error Recovery Benchmarking ✅ Process Repair and Training Restart 07:06:24.474 | claude_code By adding scene-level try-except error handling to collector.py, the critical evaluation process crash issues in scenario 122/649 were fixed, allowing skipping 130 natural capture scenarios with fingerprint mismatches. M13 analysis was completed: generating a baseline report (Random SR=0% RP=2.3%, BC-RNN SR=0% RP=0.6%), reliability analysis (Fleiss’ kappa=-0.02), and discrimination report (all SR=0). Pi0.5 Phoenix training was restarted, with improved monitoring (PYTHONUNBUFFERED, stdbuf, error capture, GPU logs), stabilizing from step 3000 with a step size of 2.0 seconds. Errors in the evaluation script were fixed (environment initialization, state verification, quaternion conversion). Three GPU evaluations (m14_cpu/pi0/pi05) were restarted, with restoration capabilities, moving towards the strategy of 649 scenarios × 4. Project documentation was updated from v4.11 to v4.12.
Token Usage Total usage: $69.06 — 106.9 million tokens
summary: 通过向量化处理以及GPU批量处理技术，我们成功优化了MIHD融合流程，使得运行速度提高了100到500倍。此外，我们还改进了STAIG算法，使其能够支持多种视觉编码方式。我们在11张幻灯片中展示了全面的视觉优化实验成果，同时修复了错误恢复基准测试中的崩溃问题，并重新启动了Pi0.5 Phoenix的训练过程，同时还改进了监控机制。
title: Bug Journal 2026-02-23

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W08 (2026-02-16 ~ 2026-02-22) Intensive week advancing two major research projects: Error Recovery Benchmark transitioned from infrastructure (Phase I) to experimentation (Phase II) with VLA integration, natural error capture (271 scenarios), and baseline evaluation pipeline; MIHD spatial transcriptomics achieved critical debugging breakthroughs (coordinate system fix improving ARI 86%), architectural innovations (Vision Refinement two-stage fusion, embedding cache), and comprehensive documentation. Both projects now operationally ready for large-scale experimentation despite cluster access challenges.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-02-16 ~ 2026-02-22 Active Days 6 / 7 Total Conversations 33 Projects 7 Tasks Completed 35 Tasks In Progress 9 Total Tokens 233,671,392 Total Cost $159.97 Daily Average Cost $26.66 Project Progress Error Recovery Benchmark (5 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Completed VLA integration infrastructure (v4.3-v4.5): policy server, error classification (20 classifiers, 24 types), natural error capture generating 271 scenarios across 8 error types Achieved M5/M9/M10 milestones with natural failure mode distribution: joint_limit_approach (47%), grasp_wrong_pose (30%) Designed and documented comprehensive Phase II roadmap (M12-M15) with 4-pillar validation framework and 16-day critical path Implemented M14 baseline infrastructure with resume support, multi-run merge, and crash-resilient evaluation (5448 episodes capacity) Validated end-to-end pipeline via M14 sanity check (60 episodes, 20 classifiers operational) Researched 17 VLA/foundation models and designed usefulness proof experiment (expect 15-30% SR improvement) Initiated Pi0.5 fine-tuning pipeline with MimicGen-to-LeRobot conversion (7/9 tasks, 4500 demos) Blockers:
⚠️ Pi0.5 GPU OOM (150MB allocation failure) prevents large-scale baseline runs ⚠️ Cluster access permissions blocking SLURM job submission across all partitions ⚠️ VLA checkpoints domain-mismatched (LIBERO-tuned vs PickPlace task) weakening baseline validity MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics (5 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Fixed critical coordinate swap bug (pxl_row/pxl_col as Y/X), improving UNI2 vision-only ARI from 0.065→0.121 (86% gain) Re-ran 33 vision + 88 fusion experiments validating multimodal approach (concat fusion reached 0.275 ARI) Implemented Vision Refinement two-stage fusion architecture (scan_cluster/stego_refine/byol_spatial preprocessing) Built embedding cache infrastructure eliminating redundant UNI2/SCAN encoder instantiation (hours saved per run) Created comprehensive documentation: RM-IDEAL metric guide, vision encoder pipeline, 25+ paper literature survey Designed Idea 7 enhancement with 6 candidate solutions and 5-phase implementation roadmap Major project cleanup: 51% documentation reduction (1554→763 lines), deleted 3 redundant scripts, organized three-tier docs structure Blockers:
⚠️ Vision Refinement initial results mixed (ARI 0.313 vs 0.387 direct concat) - compression loses fusion-valuable information ⚠️ UNI2 clusters by morphology not biology - fundamental limitation requiring domain adaptation gadget utility toolkit (2 days active) — 🔄 active Accomplishments:
Designed conversation summary feature for daily reports (per-conversation granularity with project/topic/outcome) Completed documentation coverage for all three tools (summarize/test/website) Generated 3-week historical archive (21 reports covering 2026-02-01 through 2026-02-21) Key Tasks ✅ VLA policy rollout validation and natural error capture (2026-02-16) — Fixed image passing bug, ran 10 injection + 50 natural_capture rollouts with Pi0, generated 153 new scenarios (121→271 total, 4→8 error types), achieved M5/M9/M10 milestones 🔄 Error Recovery Benchmark full M14-cpu evaluation (2026-02-22) — Launched 649 scenes × 2 policies × 3 seeds = 3,894 episodes on GPU with EGL rendering (estimated 6.5 hours) ✅ Diagnose STAIG fusion clustering collapse on DLPFC 151672 (2026-02-16) — Identified 64-dim embedding bottleneck (std=0.12) + spatial majority voting eliminates minority clusters, proposed dimension ablation study ✅ VLA Model Integration Infrastructure (v4.4) (2026-02-16) — Implemented vla_server, PolicyServerAdapter, camera obs support, PolicyErrorDetector, capture_natural_errors(). All 79 unit tests passing ✅ Policy Error Detection & Classification System (v4.3) (2026-02-16) — Implemented 20 classifiers across 3 families, 24-type taxonomy, VLM analyzer (Gemini + Claude Code CLI). All 73 unit tests passing ✅ VLA Natural Error Generation System Architecture Design (v4.5) (2026-02-16) — Explored VLA checkpoints (Pi0/Pi0.5/Phoenix), designed 7-step implementation plan with BC-RNN + pretrained encoder + rule-based offline analysis ✅ Fix MIHD coordinate swap bug (2026-02-18) — Corrected pxl_row/pxl_col mapping (Y/X semantics), cleared vision cache, re-ran experiments. UNI2 ARI improved 0.065→0.121 ✅ MIHD comprehensive evaluation documentation and literature survey (2026-02-19) — Created RM-IDEAL metric guide, vision encoder pipeline docs, 25+ paper survey (spEMO/STAIG/HEST-1k/UNI/STEGO/SCAN/BYOL) ✅ MIHD Enhancement Plan Update: Idea 7 Image-Only Clustering (2026-02-19) — Designed 5-phase plan with 6 candidate solutions (STEGO/SCAN/BYOL/DINOv2-GAT/TTT-MAE/SFDA), dependency graph, risk analysis (~500 lines) ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark M14 baseline infrastructure implementation (2026-02-19) — Added resume support to collector, multi-run merge + per-error-type breakdown, 7 Makefile targets for 454-scene × 4-policy baseline 🔄 Error Recovery Benchmark M12: Large-scale dataset construction planning (2026-02-19) — Designed 13-step plan: MimicGen augmentation (10→200 demos), BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5 natural error capture, stratified splitting. Code written but execution blocked by MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID error 🔄 Error Recovery Benchmark M13: Error taxonomy validation design (2026-02-19) — Designed three-pillar validation: (1) FMEA→ISO standards mapping, (2) 6×3 phase×family completeness matrix, (3) Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa statistical validation ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II strategic planning documentation (2026-02-19) — Updated 項目全景總結.md (v4.9→v4.10): split Phase I/II goals, added M12-M15 sub-goals, 5-phase timeline, optimized 1554→763 lines (51% reduction) preserving 87 checklist items ✅ Add embedding cache support to run_benchmark.py (2026-02-20) — Integrated CacheManager for gene/vision embeddings with variant support (standard/freq/staig_strict) and override flag propagation 🔄 Launch parallel M12 data generation jobs (2026-02-20) — Started BC-RNN injection/natural capture, demo-based generation, Pi0/Pi0.5 servers across 5 GPUs for scaling dataset 454→2000+ scenes ✅ Implement Vision Refinement two-stage fusion architecture (2026-02-22) — Added –vision_refine parameter (scan_cluster/stego_refine/byol_spatial), inserted refinement stage between vision encoding and fusion (~150 lines, 10 functions modified) ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark M14 sanity check evaluation (2026-02-22) — Ran 10 scenes × 2 policies × 3 seeds = 60 episodes via Slurm, validated collector resume, error detection (20 classifiers), JSONL output. Completed in 7 minutes ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II execution plan (2026-02-22) — Wrote 7-step plan covering M12-M15, GPU allocation strategy (50% memory threshold, 8 A800 GPUs), 16-day critical path timeline, Slurm patterns ✅ Conversation summary feature design for gadget/summarize (2026-02-16) — Designed JSON structure (per-conversation with project/source/timestamp/topic/summary/outcome), prompt modifications, Markdown rendering with outcome emoji icons ✅ VLA & Foundation Models research survey (2026-02-19) — Created 200-line survey covering 8 VLA models with error recovery (SC-VLA, CycleVLA, VLA-SCT, FailSafe, etc.) and 9 foundation models (π0, OpenVLA, Octo, etc.) ✅ Fix BC-RNN observation dimension mismatch (2026-02-20) — Fixed 65-vs-37-dim error by mapping object-state→object in _to_robosuite_obs() and using raw robosuite obs in both injection and natural capture paths ✅ Vision Refinement initial testing (151673 slice) (2026-02-22) — Tested PCA + UNI2 + scan_cluster refine + concat: ARI=0.313 (higher than image-only 0.303 but lower than direct concat 0.387) ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark experiment status verification (2026-02-22) — Used parallel agents to verify M1-M11 complete (649 scenes), MimicGen +193 scenes, BC-RNN injection +61 scenes, identified Pi0.5 OOM blocker ✅ Create Error Recovery Benchmark goal dependency topology (2026-02-22) — Created 3-level dependency visualization (G1-G7 goals, M12-M15 milestones, S-level sub-goals) identifying M14 as critical path blocker 🔄 Pi0.5 Phoenix fine-tuning pipeline setup (2026-02-22) — Downloaded 9 MimicGen datasets (4500 demos, 18.4GB), created HDF5 conversion script, completed 7/9 conversions, configured 4-GPU training (batch_size=64, 100K steps) ✅ Fix error recovery benchmark documentation errors (2026-02-16) — Corrected 15+ factual errors via registry-based verification: detector count 6→5, classifier count 19→20, error types 25→24, categories 10→8, test count 73→79 ✅ Create policy rollout visualization tool (2026-02-18) — Wrote visualize_policy_rollout.py with offscreen rendering, HUD overlay, MP4 export, support for random/BC-RNN/VLA policies (200 lines) ✅ MIHD documentation reorganization (2026-02-22) — Created three-tier docs/ structure (research/experiments/archived), merged ENHANCEMENT_PLAN files into unified plans.md with Phase 1-2 progress 🔄 Vision Refinement full fusion method sweep (2026-02-22) — Launched 7 fusion methods (mean, element_wise_sum, attention, basic_contrastive, adaln_attention, llava_mlp, qformer) with scan_cluster refinement ✅ Build unified checkpoint management system (2026-02-18) — Created checkpoints/ directory with symlinks to BC-RNN, VLA (Pi0/Pi0.5), Robomimic models for consistent path references ✅ Investigate VLA training data availability (2026-02-18) — Searched for PickPlace-specific VLA checkpoints; found only 10 source demos vs 500 demos for Coffee/Stack tasks ✅ MIHD visualization enhancement with H&E reference panels (2026-02-19) — Modified create_clustering_visualization() from 1×2 to 1×3 layout adding H&E panel; batch regenerated 11 DLPFC sections ✅ Update Error Recovery Benchmark CLAUDE.md for Slurm workflow (2026-02-22) — Replaced SSH-based GPU access with stable Slurm workflow: source set-XY-I.sh → squeue → srun –jobid= ✅ gadget batch historical daily report generation (2026-02-22) — Generated structured reports for 21 past dates (2026-02-01 through 2026-02-21) covering Error Recovery Benchmark, MIHD, and other projects Problems & Solutions 1. VLA server initial obs missing camera images, causing ‘observation/image’ KeyError [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-16) Solution: Fixed rollout_generator.py _get_current_obs() to add include_images parameter; detect PolicyServerAdapter type and pass include_images=True with camera_names=[‘agentview’,‘robot0_eye_in_hand’]
2. VLA server used wrong openpi API - manually loaded norm_stats causing model initialization failures [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-16) Solution: Replaced manual loading with openpi’s create_trained_policy(train_config=get_config(config_name), checkpoint_dir=…) which handles norm_stats, transforms, and model architecture automatically. Added –config_name CLI arg and IMAGE_KEY_MAP for correct camera key mapping
3. Pi0 (LIBERO checkpoint) achieved 100% failure rate on PickPlace task during natural_capture rollouts [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-16) Solution: Identified failure mode distribution (joint_limit_approach 47%, grasp_wrong_pose 30%, misalignment_pre_grasp 17%, overshoot 5%, premature_release 1%); recorded as natural error data; determined need for PickPlace-specific checkpoint or domain adaptation
4. VLA integration appeared complete (tests passing) but discovered non-operational when attempting actual rollout [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-16) Solution: Deep exploration revealed blockers: wrong openpi API usage, missing norm_stats.json handling, incomplete checkpoint paths, unsupported –policy vla_server flag. Created detailed fix plan and systematically implemented all corrections
5. _preprocess_obs() failed when StateExtractor returned state_info dict (different format from raw robosuite obs) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-16) Solution: Refactored _preprocess_obs() to detect input format and normalize to common structure {“state”: […], “images”: {…}}. Added robust key lookup with fallback patterns and proper float-to-uint8 image conversion
6. Vision embeddings extracted from transposed image locations due to X/Y coordinate swap in tissue_positions_list.csv parsing [MIHD] (2026-02-18) Solution: Renamed columns to pxl_row/pxl_col (semantic names), swapped coordinate order to (X, Y) = (pxl_col, pxl_row), deleted vision cache, re-ran experiments
7. Pi0_libero checkpoint achieves 0% success on PickPlace despite 96-100% on Coffee/Stack [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-18) Solution: Identified root cause: Pi0_libero is LIBERO-domain finetuned, lacks PickPlace-specific training data (only 10 source demos vs 500 for other tasks); proposed MimicGen→LeRobot→LoRA pipeline
8. No way to visually inspect VLA policy behavior on PickPlace task to diagnose 0% success rate [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-18) Solution: Built visualize_policy_rollout.py with EGL offscreen rendering, frame-by-frame capture, HUD overlay (policy/step/reward/success), and MP4 export per rollout
9. How to prove Error Recovery Benchmark is useful beyond ‘can generate scenes’ - need evidence that data actually improves models for thesis contribution [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-19) Solution: Design G4 (dataset usefulness proof) as 4-step experiment: convert 2000+ scenes to LeRobot format, LoRA fine-tune π0.5 on error scenes for 2-4 hours, evaluate on held-out test split, measure improvement (expect 15-30% SR gain with statistical significance via bootstrap CI and Wilcoxon test)
10. UNI2 foundation model features achieve only ARI 0.11-0.16 on DLPFC spatial domain clustering (vs 0.5+ for gene+image multimodal) [MIHD] (2026-02-19) Solution: Identified 5 root causes: training data domain gap, pretraining task mismatch, subtle DLPFC differences, feature redundancy with genes, lack of spatial context. Proposed 6 adaptation methods (STEGO, SCAN, BYOL, DINOv2-GAT, TTT-MAE, SFDA) following solution hierarchy: domain-specific pretraining > test-time adaptation > feature refinement
11. Error Recovery Benchmark dataset phase imbalance - 90% scenes in pre_reach/reach, transport/place completely empty (0 scenes) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-19) Solution: Design strategy using BC-RNN (60-70% SR) as key to reaching later phases, lower detector min_step thresholds from 100→5-50 to capture lift/transport failures, use MimicGen to generate 200+ demo variants for increased trajectory diversity
12. How to rigorously validate that 24-type error taxonomy is principled, complete, and non-redundant for academic publication [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-19) Solution: Three-pillar validation: (1) map 8 categories to FMEA failure modes from ISO 15066/10218/IEC 61508 standards, (2) prove completeness via 6 phase × 3 family = 18-cell matrix, (3) statistical validation using Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa (target: within-category κ>0.6, cross-category κ<0.3)
13. M14 baseline evaluation (5448 episodes, 5-15h runtime) risks losing all progress if VLA server crashes mid-run [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-19) Solution: Implemented resume support in collector.py: on startup, reads existing episodes.jsonl, builds set of completed (scene_id, policy_name, seed) tuples, skips them in triple loop, rebuilds all_episodes dict to preserve summary correctness. Added –resume flag. Now crash-safe with automatic resume
14. VS Code Remote-SSH connection to HPC cluster fails with ‘client_loop: send disconnect: Bad file descriptor’ and ’tried to write to a nonexistent pipe’ [Infrastructure] (2026-02-19) Solution: Windows OpenSSH does not support Unix domain sockets used by ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist. Commented out these 3 options in ~/.ssh/config and removed stale socket files. Connection restored immediately
15. run_benchmark.py re-instantiated encoders and extracted embeddings every run, wasting hours on already-computed UNI2/SCAN features [MIHD] (2026-02-20) Solution: Integrated CacheManager with has/load/save_*_embedding() methods; added cache checks before encoder instantiation with variant support (standard/freq/staig_strict); propagated override flag to both call sites
16. BC-RNN policy expected input_size=65 but environment provided 37 dimensions, causing RuntimeError [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-20) Solution: Root cause was object count mismatch (trained with 8 objects=56 dims, eval env has 4 objects=28 dims). Fixed _to_robosuite_obs() to map ‘object-state’→‘object’ key and use raw robosuite obs from env._env._get_observations() instead of StateExtractor. Fixed both injection and natural capture paths
17. SSH to GPU nodes (an49, an16) blocked by pam_slurm_adopt requiring active jobs, and an49 not present in any SLURM partition [Infrastructure] (2026-02-21) Solution: Verified an49 completely absent from sinfo output; created alternative tmux-based launcher (launch_eval_tmux.sh) and recommended admin intervention. Documented blocker for admin escalation
18. All SLURM job submissions fail with ‘User’s group not permitted to use this partition’ across xy-a800, ai, lava, and all other partitions [Infrastructure] (2026-02-21) Solution: Created self-contained workaround scripts ready to execute once access restored; documented the blocker for admin escalation rather than trying endless permission variants
19. Pi0.5 VLA server hits GPU OOM when loading model (150MB allocation failure), blocking M14 baseline evaluation of 7,800 episodes [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-22) Solution: Identified blocker requiring either larger GPU or reduced batch size. Documented for follow-up; initiated alternative path of fine-tuning pi05_base on MimicGen tasks to create domain-matched baseline
20. VLA checkpoint selection ambiguity: pi0_libero (LIBERO-tuned) and pi05_base (generic pretrained) both domain-mismatched for PickPlace task [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-22) Solution: User paused execution to question checkpoint relevance. Identified need for explicit decision on domain matching. Initiated fine-tuning pipeline as solution path to establish task-aligned baseline
21. Vision Refinement scan_cluster achieved ARI 0.313, lower than direct concat (0.387), indicating 256d compression loses information valuable for multimodal fusion [MIHD] (2026-02-22) Solution: Continue testing remaining 6 fusion methods to determine if certain strategies better utilize refined 256d embeddings; consider adjusting hidden_dim or testing alternative refinement methods (stego_refine, byol_spatial)
22. GPU allocation strategy unclear: initially assumed only GPU 0 available despite 8 A800 GPUs existing on Slurm node [Infrastructure] (2026-02-22) Solution: Human clarified to check all GPUs via nvidia-smi and use any with 50%+ free memory. Updated plan to dynamically check memory usage and allocate CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES accordingly, enabling parallel task execution
23. SSH connections to GPU nodes (an49, an53) unstable and disconnect frequently, interrupting long-running experiments [Infrastructure] (2026-02-22) Solution: Switched to Slurm-based workflow: use squeue to find active jobs, then srun –jobid= to attach to allocated node. Avoids direct SSH and leverages Slurm’s job management layer
Learnings Architecture (architecture) VLA end-to-end integration requires explicit camera observation handling: need include_images=True in state_extractor.extract() and camera_names specification for all camera-dependent policies. Policy observation format correctness is critical. Embedding dimension bottlenecks cause catastrophic model collapse: STAIG’s 64-dim compresses 1536-dim vision features by 96%, producing near-indistinguishable embeddings (std=0.12). Spatial refinement then eliminates minority clusters. 256-512 dim necessary to avoid this pathology. Spatial coordinate systems in medical imaging require explicit semantic naming (pxl_row=Y, pxl_col=X) and validation via physical units (pixels × scalefactor = microns). Generic pixel_x/pixel_y naming is error-prone and can halve performance. Phase imbalance in error generation stems from success rate distribution creating reachability problem: need policies with 60-70% SR (BC-RNN sweet spot) to reach late phases while producing failures. Random fails too early, Pi0 has 0% SR - only BC-RNN reaches transport/place with failures. Two-stage fusion (self-supervised refinement → multimodal integration) creates new experimental dimension, but refined embeddings not universally superior to raw embeddings. Optimization objective alignment critical: self-supervised clustering optimizes for separability, which may not align with multimodal fusion’s complementarity objectives. Cache variants (standard/freq/staig_strict) prevent collisions when same encoder produces different embeddings based on preprocessing flags. More robust than separate encoder names. Embedding cache infrastructure eliminates redundant encoder instantiation, saving hours per run. Resume logic for long-running data collection must rebuild summary state from JSONL, not just skip episodes. Correct approach: read JSONL on startup, rebuild all_episodes dict, add completed tuples to skip set, continue collection. Ensures idempotency. Baseline model selection critically affects experimental validity. Using domain-mismatched checkpoints weakens usefulness claims due to distribution shift rather than dataset quality. Must explicitly validate or fine-tune baselines to ensure fair evaluation. Observation pipeline bugs in multi-path codebases (injection vs natural capture) require exhaustive analysis - fix in one path doesn’t guarantee fix in all paths. Grep for all uses of the pattern. Dependency topology diagrams should precede execution plans. Clear visualization of M14 as critical path blocker shaped resource allocation decisions and timeline estimates, enabling strategic scheduling. Symlink-based checkpoint management enables version control of path references while preserving original data locations. Critical for shared filesystem collaboration. Domain Knowledge (domain) Pure vision encoders cluster by morphological similarity not biological domains: UNI2 groups blood vessels across different cortical layers because they look similar. Need gene expression to distinguish functional regions with similar visual appearance. Error recovery benchmarks require 4-pillar validation: (1) large-scale diverse data (2000+ scenes), (2) theoretically-grounded taxonomy (FMEA/ISO standards), (3) baseline performance characterization, (4) demonstration of training improvement (15-30% SR gain). This proves usefulness beyond infrastructure. Natural error capture more efficient than injection mode: 50 rollouts generated 150 scenes (~3 per rollout), while injection mode 10 rollouts generated only 3 scenes (~0.3 per rollout). Natural failure modes more diverse and realistic. VLA cross-task generalization fails when task domains differ (LIBERO vs PickPlace): Pi0_libero achieves 96-100% on Coffee/Stack but 0% on PickPlace. Domain-specific finetuning essential - requires 500+ demos expandable via MimicGen from 10 source demos. Cross-domain literature search reveals universal solution patterns: image-only clustering problem in spatial transcriptomics identical to fine-grained bird classification, remote sensing, microscopy. All converged on same hierarchy: domain-specific pretraining > test-time adaptation > feature refinement > task-specific fine-tuning. FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) from industrial safety standards (ISO 15066/10218, IEC 61508) provides rigorous theoretical grounding for robot error taxonomies. Academic taxonomy validation requires both theoretical grounding and empirical evidence (Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa: within-category κ>0.6, cross-category κ<0.3). Evaluation paradigm fundamentally changes what is measured: same task (tissue type identification) evaluated as supervised classification (tests linear separability) vs unsupervised clustering (tests natural cluster structure) measures different aspects of embedding quality. High KNN accuracy doesn’t guarantee good clustering ARI. VLA model landscape for error recovery: 8 models claim recovery capabilities but only 2 open-source (RoboFAC, VLAC). FailSafe and VLA-SCT most practical for integration (training-free, plug-in architecture, validated on π0/OpenVLA). Recovery methods fall into 3 categories: dual-system (fast/slow), proactive (subtask backtracking), reactive (supervisor-guided correction). Tools (tools) When integrating third-party ML libraries (openpi, robomimic), always check actual API signatures via inspect.signature() and read example code in source repo.
